It also "showed" how many books would end up in the written bread of life (Matt. 4:4): the showbread was laid out in two rows of six (Lev. 24:6), showing the 66 books in the canon of Scripture.

The showbread was baked and laid out fresh every Sabbath day (Lev. 24:8; 1 Chron. 9:32). The bread from the previous week was then taken up and eaten by the priests ["in the holy place"]{.class_3054} (Lev. 24:9), which included ["the court of the tabernacle"]{.class_3054} (Lev. 6:16). This is the law to which Jesus was referring in Mark 2:26.

The notable exception is [David.]{.class_3055} As a type of Christ, David was not only a King, he was also a prophet (Acts 2:29--30) and acted as a priest on occasion (2 Sam. 24:25). That was David, and as Christ, Jesus is the Son of David. Jesus is the ["King of the Jews"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw343.xhtml_page_70} (Matt. 2:2), He is the Prophet like unto Moses (Acts 3:22--26), and He is ["a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 7:14--17 cf. Psa. 110:4).

["And gave also to them which were with him?"]{.class_3054} If those young men had not been with David, they would have been killed for eating the showbread, but because they were with God's prophet-priest-King, they got in on the "goodies." So in the New Testament, every disciple of Jesus Christ is a priest (1 Pet. 2:5, 9). There is no special Priest class that turns bread into the literal body of Christ by a magical incantation. Every Christian serves as a priest under his great ["High Priest"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 3:1).

Verse 27:[ "The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath."]{.class_3054} That's a strange thing to say in light of the fact that a Jew was to be [killed]{.class_3055} for violating the Sabbath (see our comments on vs. 24). But there is more to that Sabbath than just giving people the chance to rest (Exod. 23:12; Deut. 5:14).

You see, the command to keep the Sabbath was the only one of the Ten Commandments given specifically to a [Jew,]{.class_3055} not a [Gentile.]{.class_3055} When Jesus said ["The sabbath was made for man,"]{.class_3054} He was not talking about mankind in general, like the Seventh-day Adventists teach; He was talking about one particular group of men: [Jews.]{.class_3055}

Ezekiel 20:12---["Moreover also I gave THEM]{.class_3054} \[the Jews\] [my sabbaths, to be a SIGN between me and THEM]{.class_3054} \[the Jews, not the Gentiles---1 Cor. 1:22\][, that they may know that I am the LORD that sanctify them."]{.class_3054}

See the same thing in Ezekiel 20:20---["And hallow my sabbaths; and they shall be a sign between me and you, that ye may know that I am the LORD your God."]{.class_3054}

The Sabbath wasn't ["made"]{.class_3054} for any Gentile or for any member of the Body of Christ. Nobody from Adam to Moses kept the Sabbath, because no ["man"]{.class_3054} even knew about the Sabbath. You say, "But God talks about blessing and sanctifying the Sabbath in Genesis 2:3." True, but it was [Moses]{.class_3055} who wrote Genesis 2:3, and according to Nehemiah 9:13--14, the Sabbath was revealed to Moses on Mt. Sinai.

When Paul lists the commandments for a New Testament believer to observe, he intentionally leaves out Sabbath observance (Rom. 13:8--10), because the Sabbath is []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw343.xhtml_page_71}["a shadow of things to come"]{.class_3054} (Col. 2:16--17). When the King of the Jews sits on the throne of David ruling over the entire earth (Zech. 14), [that's]{.class_3055} when the Sabbath will come back into its own (Ezek. 44:24, 45:17, 46:1, 3--4, 12; Isa. 2:3, 66:23). That's when all men will keep it. Until then, Seventh-day Adventists are Satanic liars pretending to be Jews (Rev. 2:9, 3:9) so they can [damn your soul]{.class_3055} (Gal. 2:16, 3:1--2, 10--11, 5:4).

Now how was ["the sabbath made for man"]{.class_3054}? According to both Ezekiel 20:12, 20, that Sabbath was given as a ["sign"]{.class_3054} to those Jews so they would know Jehovah was the right God and that He sanctified that nation, [over every other nation on earth,]{.class_3055} to be His people. That Sabbath points to a time of rest for the whole earth (the Millennium) when the [Jews]{.class_3055} are in charge and a Jewish King from Judea is on the throne of the earth. The sign is there no matter how many Jews throughout history don't understand it, ignore it, misuse it, or wind up in Hell. God knew that when perfect peace on earth finally comes, it comes from a [Jew]{.class_3055} with a [Jewish Book,]{.class_3055} and [that's]{.class_3055} why He made the Sabbath so holy.

When it comes to the Sabbath, Seventh-day Adventists don't have the sense God gave a brass monkey. I was talking to a Seventh-day Adventist about these things one time, and he bragged that he kept the Sabbath. I decided to "test his water" and see just how good of a Sabbatarian he was, so I asked him, "Do you do any cooking on the Sabbath?"

"Yes," he replied.

"Well," I said, "doesn't the Bible say you're not supposed to kindle a fire on the Sabbath day (Exod. 35:3)?"

"Oh, that doesn't apply," he said. "After all, the Sabbath was made for man; man wasn't made for the Sabbath."

I laughed at him and said, "I see. You believe just what I believe; you just pretend to believe something else."

Those birds don't keep the Sabbath. They will do what they want and talk about "the spirit of the Law and not the letter," pretending they are following the Apostle Paul when they aren't (cf. 2 Cor. 3:6). No Seventh-day Adventist goes to the synagogue on Saturday to witness to the Jews like Paul did; they all go to church, even though the early church met on Sunday (1 Cor. 16:2). They are all just as []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw343.xhtml_page_72}crooked as a dog's hind leg.

["Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath"]{.class_3054} (vs. 28). A declaration of Deity! The Liberals, Jehovah's Witnessess, and the Mohammedans all say Jesus never claimed to be God. What do you do with that verse, then? God says there in Ezekiel 20 that the Sabbath is [His]{.class_3055} (see also Lev. 23:3; Isa. 56:4; Ezek. 23:28, 44:24). Well, if the Sabbath belongs to God and Christ says He is Lord of the Sabbath, then Jesus is claiming to be God. He could make an exception to the Sabbath because He was the One who set it up. Jesus Christ is the Jehovah of the Old Testament. There is no difference between the "wrathful, vengeful" God of the Old Testament and the "loving, kind" God of the New Testament. That is an opium pipe dream of German rationalists like Gesenius, passed down from campfire to campfire until it reached apostates like Rick Warren, Brian McLaren, Rob Bell, and Joel Osteen.

Notice, though, that Jesus doesn't refer to Himself as the Son of God in verse 28, even though He is talking about His Deity. He's ["the Son of man."]{.class_3054} So ["man"]{.class_3054} in verse 27 isn't a reference to mankind in general, nor is it just a reference to the Jews. It is a reference to [one particular man:]{.class_3071} ["the man Christ Jesus"]{.class_3054} (1 Tim. 2:5). The Sabbath was made for [Him.]{.class_3071} It is [His]{.class_3071} Millennial reign to which it points prophetically, so [He]{.class_3071} can do with it, in the meantime, whatever [He]{.class_3071} pleases. As the Holy Spirit told you in Colossians 1:16---["ALL THINGS]{.class_3054} \[including the Sabbath\][ were created by him ]{.class_3054}\[Gen. 2:3 cf. John 1:3\][, and FOR HIM]{.class_3054} \[Mark 2:27--28\][."]{.class_3054}

[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml}

# Chapter 3 {.heading_97}

3:1 And he entered again into the synagogue; and there was a man there which had a withered hand.

2 And they watched him, whether he would heal him on the sabbath day; that they might accuse him.

3 And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.

4 And he saith unto them, Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? But they held their peace.

5 And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.

6 And the Pharisees went forth, and straightway took counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.

Here is another incident that took place in a synagogue on the Sabbath day. Back in chapter 1 when Christ cast the devil out of a man in the synagogue, nothing was said; it was all completely new to everyone (1:27). But after the confrontation in 2:23--28, the Pharisees and scribes are watching Christ like a hawk, and they only have one motivation in mind: to accuse Him of breaking the Sabbath (vs. 2) so the multitudes will stop listening to the One with authority (1:22) and start listening to [them]{.class_3055} again.

[In the synagogue was a man with ]{.class_3062}["a withered hand"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 1). All eyes were on Christ to see if He would heal the man. Jesus doesn't just heal the fellow, He makes a public example of him to "try the hearts" of the self-righteous religious leaders (Psa. 7:9; Prov. 17:3).]{.class_3062}

["Stand forth"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 3): Jesus calls the man with the withered hand "front and center." Then He addresses the Pharisees and scribes who are watching the whole thing in order to bring accusation against Him: ]{.class_3062}["Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill?"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 4).]{.class_3062}

[Now we will get into this in more detail when we get to chapter 12, but notice that Jesus Christ puts the question to the Pharisees and scribes, just as He did back in 2:8--9. It is a peculiar phenomenon in the Gospels that when the religious leaders question Jesus, He can always answer them, but when Jesus questions them, they can't (or ]{.class_3062}[won't]{.class_3063}[---vs. 4 cf. 11:33) answer Him. The picture is a sinner at the White Throne Judgment (Rom. 3:4, 19).]{.class_3062}

[When it comes to Christ's questions here, the answer is obvious to any honest man, but these Pharisees won't open their yaps because they have ulterior motives. There was nothing in the Mosaic Law against doing good to someone or saving someone's life on the Sabbath day. The command in Proverbs 3:27 is: ]{.class_3062}["Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thine hand to do it."]{.class_3061}

[The verse that follows that is particularly relevant to what you are reading here in Mark 3: ]{.class_3062}["Say not unto thy neighbour, Go, and come again, and TO MORROW I will give; when thou hast it by thee"]{.class_3061}[ (Prov. 3:28).]{.class_3062}

[The command to keep the Sabbath did not negate or trump the command to ]{.class_3062}["love thy neighbour as thyself"]{.class_3061}[ (Lev. 19:18). Here in Mark 3, the Jewish leaders have no concern at all for their ]{.class_3062}["neighbour"]{.class_3061}[ (the fellow with the withered hand); their goal is ]{.class_3062}["to do evil"]{.class_3061}[ to Jesus Christ and, in so doing, deprive the fellow of the chance for God to heal him. That was ]{.class_3062}[never]{.class_3063}[ the intent of the fourth commandment.]{.class_3062}

[Verse 5: ]{.class_3062}["And when he had looked round about on them WITH ANGER."]{.class_3061}[ Oh, Jesus, you should never have done that. It is a sin to get angry, isn't it? Well, it is in the new "Bibles." The modern versions make a sinner out of Jesus Christ by removing ]{.class_3062}["without a cause"]{.class_3061}[ from Matthew 5:22.]{.class_3062}

[Jesus Christ got angry with a bunch of self-righteous hypocrites (and if you don't believe me, read Matt. 23 and tell me with a straight face that was the "gentle Jesus" "sharing the love of Christ"), and He did so on more than one occasion (see John 2:15). Christ had just cause to be angry, so no sin was involved. In fact, the apostle whose example you are to follow (1 Cor. 11:1) ]{.class_3062}[commanded]{.class_3063}[ you to be angry as long as you didn't sin in your anger (Eph. 4:26). There are things about which you should be angry, and I don't mean personal affronts against yourself.]{.class_3062}

[Here in Mark 3, Jesus had the right motive for being angry: ]{.class_3062}["being grieved for the hardness of their hearts."]{.class_3061}[ This is the same God who became angry with Israel when they came out of Egypt and made a []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_75}golden calf, and murmured and complained, and lusted after the things of Egypt, and refused to enter the Promised Land because of the hardness of their hearts (Psa. 78:21). As Jehovah in the flesh, Jesus Christ had good reason to be angry with a people who had no use for His Law and tried to make a liar out of His Father. If you want to see just how angry He can get with everybody in Pensacola, Mobile, Ft. Walton Beach, New Orleans, Dallas, New York City, Washington, D.C., Atlanta, San Francisco, Chicago, Des Moines, Seattle, Los Angeles, Rome, London, Paris, Mecca, Medina, Beijing, Hyderabad, Delhi, Johannesburg, Rio de Janeiro, Mexico City, etc., etc., etc., who turns down His free offer of salvation, just wait till the White Throne Judgment.]{.class_3062}

["He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the WRATH OF GOD ABIDETH ON HIM"]{.class_3061}[ (John 3:36).]{.class_3062}

["The same shall drink of the wine of THE WRATH OF GOD, which is poured out without mixture into the cup of his indignation; and he shall be TORMENTED WITH FIRE and brimstone in the presence of the holy angels, and IN THE PRESENCE OF THE LAMB" ]{.class_3061}[(Rev. 14:10).]{.class_3090}

[That's ]{.class_3062}["the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world"]{.class_3061}[ (John 1:29). He will take every Christ-rejecting sinner who turns up his nose at the blood atonement for his sins and pitch him into the Lake of Fire (Matt. 25:41) and not think twice about it. And He will do it because He ]{.class_3062}["is angry with the wicked every day"]{.class_3061}[ (Psa. 7:11).]{.class_3062}

[Listen, people, God, if He is God, has to be ]{.class_3062}[perfect;]{.class_3063}[ He has to be ]{.class_3062}[sinless.]{.class_3063}[ But not only is He absolutely holy, He is also a balanced Being. That means if He is holy, He isn't going to tolerate sin and unrighteousness forever. He is not going to love everybody the same way or to the same degree.]{.class_3062}

[It is true that ]{.class_3062}["God so loved the world, that he gave His only begotten Son"]{.class_3061}[ (John 3:16). When it came to making the payment for sin, Jesus Christ loved Adolf Hitler, Joseph Stalin, Ted Bundy, Torquemada, Nero, Caligula, Genghis Khan, Mohammed, Tamerlane, Pope Innocent IV, Pope Urban VI, Bloody Mary, H. H. Holmes, and Ed Gein just as much as He loved Mary, Joseph, John the Baptist, Peter, John, Paul, Polycarp, Ignatius, Martin Luther, William Tyndale, John Knox, William Carey, Adoniram Judson, Queen Victoria, D. L. Moody, and William Booth. But if you think God loves them all enough to take them all to Heaven with no regard as to how they respond to His Son, you []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_76}have another think coming.]{.class_3062}

[A God who loves purity, truth, and righteousness as much as He loves filth, deception, and wickedness is a twisted, perverted God. That is NOT the God of the Bible. The prophet Habakkuk said of God, ]{.class_3062}["Thou art of purer eyes than to behold evil, and canst not look on iniquity"]{.class_3061}[ (Hab. 1:13).]{.class_3062}

[The psalmist said, ]{.class_3062}["For thou art not a God that hath pleasure in wickedness: neither shall evil dwell with thee . . . THOU HATEST ALL WORKERS OF INIQUITY"]{.class_3061}[ (Psa. 5:4--5). That's not God "loving the sinner and hating the sin" at all; that's God ]{.class_3062}[hating the sinner himself.]{.class_3063}[ He hates him so much that He ]{.class_3062}[burns him forever]{.class_3063}[ (Psa. 11:5--6; Matt. 25:41; Rev. 14:11, 20:10--15).]{.class_3062}

[That]{.class_3063}[ is the God of the Bible. That is the God modern Americans and Europeans can't stand. He is not only a God of perfect love and grace, He is a God of ]{.class_3062}[perfect hatred]{.class_3063}[ (and yes! there is such a thing---Psa. 139:22) and judgment. They don't mind that God of love, but that God of ]{.class_3062}[vengeance]{.class_3063}[ "am sumptin' else"! But ]{.class_3062}[that]{.class_3063}[ is the Jesus Christ of the Book---He's ]{.class_3062}[both]{.class_3063}[ (see 2 Thess. 1:7--8).]{.class_3062}

[The One who ]{.class_3062}[died]{.class_3063}[ for you to keep you out of Hell wouldn't do wrong or cut corners to keep you out. If you turn down the ]{.class_3062}[one]{.class_3063}[ right way to get you out, He'll just let you burn to satisfy His justice, and Americans have been raised on so much sentimental slop that they can't envision a God like that. But that's the God of that Book, and it doesn't matter one way or another what you think of Him. You can reject Him because you don't like Him being that way if you want, but you will do it at your own peril.]{.class_3062}

[Back to Mark 3:5---]{.class_3062}["he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand."]{.class_3061}[ Christ is commanding the man to do something ]{.class_3062}[he can't do.]{.class_3063}[ The fellow couldn't stretch out his hand; it was withered. The guy had to ]{.class_3062}[act by faith:]{.class_3063}[ he had to believe he could do something he couldn't do.]{.class_3062}

[That's exactly what God asks any sinner in this age to do: He asks you to believe a Jew 2,000 years ago lived a sinless life, then died on a Roman cross to take away your sins, then rose from the dead. You've never seen any of that, but you are to believe it nevertheless. You are to believe it with no "empirical evidence" that you can see or reproduce under "controlled conditions." You are to believe that if your body rots to dust or is cremated or is eaten by sharks or is blown up by a bomb, that God can put it back together again. That's ]{.class_3062}[impossible]{.class_3063}[ from a human standpoint. But that's on what I am depending to get me to Glory.]{.class_3062}

[That Jew believed what Christ told him to do and stretched out his []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_77}hand. When he did, he was healed. You have a better promise than that: ]{.class_3062}["Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and THOU SHALT BE SAVED"]{.class_3061}[ (Acts 16:31). Receive Jesus Christ as your Saviour (John 1:12), and you will become just like Him someday (1 John 3:1--2).]{.class_3062}

[What was the reaction of the Jewish leadership to this fellow being healed? Verse 6---]{.class_3072}["And the Pharisees . . . ."]{.class_3073}[ Those were the Bible teachers of the nation; they were the Conservative Fundamentalists of their day. ]{.class_3072}[". . . went forth, and straightway took counsel with the Herodians . . . ."]{.class_3074}[ ]{.class_3075}[That was a political party. The Herodians supported an egomaniacal Edomite as "King of the Jews" in complete disregard and opposition to Deuteronomy 17:15. These were political liberals. So the "religious right" got together with the political "left" ]{.class_3062}[against Jesus Christ:]{.class_3063}[ ]{.class_3062}[". . . against him, how they might destroy him."]{.class_3061}[ Like I told you back under 2:12, amazement and glorifying God quickly gave way to thoughts of ]{.class_3062}[murder]{.class_3063}[ because of ]{.class_3062}[envy]{.class_3063}[ (15:10).]{.class_3062}

7 But Jesus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judaea,

8 And from Jerusalem, and from Idumaea, and from beyond Jordan; and they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they had heard what great things he did, came unto him.

9 And he spake to his disciples, that a small ship should wait on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.

10 For he had healed many; insomuch that they pressed upon him for to touch him, as many as had plagues.

11 And unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.

12 And he straitly charged them that they should not make him known.

Once more Jesus has to leave the city and go out by the seashore of Galilee because of the multitude of people that thronged to see Him. Notice in verse 7 that they ["followed HIM."]{.class_3054} With all the "to do" made by the Catholics over "Mary, the mother of God" and "our Co-Redemptrix," one should carefully note how [seldom]{.class_3055} she appears in the Gospel narratives.[ No one ]{.class_3055}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_78}in the Gospels follows Mary or prays to her; they follow [Jesus.]{.class_3055} And when you get to the epistles, [no one]{.class_3055} is commanded to follow Mary across the street or to give her the time of day. But at least [four times]{.class_3055} (1 Cor. 4:16, 11:1; Phil. 3:17; 1 Thess. 1:6) you are told to follow the Apostle to the Gentiles (Rom. 11:13) who wrote the epistle to the Romans (Rom. 1:1--7), and it [wasn't Peter]{.class_3055} (Gal. 2:7). Someone is trying to tie you to a [female demon]{.class_3055} to destroy you (Jer. 7:18).

[Notice the following Christ had: ]{.class_3062}["from Galilee"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 7)---that's northern Israel; ]{.class_3062}["from Judaea"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 7)---that's southern Israel; ]{.class_3062}["from Jerusalem"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 8)---the capital of Israel; ]{.class_3062}["from Idumaea"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 8)---that's Edomite Gentile territory down below the Dead Sea; ]{.class_3062}["from beyond Jordan"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 8)---that's Transjordan where Reuben, Gad, and the half tribe of Manasseh lived back in the Old Testament; ]{.class_3062}["about Tyre and Sidon"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 8)---Gentile territory up beyond Galilee by the Mediterranean. So people are coming to see Jesus from as far south as northern Arabia, as far north as Lebanon, and from all over Israel on both sides of the Jordan; and both Jews and Gentiles are coming to see Him. No wonder those Jewish leaders are out to get rid of Him; for those intent on keeping control of the ]{.class_3062}["vineyard"]{.class_3061}[ (see chap. 12), Jesus Christ was "bad news."]{.class_3062}

[Now He is by the seashore, and the crowd is thronging Him to the point where they are pushing Him into the water. So just as He did over in Luke 5, Jesus gets into ]{.class_3062}["a small ship"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 9) and puts some space between Him and the crowd. This is not the same incident as Luke 5 (see our remarks under 2:1), but one should not be surprised or claim a contradiction just because he is not experienced in real life. "Celebrities" often get mobbed wherever they go and have to resort to a "Standard Operating Procedure" to get away. It would not surprise me at all to find out that Jesus often had to get in a boat whenever He taught by the Sea of Galilee, but that you only have two or three incidents of it recorded in the Scriptures for you.]{.class_3062}

[Verse 10 tells you that ]{.class_3062}["many as had plagues"]{.class_3061}[ ]{.class_3062}["pressed upon him for to touch him."]{.class_3061}[ Why? Because ]{.class_3062}["he had healed many,"]{.class_3061}[ and they figured if they could just touch Him, they would be healed. Luke 6:19 tells you that is exactly what happened. That's why the woman with the ]{.class_3062}["issue of blood"]{.class_3061}[ over in chapter 5 here in Mark tries to touch Him; she hears about what happens here in verse 10 and wants to "get in on the goodies."]{.class_3062}

[Now incidents like verse 10 and 5:28--29 are what led to such nonsense as]{.class_3062}[ "The Robe."]{.class_3063}[ The pagan idea by the Catholics is that there is something magical in Christ's clothing (never called a "robe" anywhere in the Gospels; see our comments on 15:24) because it had touched His physical body. That is called "veneration of relics" in Catholic mythology and has resulted in such obscene, absurd nonsense as a vial of Mary's breast milk and two skulls of John the Baptist (one from when he was a child and the other from when he was an adult!). There was nothing miraculous in Christ's garments; it was the ]{.class_3062}[faith]{.class_3063}[ placed in Jesus Christ to heal that God honored (5:34). If you did have Christ's foreskin (supposedly given to Charlemagne by Pope Leo II in A.D. 800) or some towel Jesus used to dry His face (the "Mandylion of Edessa"), you wouldn't have anything that could cure you of a hangnail.]{.class_3062}

We have already discussed the material in verses 11--12 in our comments on 1:24--25 (which see).

13 And he goeth up into a mountain, and calleth unto him whom he would: and they came unto him.

14 And he ordained twelve, that they should be with him, and that he might send them forth to preach,

15 And to have power to heal sicknesses, and to cast out devils:

16 And Simon he surnamed Peter;

17 And James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he surnamed them Boanerges, which is, The sons of thunder:

18 And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Canaanite,

19 And Judas Iscariot, which also betrayed him: and they went into an house.

Now what transpired in verses 7--12 is the practical reason for what you read here. Jesus Christ was restricted in His earthly body. Although He never gave up His omnipresense (see our comments on John 3:13 in that Commentary), while here on earth, Jesus limited Himself to the same limitations of any normal human being: He hungered, He thirsted, He became tired. As a man, Jesus' body could []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_80}not be everywhere at once, so He chose twelve of His disciples to go out and preach for Him (vs. 14), and He gave them the same power ["to heal sicknesses, and to cast out devils"]{.class_3054} as He had (vs. 15).

["He ordained twelve"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 14). This is an official transference of authority and power to fulfill a given commission, symbolized by the laying on of hands from the "Commissioner" to the "commissionee" (see Acts 6:6, 13:3). The number twelve is chosen because they are commissioned to preach to Israel (Matt. 10:6), and twelve is the number of Israel (twelve sons of Jacob, twelve tribes of Israel). Although there is not necessarily a disciple ]{.class_3062}[from]{.class_3063}[ each of the twelve tribes (like the spies in Num. 13), each of those disciples ]{.class_3062}[represents]{.class_3063}[ one of the twelve tribes (cf. Matt. 19:28).]{.class_3062}

[These twelve men are called ]{.class_3062}["apostles"]{.class_3061}[ in Matthew 10:2. The word means "sent one" (]{.class_3062}["that he might SEND THEM FORTH"]{.class_3061}[). It became an official office of the Church (Eph. 4:11), and like all offices of the Church, the apostles were ]{.class_3062}[male]{.class_3063}[ (see our comments on Rom. 16:1 in that Commentary). It was a special office, with accompanying ]{.class_3062}[signs]{.class_3063}[ (Mark 16:17--18 cf. 2 Cor. 12:12), that does not exist today (see our comments on Rev. 2:2 in that Commentary). In the book of Acts, the apostles were divided into two groups: the Twelve (including Matthias who took Judas' place---Acts 1:20--26) and a secondary group, which included ]{.class_3062}["James the Lord's brother"]{.class_3061}[ (Gal. 1:19), Barnabas (Acts 14:14), and, of course, Paul (1 Cor. 1:1). You can find a complete list of these other apostles in our comments on 1 Corinthians 4:2 in that Commentary.]{.class_3062}

Here, we will take each of these men and give you the basic things you need to know about him. In the lists of apostles as given in Matthew 10, here, Luke 6, and Acts 1, Peter is always the first name on the list. So the Popes use this as one of the reasons for the supremacy of Peter above the other apostles. That ignores several facts:

[1) He was the only one of the apostles to be called ]{.class_3062}[Satan]{.class_3063}[ (Matt. 16:23).]{.class_3062}

2\) He was not in charge of the assembly of the apostles and elders at Jerusalem in Acts 15 (see Acts 15:13).

[3) Peter himself said he was only an elder among other elders (1 Pet. 5:1) and that Jesus Christ was ]{.class_3062}["the chief Shepherd"]{.class_3061}[ (1 Pet. 5:4).]{.class_3062}

[4) ]{.class_3062}[Paul,]{.class_3063}[ not Peter, was given as the apostle you are to follow (1 Cor. 11:1), and where Peter contradicted Paul, Peter was ]{.class_3062}[rebuked]{.class_3063}[ for it (Gal. 2:11).]{.class_3062}

[Notice that Peter is given by both of his names: ]{.class_3062}["Simon,"]{.class_3061}[ meaning "shifting sand," and ]{.class_3062}["Peter,"]{.class_3061}[ meaning ]{.class_3062}["a stone"]{.class_3061}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_81}[ (John 1:42). That's a symbol of ]{.class_3062}[conversion]{.class_3063}[ (see our remarks under 2:14).]{.class_3062}

[Here is another perversion of the words of Christ by the Pope. While the word ]{.class_3062}[Peter]{.class_3063}[ may indeed mean "a stone" or "a rock," he is most certainly NOT the Rock on which the Church was founded or built (see Matt. 16:18 and our comments on that passage in that Commentary). That Rock is identified as ]{.class_3062}[Jesus Christ]{.class_3063}[ by Paul (1 Cor. 10:4, 3:11; Eph. 2:20), by ]{.class_3062}[Peter]{.class_3063}[ (1 Pet. 2:6--8), and ]{.class_3062}[Jesus Christ Himself]{.class_3063}[ (Matt. 21:42--44; Mark 12:10; Luke 20:17--18).]{.class_3062}

The next two are dealt with together; they are James and John. As we learned back in 1:19, they are brothers, and their daddy is Zebedee. They follow Peter here because those three are part of Christ's famous "inner circle" (5:37, 9:2, 14:33). That is why Andrew is separated from his brother Peter in verse 18.

Another reason James and John are given together is because they stick together as brothers. In 10:35, they both come to Christ together to ask Him for the right and left hands in His kingdom.

[Notice that Jesus calls them together ]{.class_3062}["Boanerges"]{.class_3061}[; the name means ]{.class_3062}["sons of thunder."]{.class_3061}[ Christ calls them that because they have a temper, and if you want to see an example of that temper, read Luke 9:54. John starts out as a "son of thunder," but he ends up "the beloved apostle" (John 21:20, 24). It was close fellowship with Jesus Christ that curbed that temper.]{.class_3062}

[Verse 18: ]{.class_3062}["And Andrew, and Philip."]{.class_3061}[ Those are the personal workers of the bunch. It was Andrew who brought Peter to Christ (John 1:40--41), and it was Andrew who brought the lad to Christ with the five loaves and two fish when Christ fed the 5,000 (John 6:8--9). It was Philip who brought Nathanael to Christ in John 1:45; he too comes up by name in the feeding of the 5,000 (John 6:5--7).]{.class_3062}

Also, Philip is the disciple who asks Jesus at the Last Supper to show the disciples the Father. Jesus' answer is one of Christ's famous claims of Deity that no Liberal or Mohammedan can seem to find:

["Jesus saith unto him, Have I been so long time with you, and yet hast thou not known me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the Father; and how sayest thou then, Shew us the Father?"]{.class_3061}[ (John 14:9).]{.class_3062}

[The next two are ]{.class_3062}["Bartholomew, and Matthew."]{.class_3061}[ Matthew is the Levi of 2:14 (cf. Matt. 9:9). Bartholomew is usually identified with the Nathanael of the Gospel of John (John 1:46--49, 21:2).]{.class_3062}

[Then there are ]{.class_3062}["Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus."]{.class_3061}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_82}[ This is the famous "Doubting Thomas" of John 20:24--29. He was called ]{.class_3062}["Didymus,"]{.class_3061}[ which is the Greek word for "twin" (John 20:24). Outside of John 20, he is known for being willing to die with Christ in John 11:16 and for soliciting Christ's famous declaration of being ]{.class_3062}["the way, the truth, and the life"]{.class_3061}[ in John 14:5--6.]{.class_3062}

["James the son of Alphaeus"]{.class_3061}[ is a second James among the apostles. We have already commented on his possible relationship to Matthew under our remarks on 2:14. He is identified with ]{.class_3062}["James the less"]{.class_3061}[ by some expositors in Mark 15:40 because the Mary of that verse was married to a man named ]{.class_3062}["Cleophas"]{.class_3061}[ (John 19:25), and Cleophas is supposed to be the Greek form of the Hebrew word ]{.class_3062}[Alphaeus.]{.class_3063}[ According to some expositors, the designation ]{.class_3062}["the less"]{.class_3061}[ means that he was a short fellow; either shorter in stature than ]{.class_3062}["James the son of Zebedee"]{.class_3061}[ or shorter than any of the other apostles. It should be noted that this James is not ]{.class_3062}["the Lord's brother"]{.class_3061}[ of Galatians 1:9. We will cover that more when we get to 6:3.]{.class_3062}

[Thomas and the other James are followed by]{.class_3059}[ "Thaddaeus, and Simon the Canaanite."]{.class_3060}[ Thaddaeus is known by two other names in the Gospels. His ]{.class_3059}["surname"]{.class_3060}[ (i.e., his family name; like a last name) was Thaddaeus, but his given name was ]{.class_3059}["Lebbaeus"]{.class_3060}[ (Matt. 10:3). In comparing the apostolic lists in Matthew 10 and here with those of Luke 6:16 and Acts 1:13, it is apparent that Thaddaeus had a third name:]{.class_3059}[ "Judas the brother of James"]{.class_3060}[ (James being ]{.class_3059}["James the son of Alphaeus"]{.class_3060}[). This would be the author of the book of Jude (Jude 1; see our remarks on the authorship of Jude in the Introduction to that book in the ]{.class_3059}[Bible Believer's Commentary on the General Epistles,]{.class_3094}[ Vol. II). In John 14:22, Thaddaeus is called ]{.class_3059}["Judas . . . ]{.class_3060}[not Iscariot"]{.class_3061}[ to distinguish him from ]{.class_3062}["Judas . . . the traitor"]{.class_3061}[ (Luke 6:16). There is a chance that Thaddaeus was given or took the name ]{.class_3062}[Judas]{.class_3063}[ upon his conversion to Christ, much as Levi took the name ]{.class_3062}[Matthew.]{.class_3063}[ If Matthew were related to the second James through Alphaeus, this would give a family of three brothers among the apostles: Matthew (Levi), James, and Judas (Thaddaeus).]{.class_3062}

["Simon the Canaanite"]{.class_3061}[ is the ]{.class_3062}["Simon Zelotes"]{.class_3061}[ of Luke 6:15 and Acts 1:13. The Zealots were a militant group that fought against the Roman army of occupation much as the "Stern Gang" fought against the British in the 1940s (see ]{.class_3062}[Israel: A Deadly Piece of Dirt,]{.class_3063}[ 2001, pg. 287). They would have been considered terrorists by the Romans. Simon was called ]{.class_3062}["the Canaanite"]{.class_3061}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_83}[ not because that was his nationality (cf. Matt. 15:22), but because he believed the land of Canaan (the Biblical designation for the Promised Land. The Romans were the ones who called it "Palestine," Palestine being the Latin word for ]{.class_3062}[Philistine]{.class_3063}[) belonged to the ]{.class_3062}[Jews,]{.class_3063}[ not the Romans. Jesus got this rabid "Zionist" to forsake his guerilla warfare against the Romans and become a preacher. Contrast that with getting a Jewish tax collector for Rome to forsake his wealth to do the same thing. Politically, Matthew and Simon were at opposite ends of the spectrum, but as disciples of Christ, they came to love each other (John 13:34, 15:12, 17).]{.class_3062}

[Now here in verse 19, ]{.class_3062}["Judas Iscariot"]{.class_3061}[ is set apart from all the rest of the apostles, and the reason is obvious: he is the betrayer of Christ. The first time he shows up in all four Gospels, that is how he is identified: that will be Matthew 10:4; Mark 3:19; Luke 6:16; and John 6:71.]{.class_3062}

[Judas stands out in other ways, though. He was the only apostle who was not from Galilee. His surname ]{.class_3062}[Iscariot]{.class_3063}[ means "man of Kerioth" (]{.class_3062}[Is]{.class_3063}[---"man"; ]{.class_3062}[cariot---]{.class_3063}["Kerioth"). There are two Kerioths in the Bible. The first is in Judah (Josh. 15:25); the other is in Moab (Jer. 48:24, 41; Amos 2:2). I take Judas to be from the one in Moab (which would have been in the Jewish province of Perea during the time of Christ). Now why is that? Because right after the first mention of Kerioth of Moab, the Holy Spirit told you this:]{.class_3062}

["The HORN OF MOAB is cut off, and HIS arm is broken, saith the LORD"]{.class_3061}[ (Jer. 48:25).]{.class_3062}

[That ]{.class_3062}["horn"]{.class_3061}[ there in Jeremiah 48:25 is a ]{.class_3062}[person]{.class_3063}[ (note the ]{.class_3062}["his"]{.class_3061}[) who has a ]{.class_3062}[broken arm.]{.class_3063}[ Why, I know someone in the Scriptures who is called ]{.class_3062}["a little horn"]{.class_3061}[ and who has a bad arm. It is the ]{.class_3062}[Antichrist.]{.class_3063}

["I considered the horns, and, behold, there came up among them another LITTLE HORN, before whom there were three of the first horns plucked up by the roots: and, behold, in this horn were eyes like the eyes of man, and a mouth speaking great things]{.class_3061}[ \[see Rev. 13:4--5\]]{.class_3062}["]{.class_3061}[ (Dan. 7:8).]{.class_3062}

["Woe to the IDOL SHEPHERD]{.class_3061}[ \[you read it right. That wasn't a "mistake" in your ]{.class_3062}[King James]{.class_3063}[ Bible. That's a ]{.class_3062}[false]{.class_3063}[ shepherd connected with ]{.class_3062}[idolatry; ]{.class_3063}[as in Rev. 13:14--15\]]{.class_3062}[ that leaveth the flock! THE SWORD SHALL BE UPON HIS ARM, and upon his right eye: HIS ARM SHALL BE CLEAN DRIED UP, and his right eye shall be utterly darkened"]{.class_3061}[ (Zech. 11:17).]{.class_3062}

[The Antichrist is called ]{.class_3062}["the son of perdition"]{.class_3061}[ in 2 Thessalonians []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_84}2:3. Why, that is exactly what Christ called Judas in John 17:12. So Judas Iscariot is a type of the Antichrist, which is why I connect him with Kerioth of Moab instead of the one in Judah.]{.class_3062}

Moreover, Judas has a nature that is more than just a man. Look at John 6:70--71.

"Jesus answered them, Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is A DEVIL? He spake of JUDAS ISCARIOT the son of Simon: for he it was that should betray him, being one of the twelve."

[In that passage, there is ]{.class_3062}["A devil"]{.class_3061}[ who is not THE Devil (see our remarks under 1:32). But that devil is ]{.class_3062}[Judas Iscariot.]{.class_3063}[ So while Judas may have had a ]{.class_3062}[human]{.class_3063}[ nature (]{.class_3062}["son of Simon"]{.class_3061}[), he also had a ]{.class_3062}[diabolic]{.class_3063}[ nature: that of ]{.class_3062}["a devil."]{.class_3061}[ Judas is a ]{.class_3062}[devil incarnate.]{.class_3063}

[That's called ]{.class_3062}["the mystery of iniquity"]{.class_3061}[ in 2 Thessalonians 2:7, and it stands in contrast to ]{.class_3062}["the mystery of godliness"]{.class_3061}[ in 1 Timothy 3:16. That is, just as Jesus Christ is ]{.class_3062}["God . . . manifest in the flesh"]{.class_3061}[ (changed in all the new "Bibles" so you will lose the ]{.class_3062}[definitive]{.class_3063}[ passage on the doctrine of the Incarnation), the Antichrist is ]{.class_3062}[Satan in the flesh.]{.class_3063}[ Jesus is the Son of God; the Antichrist is the Son of Perdition. That word ]{.class_3062}["perdition"]{.class_3061}[ there in 2 Thessalonians 2:3 isn't just a ]{.class_3062}[state]{.class_3063}[ ]{.class_3062}[of damnation;]{.class_3063}[ it is the ]{.class_3062}[name of a being.]{.class_3063}[ It's a proper name in Revelation 9:11---]{.class_3062}["Apollyon."]{.class_3061}[ Apollyon is a fallen angel who is King over the demoniac spirits in the Bottomless Pit.]{.class_3062}

[As we told you back in 1:24, the time when Christ destroys the Devil and sends him to the Bottomless Pit is ]{.class_3062}[not now;]{.class_3063}[ it's the ]{.class_3062}[Second Advent]{.class_3063}[ (Rev. 20:1--2). But there is one devil who made it there ahead of schedule: Judas Iscariot. When Judas hung himself, he didn't go to Hell; he went ]{.class_3062}["TO HIS OWN PLACE"]{.class_3061}[ (Acts 1:25)---the ]{.class_3062}[Bottomless Pit.]{.class_3063}

[Do you know what you read in Revelation 17:8? ]{.class_3062}["The beast]{.class_3061}[ \[that's the Son of Perdition\] ]{.class_3062}[that thou sawest WAS]{.class_3061}[ \[Judas had been on earth with John, the author of Revelation, back in the Gospels\]]{.class_3062}[, and IS NOT]{.class_3061}[ \[Judas wasn't on earth when John wrote the Revelation\]]{.class_3062}[; and SHALL ASCEND OUT OF THE BOTTOMLESS PIT]{.class_3061}[ \[that's Apollyon---the spirit of Judas---coming back as the Antichrist in the Tribulation\]]{.class_3062}[, and GO INTO PERDITION]{.class_3061}[ \[the Antichrist is thrown into the Lake of Fire at the Second Advent---Rev. 19:20---and Satan is chained in the Bottomless Pit\]]{.class_3062}[."]{.class_3061}

[That]{.class_3063}[ is why Judas is set off by himself here in Mark 3:19.]{.class_3062}

20 And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.

21 And when his friends heard of it, they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is beside himself.

22 And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils.

23 And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?

24 And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.

25 And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.

26 And if Satan rise up against himself, and be divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.

27 No man can enter into a strong man's house, and spoil his goods, except he will first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.

Now look at what follows the one apostle who was ["a devil."]{.class_3054} After choosing His apostles, Jesus goes with them to a house, and just as you read back in 1:33 and 2:1--2, a crowd gathers where Jesus and the apostles are until they can't even have dinner.

[Verse 21 says, ]{.class_3062}["And when his FRIENDS heard of it, they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is beside himself."]{.class_3061}[ Now those ]{.class_3062}["friends"]{.class_3061}[ aren't His disciples (cf. Luke 12:4; John 15:15) who believe on Him and what He says. These ]{.class_3062}["friends"]{.class_3061}[ are trying to haul Him off to the "funny farm" because they think He's ]{.class_3062}[nuts.]{.class_3063}[ ]{.class_3062}["He is beside himself"]{.class_3061}[ is an idiom for saying "He's mad," "he's crazy" (see Acts 26:24).]{.class_3062}

[Now who are these ]{.class_3062}["friends"]{.class_3061}[ here in verse 21? Look at verse 31: ]{.class_3062}["Then came HIS BRETHREN AND HIS MOTHER, and standing without, sent unto him, calling him."]{.class_3061}[ When this is pointed out to Jesus, He distinguishes His disciples from His physical family (vss. 33--35). Why is that? Because ]{.class_3062}[His brethren didn't believe in Him]{.class_3063}[ (John 7:5). So in verse 21, His earthly family, who loved Him (]{.class_3062}["his friends"]{.class_3061}[), are trying to take Him away because they are concerned for His sanity; they do that because, to them, He is just "big brother," not the Son of God.]{.class_3062}

[Notice the conclusion the scribes reach in verse 22 about Christ's alleged "madness":]{.class_3062}[ "He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 22). Over in John 10:20, His enemies say,]{.class_3062}[ "He hath a devil, and is mad."]{.class_3061}

[Now we are not going to comment here on the connection of madness to demon possession; we will reserve those comments for 9:22. Suffice it to say, here, that some madness is caused by devils and some ]{.class_3062}[is not.]{.class_3063}[ The scribes believe a demon is causing madness in Jesus because He is claiming to be ]{.class_3062}[God]{.class_3063}[ (see John 10:30--33). If Jesus were not God, He was either one of the most horrendous, despicable ]{.class_3062}[liars]{.class_3063}[ who ever lived, or He was as ]{.class_3062}[mad as a hatter.]{.class_3063}[ Some wit likened it to a fellow saying he was a ]{.class_3062}[poached egg.]{.class_3063}[ Jesus Christ was either a ]{.class_3062}[liar]{.class_3063}[ or a ]{.class_3062}[lunatic,]{.class_3063}[ or He was who He said He was: the ]{.class_3062}[Lord.]{.class_3063}

["He hath Beelzebub."]{.class_3061}[ The letter "B" has bad connotations in the Scriptures. "J" has good associations: Joshua, Judah, Jehoshaphat, Jesus Christ, et al. That's because the letter "J" is the first letter in ]{.class_3062}[Jehovah.]{.class_3063}[ The reason God called Jeconiah ]{.class_3062}["Coniah"]{.class_3061}[ in Jeremiah 22:24--28 is because Jeconiah was so wicked that God didn't want His name associated with the man. The "Je" on Jeconiah is an abbreviation for Jehovah, so God takes it off the guy's name.]{.class_3062}

["B," on the other hand, has bad associations: Baal, Balak, Balaam, Baasha, Babel, Babylon. All that comes from Baal (also called Bel in Isa. 46:1; Jer. 50:2, 51:44). Baal was the false god that plagued Israel all throughout the Old Testament; it was associated with the worship of the sun (see our comments on Judges 2:13 in the ]{.class_3062}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Judges and Ruth]{.class_3063}[). He was a Canaanite/Phoenician god, and ]{.class_3062}[sex]{.class_3063}[ was a part of his worship. Baal and Ashtoreth worship became the source for the ]{.class_3062}[Roman Catholic religion.]{.class_3063}

[The word ]{.class_3062}[Baal]{.class_3063}[ means "Lord," and it is obviously a ]{.class_3062}[false]{.class_3063}[ lord (see 1 Cor. 8:5) in opposition to the true LORD (]{.class_3062}[Jehovah]{.class_3063}[). ]{.class_3062}["Beelzebub"]{.class_3061}[ is the Greek form of the Old Testament ]{.class_3062}["Baalzebub,"]{.class_3061}[ a Philistine god (2 Kings 1:2). The name means "Lord of flies." Beelzebub was the god of ]{.class_3062}[filth, ]{.class_3063}[and the Jews of Christ's day counted it as another name for Satan (]{.class_3062}["the prince of the devils"]{.class_3061}[).]{.class_3062}

[Notice that ]{.class_3062}["devils"]{.class_3061}[ are likened here to ]{.class_3062}[flies]{.class_3063}[---small winged creatures. So demons (devils) are ]{.class_3062}[not]{.class_3063}[ fallen angels: no angel in the Book has wings. Angels in that Book appear as ]{.class_3062}[men]{.class_3063}[ (see our comments on Judges 13:6 in that Commentary). That being the case, you are not going to fit seven of them in one woman (see our comments on Luke 8:2 []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_87}in that Commentary) or a thousand of them in one man (see our remarks in this Commentary under Mark 5:1--13). But if devils were the size of birds (4:4, 15) or winged insects, then it would be no problem.]{.class_3062}

[Jesus answers the accusation of these scribes (He is casting out devils by the power of Satan) with ]{.class_3059}["parables."]{.class_3060}[ Parables are illustrative stories given to convey spiritual truths. The three parables given here are: 1) ]{.class_3059}["a kingdom . . . divided against itself"]{.class_3060}[ (vs. 24), 2) ]{.class_3059}["a house . . . ]{.class_3060}[divided against itself"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 25), and 3) ]{.class_3062}["bind the strong man; and . . . spoil his house"]{.class_3061}[ (vs. 27).]{.class_3062}

[The first two ]{.class_3062}["parables"]{.class_3061}[ are simple, but to the point. If a kingdom is ]{.class_3062}["divided against itself,"]{.class_3061}[ then there is ]{.class_3062}[civil war;]{.class_3063}[ a house ]{.class_3062}["divided against itself"]{.class_3061}[ is a ]{.class_3062}[divorce]{.class_3063}[ or a ]{.class_3062}[disowning]{.class_3063}[ or ]{.class_3062}[disinheriting.]{.class_3063}[ If Satan goes around casting out his own evil spirits, then he is undermining his cause.]{.class_3062}

[Now what that means in regards to "proselytizing" (transferring from one religion to another) is that the Devil is not leaving a Jew who converts to Catholicism or a Methodist who converts to Islam or an Episcopalian who converts to Buddhism, etc. That's just a "feint" on the part of Satan: it's a ]{.class_3062}[deception,]{.class_3063}[ a ]{.class_3062}[distraction.]{.class_3063}[ Those Pharisees who had the Devil for their father (John 8:44), when they sailed over to Athens or Rome and converted some Zeus-worshiper or Jupiter-worshiper to Rabbinical Judaism, ]{.class_3062}[nothing]{.class_3063}[ happened to the "convert" but ]{.class_3062}[double damnation.]{.class_3063}

["Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye compass sea and land to make one proselyte, and when he is made, ye make him TWOFOLD MORE THE CHILD OF HELL than yourselves"]{.class_3061}[ (Matt. 23:15).]{.class_3062}

[So when you see some Charismatic fakir "casting out demons" or a Roman Catholic Priest performing an "exorcism," that is not Satan against Satan at all. That is ]{.class_3062}[religious theater,]{.class_3063}[ put on for a ]{.class_3062}[show,]{.class_3063}[ in order to ]{.class_3062}[deceive the gullible.]{.class_3063}[ When Jesus Christ or the apostles cast out devils, it was for the intent of freeing the person so he or she could experience ]{.class_3062}[salvation]{.class_3063}[ and the ]{.class_3062}[New Birth.]{.class_3063}[ With Jesus Christ and the apostles, when the demon-possessed person was freed, a transference of kingdoms occurred (Col. 1:13); the person was put in a new household (Eph. 2:19). ]{.class_3062}["The strong man"]{.class_3061}[ was spoiled of his ]{.class_3062}["goods"]{.class_3061}[ (Mark 3:27).]{.class_3062}

[Now verse 27 has a further ]{.class_3062}[prophetic]{.class_3063}[ element to it that is not readily apparent in the passage.]{.class_3062}[ "The strong man"]{.class_3061}[ of the parable is obviously the ]{.class_3062}[Devil,]{.class_3063}[ and the ]{.class_3062}["man"]{.class_3061}[ who enters the ]{.class_3062}["strong man's house"]{.class_3061}[ to ]{.class_3062}["spoil his goods"]{.class_3061}[ is ]{.class_3062}[Jesus Christ.]{.class_3063}[ In the book of Revelation, Jesus likens Himself to ]{.class_3062}["a thief"]{.class_3061}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_88}[ at His Second Coming (Rev. 3:3, 16:15). Both Paul and Peter liken the Second Advent to ]{.class_3062}["a thief in the night"]{.class_3061}[ (1 Thess. 5:2; 2 Pet. 3:10). So when Jesus is crucified, He is crucified between ]{.class_3062}["two thieves"]{.class_3061}[ as one of them (15:27).]{.class_3062}

[When Jesus Christ comes back, he comes to "steal" everything Satan has, starting with the most valuable possessions. That's ]{.class_3062}[you]{.class_3063}[ if you are saved. Satan is called ]{.class_3062}["the god of this world"]{.class_3061}[ in 2 Corinthians 4:4. At the Lord's temptation, the Devil told Christ that ]{.class_3062}["all the kingdoms of the world"]{.class_3061}[ belonged to him to give ]{.class_3062}["to whomsoever I will"]{.class_3061}[ (Luke 4:5--6), and Christ didn't contradict him. So the Devil's ]{.class_3062}["goods"]{.class_3061}[ include both lost sinners and the political kingdoms of this world. If you ever wondered why the Devil opposes personal workers and soulwinners so much, think of it this way: would you appreciate it if someone broke into your house and kidnapped your kids? Well, when you win a sinner to Jesus Christ, you are kidnapping the Devil's children (Eph. 2:2); he doesn't like that one bit.]{.class_3062}

[Right now, because the Christian still lives in the Devil's ]{.class_3062}["house,"]{.class_3061}[ Satan still has a certain amount of power over him. If the Lord tarries, you are going to drop dead someday. Why? Because you are still in a sinful body subject to death, and the Devil has been given ]{.class_3062}["the power of death"]{.class_3061}[ by God (Heb. 2:14). Of course, we understand there are restrictions to the Devil's power over the believer. Satan can't take the life of any child of God without the Lord's permission (see Job 1--2), but he still possesses that power nonetheless.]{.class_3062}

[The reason you ultimately overcome death (1 Cor. 15:54--55) is because you get into someone (1 Cor. 12:13) who beat the Devil at his own game. Jesus Christ ]{.class_3062}["humbled himself, and became obedient unto death"]{.class_3061}[ (Phil. 2:8), but He didn't stay dead. When Jesus Christ came out of the grave, the Bible says He ]{.class_3062}["SPOILED principalities and powers"]{.class_3061}[ (Col. 2:15). So in a spiritual sense, there was a "binding" and "spoiling" that took place at Calvary and the Resurrection (2 Tim. 1:10), but the ]{.class_3062}[complete, literal]{.class_3063}[ "spoiling" and "binding" is yet to come (Rom. 16:20).]{.class_3062}

[One day, at the Rapture, the Lord Jesus will take us completely out of the Devil's ]{.class_3062}["house"]{.class_3061}[ and up to His ]{.class_3062}["Father's house"]{.class_3061}[ (John 14:2--3). He does it again, at the end of the Tribulation, for the Tribulation saints (Matt. 24:42--44). After He has robbed Satan of his most valuable ]{.class_3062}["goods"]{.class_3061}[ (]{.class_3062}[saved sinners!]{.class_3063}[), He comes at the Second Advent and binds ]{.class_3062}["the strong man"]{.class_3061}[ (Rev. 20:2) and takes ]{.class_3062}[everything else]{.class_3063}[ (Rev. 11:15).]{.class_3062}

28 Verily I say unto you, All sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:

29 But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost hath never forgiveness, but is in danger of eternal damnation:

30 Because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.

I have set these verses apart from the rest of Christ's discourse in verses 23--27 because verses 28--30 deal with what is known as "the unpardonable sin." Immediately the question that arises (or should arise for anyone who takes the Bible seriously) is, did Christ die for that sin? Look at verse 29: ["hath NEVER forgiveness."]{.class_3054} Then Christ didn't die for it, right? Aren't you ["justified from ALL things"]{.class_3054} by faith in Him (Acts 13:30)? Well, how could He miss the sin here in verse 29?

But look at the rest of the verse: ["is in DANGER of eternal damnation."]{.class_3054} Well, if the person who commits this sin is only ["in danger"]{.class_3054} of going to Hell, is there possibly a chance he can get out of it? Do you see the mess you can make of the Bible if you don't rightly divide it?

Now there have been some great sermons preached by well-meaning men who believe in the blood atonement of Christ for all sins, who believe in salvation by grace through faith without works, and who believe in eternal security. What they say about the passage to move sinners to accept Christ has [nothing whatsoever to do with sound doctrine]{.class_3055} in the matter.

The standard way to preach it by Fundamental Baptists is to say the sin is [rejecting Christ.]{.class_3055} Of course, that has nothing to do with [reality.]{.class_3055} I have known men who got saved after rejecting Christ a hundred times. A lot of Southerners are like that. Some of those "good ole' boys" have been to church every Sunday morning of their childhood and two revival meetings a year for decades, and some of them don't get saved until they're into their seventies. So some preachers will make it [final]{.class_3055} rejection of Christ, and of course, that has nothing to do with the passage at all. Faith in the blood atonement is nowhere in the chapter.

The Charismatics don't know; that is why they live in mortal terror that they might commit it. Someone generalized Christ's words in verses 23--27 and said, "Well, the sin is attributing the works of the Holy Spirit to Satan." That is a smokescreen to prevent a Bible believer who rightly divides the Book from calling attention to their unscriptural nonsense like modern tongues, "signs," and healings. If you point out the fact that some Charismatic healer is a con artist "fleecing the flock," you are committing the unpardonable sin. To that, we say, "NUTS!"---pecan, cashew, Brazil, and macadamia! Jesus Christ commended the church at Ephesus for calling the hand of a bunch of liars claiming to have the apostolic signs when they didn't (Rev. 2:2).

A fine, soulwinning evangelist named J. Harold Smith had a sermon he preached called "God's Three Deadlines." In that sermon, he said the blasphemy against the Holy Ghost was actually cussing out the Holy Ghost. He gave illustrations of men he knew who did that and then died within 48 hours. Now that may be wonderful evangelistic preaching, but the old saying is: "Not all good preaching makes good doctrine, and not all good doctrine makes good preaching."

What Bro. Smith did was just as bad as what the Charismatics do: he interpreted the Bible by his experience and not his experience by the Bible. You mean to tell me that the only way to blaspheme the Holy Spirit is to say, "Blankety-blank you, Holy Ghost"? How about claiming to speak ["ex cathedra,"]{.class_3055} like the Popes? You mean to tell me usurping the authority of the Holy Ghost is not blasphemy? Why, if it were, then every Bible reviser---like Westcott, Hort, Schaff, Driver, Nestle, Aland, Metzger, Thayer, Moffat, Goodspeed, Weigle, Phillips, Berkeley, Taylor, et al.---would be damned: they all played "Holy Ghost" and claimed to be able to tell you what God did and did not say.

Along the line of cussing out the Holy Ghost, there was a phenomenon on the "Internet" a few years ago where teenagers from Christian homes---especially from [preachers'  ]{.class_3055}homes---were encouraged by infidels and atheists to curse the Holy Spirit, and to [film]{.class_3055} themselves doing it so there would be no doubt that they had done it. Those films were then put on the "web" as a "testimony" of []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_91}those teenagers once and for all rejecting Christ and the Bible. They could never be forgiven; all their bridges had been burned. So now they could fornicate, become sex perverts, become sot drunks, blow their minds on drugs, and generally live like hell because there was no turning back. Those poor saps were drop-kicked into Hell, not by cussing out the Holy Ghost, but by the inability of preachers and Bible teachers to read more than two verses on a subject.

Do you want to know what blasphemy of the Holy Ghost is? I mean, if it is an unpardonable sin, don't you think you had better get it settled once and for all [exactly]{.class_3055} what it is? After all, your eternal destiny could depend on it. Well, I'll tell you a really complicated, crafty, highly intellectual way to find out---READ THE NEXT VERSE THAT TELLS YOU WHAT IT IS! I mean, nothing like a Bible to clear up a theological education. What did they do that put them ["in danger of eternal damnation"]{.class_3054} because what they did ["hath never forgiveness"]{.class_3054}?

Verse 30: ["Because they said, He ]{.class_3054}\[talking about Jesus Christ\] [hath an unclean spirit."]{.class_3054}

Have you ever said that? If you did, what would it mean? Nothing! Notice the statement is in the [present]{.class_3055} tense: when Jesus was here [on earth.]{.class_3055} Well, then, it wouldn't mean anything [right now;]{.class_3055} He's not [here]{.class_3055} right now. No man, saved or lost, knows ["Christ after the flesh"]{.class_3054} during the Church Age (see our comments on 2 Cor. 5:16 in that Commentary). Now it will mean something when He is on the throne at Jerusalem during the Millennium, but no one you know right now will be saying it. They will either be saved and like Christ and won't be saying it (1 John 3:2), or they will be dead and in Hell already (2 Thess. 2:11--12, 1:7--8). The Holy Ghost has saved many a Jew who at one time thought Jesus Christ had a devil (John 10:20 cf. Acts 6:7, 26:9; 1 Tim. 1:13).

Listen, people, you have to get things where they belong in that Book. In verse 29, Jesus is talking to Jews under the Law, who are present during His earthly ministry. He hasn't died for [anyone's]{.class_3055} sins yet. If any of those Jews who said He had a devil died before Christ's crucifixion, they would have gone to Hell. Why? [Because there was no blood atonement in the Old Testament for blasphemy against the Holy Ghost.]{.class_3055} There sure is in the New Testament.

["Be it known unto you therefore, men and brethren, that through this man is preached unto you the forgiveness of sins: And by him ALL that believe ]{.class_3054}\[[no exceptions]{.class_3055}\] [are justified from ALL THINGS]{.class_3054} \[[no exceptions]{.class_3055}\][, from which ye could not be justified by the law of Moses"]{.class_3054} (Acts 13:38--39).

["But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship one with another, and the blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanseth us from ALL SIN"]{.class_3054} (1 John 1:7)---NO EXCEPTIONS!

31 There came then his brethren and his mother, and, standing without, sent unto him, calling him.

32 And the multitude sat about him, and they said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee.

33 And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?

34 And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!

35 For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.

This is a rough spot for a Roman Catholic. Jesus Christ doesn't give His mother the time of day. Mary can't make a dime off her Son, here. He doesn't even acknowledge her as His mother. Look at it!

["Who is my mother . . . ?"]{.class_3054} (vs. 33). Well, [not Mary!]{.class_3055} According to whom? According to [Jesus Christ!]{.class_3055}

["And he looked about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother . . ."]{.class_3054} (vs. 34). Then it wasn't Mary, because she was outside (vs. 32).

Now, certainly Mary qualified as His mother according to what follows in verse 35: ["For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my . . . mother."]{.class_3054} But that was nothing exclusive to Mary. Mary Magdalene, Joanna, and Susanna qualified too (Luke 8:2--3). So did the mother of John Mark (Acts 12:12), Tabitha (Acts 9:36), Priscilla (Acts 18:2), Phebe (Rom. 16:1--2), Lois and Eunice (2 []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw475.xhtml_page_93}Tim. 1:5), and any Christian lady you know who is trying to live right (1 Tim. 2; Tit. 2; 1 Pet. 3). If you took all the Christian women who lived, Christ couldn't possibly have any less than a couple of million mothers.

Jesus [never]{.class_3055} acknowledged Mary as His physical mother. He didn't call her "Mother" in John 2:4, used by the Catholics to prove Jesus listens to the intercessory prayers of His mother. He wouldn't even acknowledge her when He was dying on the cross. He said, ["WOMAN]{.class_3054} \[not "Mother"\][, behold thy son!"]{.class_3054} (John 19:26); and He said to John, ["Behold THY mother"]{.class_3054} (John 19:27).

Do you know [why]{.class_3055} He did that? That wasn't Jesus not keeping the fifth commandment in regards to His mother. That was the Lord going out of His way to make sure nearly a billion pagan idolaters had no excuse for treating His earthly mother as some demoniac Queen of Heaven (see Jer. 7:18, 44:17--19, 25), set up as a "Co-Redemptrix" to prevent you from getting to the ["one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus"]{.class_3054} (1 Tim. 2:5).

[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw621.xhtml}

# Chapter 4 {.heading_97}

4:1 And he began again to teach by the sea side: and there was gathered unto him a great multitude, so that he entered into a ship, and sat in the sea; and the whole multitude was by the sea on the land.

2 And he taught them many things by parables, and said unto them in his doctrine,

Once more Jesus is teaching by the Sea of Galilee. As before, back in 3:9 (see our remarks on that verse), the crowd is so great that He teaches from a ship thrust out into the water a little ways from the land while His congregation listens to Him from the seashore.

While Jesus had [spoken]{.class_3071}[ ]{.class_3054}some parables to the scribes and Pharisees back in 3:23, for the first time in His ministry, He [teaches]{.class_3071} the multitudes[ "by parables" ]{.class_3054}(vs. 2), and He gives the reason for doing so in verses 10--12.

To listen to the average teacher or preacher on radio or television, one would gather that the reason Christ taught in parables was to make it easier for the multitudes to understand His doctrine. That is exactly the[ ]{.class_3054}[opposite]{.class_3055}[ ]{.class_3054}of the reason Jesus Himself gave for teaching in parables. The reason[ ]{.class_3054}[He said]{.class_3055}[ ]{.class_3054}He taught in parables was to[ ]{.class_3054}[obscure]{.class_3055} His doctrine and [damn]{.class_3055} a bunch of self-righteous religionists who wouldn't believe Him.

["All these things are done in parables: That seeing they may see, and NOT PERCEIVE; and hearing they may hear, and NOT UNDERSTAND; lest at any time they should be CONVERTED, and their SINS BE FORGIVEN THEM"]{.class_3054} (vss. 11--12).

People, that Book[ "is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart" ]{.class_3054}(Heb. 4:12), and if you approach that Book with the wrong heart, God will [mess you up.]{.class_3055}[ ]{.class_3054}Don't believe me? Look up Ezekiel 14:1--11 and READ IT! God will[ ]{.class_3054}[purposely deceive]{.class_3055}[ ]{.class_3054}a person who approaches Him with an idolatrous heart and [won't receive the truth]{.class_3055}[ ]{.class_3054}when it is presented (2 Thess. 2:10--11).

Note in verse 2 that Jesus teaches ["doctrine"]{.class_3054} by His parables. []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw621.xhtml_page_95}The reason that is significant is because the scholars all agree that you [can't]{.class_3055} teach doctrine from parables. That is why the modern versions change the word ["doctrine"]{.class_3054} in verse 2 to ["teaching."]{.class_3055}

Doctrine is not just "teaching" in general; it is [correct]{.class_3071} teaching on what you are supposed to believe from the Book. I can teach you about how to fix a car or bake a cake, and that won't be "doctrine." Doctrine is not teaching on the rules of grammar or your "times tables" (multiplication). That is why [doctrine]{.class_3071} is the Bible word, and [teaching]{.class_3071} is merely accommodating the ignorance of people who went through the public school system and blew their money down a rathole in College (secular or "Christian").

3 Hearken; Behold, there went out a sower to sow:

4 And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.

5 And some fell on stony ground, where it had not much earth; and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of earth:

6 But when the sun was up, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.

7 And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.

8 And other fell on good ground, and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased; and brought forth, some thirty, and some sixty, and some an hundred.

Now this is the Parable of the Sower; that is what Jesus Himself calls it in Matthew 13:18. Some expositors like to call it the "Parable of the Soils" because the parable is about the four types of soil into which the seed falls. But the reason Jesus calls it the Parable of the Sower is because [He]{.class_3055} is the Sower (Matt. 13:37). The four types of soil represent the four different hearts of men who hear [what Jesus says]{.class_3055} and how they react to it. So the parable really is all about [Him]{.class_3055} and not about [us.]{.class_3055}

9 And he said unto them, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

10 And when he was alone, they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parable.

11 And he said unto them, Unto you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all these things are done in parables:

12 That seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest at any time they should be converted, and their sins should be forgiven them.

This parable was given to the crowd at large, but Jesus did not explain it to them. The ones who get the explanation are those who get ["alone"]{.class_3054} with Jesus and [ask]{.class_3055} Him the meaning of the parable (vs. 10). There are some things in the Scriptures of which you will only gain understanding by spending time alone in your Bible and in prayer. The key will not be digging through commentaries or "the original languages"; it will be letting the Spirit of Christ in you lead you and ["guide you into all truth"]{.class_3054} (John 16:13). I didn't gain my knowledge of the Bible by attending Bob Jones University. I gained it by spending time alone in hotel rooms after meetings searching the Scriptures and praying.

Notice it is more than ["the twelve"]{.class_3054} who are alone with Christ in verse 10. There were twelve disciples Jesus chose to be apostles, but several others followed Him faithfully wherever He went. We know this to be so because when it came time to choose a replacement for Judas, Peter spoke of ["men which have companied with us all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us, Beginning from the baptism of John, unto the same day that he was taken up from us"]{.class_3054} (Acts 1:21--22), two of whom were[ "Justus, and Matthias"]{.class_3054} (Acts 1:23). Women also followed Jesus and ["ministered unto him of their substance,"]{.class_3054} one of whom was Mary Magdalene (Luke 8:1--3), so what Jesus is about to reveal here in private was not restricted just to men. Unlike in Islam, women held a place of respect and service in the ministry of Christ and in the early church.

Verse 11:[ "And he said unto them, Unto you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God."]{.class_3054} The Lord puts a []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw621.xhtml_page_97}division between those to whom He gives revelation and those from whom He withholds it, and it has nothing to do with "God's sovereign will" or any other Calvinistic claptrap. It has to do with Christ's admonition in verse 9:[ "He that hath ears to hear, let him hear."]{.class_3054} The same admonition in verse 23 is accompanied by the additional warning: ["Take heed WHAT ye hear"]{.class_3054} (vs. 24). In the context, it is hearing and believing the word of God (vs. 14), so Paul's words in Romans 10:17 are appropriate to understanding the passage here in Mark 4.

"So then faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God."

In the parallel passage to Mark 3:34--35 in Luke 8:21, Jesus said, ["My mother and my brethren are these which HEAR the word of God, and DO it."]{.class_3054} Furthermore, Jesus promised a special blessing to those ["that HEAR the word of God, and KEEP it"]{.class_3054} (Luke 11:28). So those who heard Jesus in faith received more from Him (Mark 4:24), and those who didn't hear Him in faith got only the bare parable (Mark 4:25).

Notice that what is under discussion is ["the mystery of the kingdom of God."]{.class_3054} So this is [different]{.class_3055} from ["the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven"]{.class_3054} given in Matthew 13:11. Matthew is discussing a literal, physical kingdom promised to the Jews, while Mark 4 is dealing with a spiritual kingdom of righteousness, peace, and joy (see our remarks under 1:14--15). The similarities between the account here and the one in Matthew 13 is what screws up all the scholars. Just because this parable (as well as that of the candlestick in vss. 21--22 and the mustard seed in vss. 31--32) can match either the physical or spiritual kingdom does not make the two the same. The Parable of the Seed and its Growth in verses 26--27 is a parable exclusive to the Kingdom of God.

So the parables here in Mark 4 have definite application to the Church Age. The ["mystery"]{.class_3054} that is being hidden from those Jews who have rejected Jesus Christ is that the physical Kingdom of Heaven would be hidden away with the King they rejected, and that the only way they could get that kingdom was to go through a spiritual kingdom first. That is what is going on [right now.]{.class_3055} A Jew must trust Christ as his [Saviour]{.class_3055} before he will ever see Him as []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw621.xhtml_page_98}[Messiah.]{.class_3055}

Now verse 12 is a quote from Isaiah 6:9--10. That prophecy is of such importance to the nation of Israel that it is given [five times]{.class_3055} in the New Testament: Matthew 13:14--15; here; John 12:38--40; Acts 28:26--27; and Romans 11:8. It is an illustration of the [temporary]{.class_3055} rejection by God of Israel throughout the Church Age due to the nation's rejection of her Messiah and Saviour in the Gospels and Acts. It is a [spiritual]{.class_3055} blindness that requires a [spiritual]{.class_3055} remedy, and that remedy is [faith in Christ.]{.class_3055} That is the whole theme of Romans 11. As a whole, the nation is blind, but that blindness is lifted from any individual Jew who will trust Christ as Saviour.

The final lifting of that blindness occurs at the Second Advent (Rom. 11:25--27). The nation repents (Zech. 12), and the new covenant is applied to it as a whole (Heb. 8:8--10). When that happens, every Jew alive when Jesus returns has the Holy Spirit poured on him and enters the physical kingdom through the spiritual kingdom (Acts 2:16--21). The nation is converted in a day, and [then]{.class_3071} the Millennial kingdom can begin.

13 And he said unto them, Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?

14 The sower soweth the word.

15 And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, Satan cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.

16 And these are they likewise which are sown on stony ground; who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with gladness;

17 And have no root in themselves, and so endure but for a time: afterward, when affliction or persecution ariseth for the word's sake, immediately they are offended.

18 And these are they which are sown among thorns; such as hear the word,

19 And the cares of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in, choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.

20 And these are they which are sown on good ground; such as hear the word, and receive it, and bring forth fruit, some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some an hundred.

["Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?"]{.class_3054} (vs. 13). So the Parable of the Sower is given and interpreted as a pattern for all other parables. That is why the interpretation is given in the context. From the interpretation Christ gave, we learn that [every detail of a parable has an exact meaning.]{.class_3055} That is contrary to the teaching of the Scholars' Union, that says a parable only presents a "central truth." The scholars say that because [they can't handle the interpretation of parables.]{.class_3055} As we will see when we get down to the Parable of the Mustard Seed, that when it comes to expositing Christ's parables, the "consensus of scholars" is usually [speculative guesswork]{.class_3055} based on [wishful thinking.]{.class_3055} That Bible says ["a parable in the mouth of fools"]{.class_3054} is like lame legs (Prov. 26:7) and a drunkard's hand that has been pierced by a thorn (Prov. 26:9). In this case, the "fool" is a [religious, educated]{.class_3055} fool.

Verse 14: ["The sower soweth THE WORD."]{.class_3054} Here begins the interpretation of the parable Christ gave back in verses 3--8. The first and most important detail is not [where]{.class_3055} the Sower is sowing, but [what]{.class_3055} He is sowing. The seed being sown is ["the word of God"]{.class_3054} (Luke 8:11).

Immediately you can see the difference between a Kingdom of Heaven parable, as given in Matthew 13, and a Kingdom of God parable, as you find here in Mark 4, even though both parables are the same parable. When Christ interprets the Parable of the Sower in Matthew 13, He defines the seed as ["the word of THE KINGDOM"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 13:49). You say, "What's the difference?" The difference is the word about a literal, visible, physical kingdom here on earth (Isa. 2:3) and the word that will produce the New Birth in the hearts of those who believe (James 1:18; 1 Pet. 1:23).

So Jesus Christ is the Sower, the seed is the word of God, and the four different soils are the [hearts]{.class_3055} of those who hear the words of Christ. Notice the end of verse 15: ["the word that was sown in their hearts."]{.class_3054} The first hearer is [careless]{.class_3055} or [negligent;]{.class_3055} he is represented by the [hardened ]{.class_3055}soil of the wayside. This is a path []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw621.xhtml_page_100}around the edge of the field where the soil has been packed hard by men walking over it and driving carts over it. The seed just lays on the surface and doesn't sink down into the soil. The warning in Hebrews is: ["To day if ye will HEAR HIS VOICE, harden not your hearts"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 3:15, 4:7).

Now when the word of God falls on the hard heart of the careless hearer, ["Satan cometh IMMEDIATELY"]{.class_3054}---no delay. He is ["the fowls of the air"]{.class_3054} from verse 4, and we have already commented on the Devil being an unclean [spirit]{.class_3055} (singular) composed of unclean [spirits]{.class_3055} (plural) back under our remarks on 1:24. Once again, we are faced with the fact that devils are pictured as birds; specifically, [unclean birds]{.class_3055} (Rev. 18:2). So all those unclean birds listed in Leviticus 11:13--19 are pictures of unclean spirits that end up in the Lake of Fire (Isa. 34:9--15). You are commanded by God Almighty to: ["Seek ye out of the book of the LORD, and READ"]{.class_3054} so you will know these things (Isa. 34:16).

So when the word of God is preached, the Devil is [right there]{.class_3055} to take ["away the word that was sown."]{.class_3054} The Devil wants to keep a sinner from getting the word of God. He will keep you from [hearing]{.class_3055} it; that's why the Bible is forbidden in all Moslem countries, all Communist countries, and in the Federal school system. That's the work of [Satan]{.class_3055} preventing people from being exposed to what God said.

If the Devil can't stop you from hearing it, he will stop you from [receiving]{.class_3055} it. He'll convince you that it is just the word of men and not God (cf. 1 Thess. 2:13); he'll make you think the Bible is just one among many "scriptures" of world religions; he'll make you believe it is just mythology or that prophecy was written after the fact or that Jesus Christ lied when He said Moses wrote Genesis. Anything to get you not to receive the Scriptures as the word of God.
