Corrupt versions, like the [ASV,]{.class_3055} the [NASV,]{.class_3055} the [RSV, ]{.class_3055}the [ESV,]{.class_3055} the [NIV,]{.class_3055} the [New Living Translation,]{.class_3055} and [Good News for Modern Man,]{.class_3055} all join the Jehovah's Witnesses'[ New World Translation]{.class_3055} to remove the word ["Lord"]{.class_3054} from the verse. The ["New" King James Version]{.class_3055} (which is not a [King James Version]{.class_3055} at all) won't do that here, but it will remove the word ["Lord"]{.class_3054} in 66 other places. In other words, the last thing you have in a ["New" King James Version]{.class_3055} is a [King James]{.class_3055} Bible---[any edition!]{.class_3055}

In religious circles, the casting out of devils is known as "exorcism." The practice is only referred to by that term one time in your Bible in Acts 19:13, and it is a reference to a band of lost ["vagabond Jews."]{.class_3054} The reason Jesus and the apostles weren't called "exorcists" was because exorcism involves casting out evil spirits by []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_240}[religious rituals or ceremonies.]{.class_3055}

Those Jewish exorcists thought they could invoke the name of Jesus like a magical incantation. You don't treat the ["name above every name"]{.class_3054} (Phil. 2:9) like that. If you don't know Jesus Christ as your personal Saviour and you try to use His name to cast out demons, those devils are liable to jump on [you]{.class_3055} and tear you up (Acts 19:15--16). The spirit here couldn't attack Jesus, so he attacked the boy: ["And the spirit cried, and rent him sore"]{.class_3054} (vs. 26).

Then you have the Charismatic practice of being "slain in the spirit": ["and he was as one dead; insomuch that many said, He is dead."]{.class_3054} The only problem is that the Holy Spirit wasn't the one doing the "slaying." This is the work of an [unclean spirit.]{.class_3055} In my 67 years in Christ, I have observed every characteristic of this demon-possessed boy practiced in Charismatic meetings and "revivals" with the exception of people throwing themselves in fire and water. The "manifestations of the Spirit" in Charismatic services never leave the one who receives them with assurance of salvation or victory over sin. In the so-called "Brownsville Revival" that took place here in Pensacola in the late 90s, a lot of the so-called "conversions" that took place were professing Christians, some of whom came to the altar as many as [seven times]{.class_3055} to get saved. You can get a critique of the whole Satanic mess in our work [Modern Charismatic Revival Doctrines Examined]{.class_3055} (formerly [The Clownsville Carnival]{.class_3055}).

28 And when he was come into the house, his disciples asked him privately, Why could not we cast him out?

29 And he said unto them, This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting.

The disciples are understandably embarrassed over their inability to cast the devil out of the boy, so they wait until they are alone with Christ to find out what went wrong. After all, Jesus had given ["them power over unclean spirits"]{.class_3054} (6:7).

Christ's answer reveals a couple of things. First, there are "kinds" of devils. All devils are not "created equal." They differ in [wickedness]{.class_3055} (Matt. 12:45; Luke 11:26), and in [power and authority]{.class_3055} (Eph. 6:12; Col. 1:16). Second, with some devils, it takes more than just ["prayer"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_241} (calling on the name of the Lord) to get rid of them. It is going to take [self-deprivation;]{.class_3055} in this case, depriving yourself of [food]{.class_3055} to seek God's power---["fasting."]{.class_3054} So what do the "New Age Bible versions" do that covers up for Satan? They get rid of ["fasting"]{.class_3054} in verse 19. That's the [ASV]{.class_3055} and [NASV]{.class_3055} of the Fundamentalists, the [RSV]{.class_3055} and [NRSV]{.class_3055} of the Liberals, the [NIV]{.class_3055} and [ESV]{.class_3055} of the Evangelicals, and the [New Living Translation]{.class_3055} and [Good News For Modern Man]{.class_3055} of the New Evangelicals; not to mention the [New American Bible]{.class_3055} and [New Jerusalem Bible]{.class_3055} of the Catholics. The Devil made sure he removed a necessary tool God gave you to deliver someone from his clutches. Prayer alone won't do it; it takes ["prayer AND fasting."]{.class_3054}

Now, fasting is not just an Old Testament practice. The leaders in the church at Antioch (where the disciples were first called ["Christians"]{.class_3054}---Acts 11:26) were fasting when the Holy Ghost called the first missionaries (Acts 13:2--3). Paul practiced fasting (2 Cor. 6:5, 11:27) and commanded it for husbands and wives having marital problems (1 Cor. 7:5). There are times when a Christian should fast, as well as pray, to seek God's face on an important matter.

30 And they departed thence, and passed through Galilee; and he would not that any man should know it.

31 For he taught his disciples, and said unto them, The Son of man is delivered into the hands of men, and they shall kill him; and after that he is killed, he shall rise the third day.

32 But they understood not that saying, and were afraid to ask him.

Jesus and His disciples leave the place where He had delivered the demon-possessed boy, and they pass through Galilee on their way to Capernaum (vs. 33). Jesus wants to remain "incognito" on this trip so He can teach the disciples more about His upcoming death and resurrection at Jerusalem. Just as back in 8:31--32 and 9:9--10 (see our comments on both passages), they haven't a clue about what He is teaching, and they are afraid to ask Him lest they receive the same kind of response they got back in 7:17--18 and []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_242}8:16--18, 33 (which see).

33 And he came to Capernaum: and being in the house he asked them, What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way?

34 But they held their peace: for by the way they had disputed among themselves, who should be the greatest.

35 And he sat down, and called the twelve, and saith unto them, If any man desire to be first, the same shall be last of all, and servant of all.

36 And he took a child, and set him in the midst of them: and when he had taken him in his arms, he said unto them,

37 Whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name, receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.

Now begins a debate among the disciples that lasts all the way to the Last Supper (Luke 22:24). Here in Mark, it shows up twice: here and in 10:35--37. Both times it follows on the heels of Jesus telling them of His death and resurrection (9:31, 10:34). As Jesus was discussing [His humiliation]{.class_3055} (Phil. 2:8), His followers were debating [their exaltation.]{.class_3055} No wonder Jesus called them (and [us]{.class_3055}---Rom. 3:19) ["evil"]{.class_3054} (Luke 11:13).

Of course, Jesus knew about what they had been arguing, even though they wouldn't tell Him, because He perceived ["the thought of their heart"]{.class_3054} (Luke 9:47). Christ's response to their question was the way up is [down:]{.class_3055} ["If any man desire to be first, the same shall be last of all, and servant of all"]{.class_3054} (vs. 35). Jesus Himself was the great example of that (Luke 22:27).

To illustrate this, Jesus ["took a child, and set him in the midst of them"]{.class_3054} (vs. 36). The [spiritual]{.class_3055} lesson for us this side of Calvary is that for a sinner to be converted, he must come to Christ in child-like faith (Matt. 18:3). That, of course, has nothing to do with the [doctrinal]{.class_3055} application of verse 37.

["Whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name, RECEIVETH ME"]{.class_3054} (vs. 37). Is that what John meant in []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_243}John 1:12? Is that what Paul meant in Colossians 2:6? Are you saved by receiving little children in Christ's name? Don't bet your eternal soul on it, [at least not this side of the Rapture.]{.class_3055}

Look at what follows in verse 41: ["For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink IN MY NAME, because YE BELONG TO CHRIST, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward."]{.class_3054} Over in John 13:20, Jesus told the disciples, ["He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me."]{.class_3054} Back when Jesus first sent them out to preach, He told them, ["He that receiveth you receiveth me"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 10:40), and He followed it up with the statement about the ["cup of water"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 10:42).

The illustration is that the disciples were to be like that little child in their faith and humility, and if they were, any act of kindness done toward them would be counted as an act of kindness toward Christ, which would result in the giver's salvation. Well, that's not the Pauline revelation about salvation in the Church Age. The time when a person is saved by ["Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these my brethren, ye have done it unto me"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 25:40) is during the Tribulation (cf. Matt. 25:31).

In the Tribulation, that persecuted Jew who won't take the mark or bow to the image of the Antichrist stands in the place of Christ, and at the Judgment of the Nations, when Christ returns in power, the survivors of the Gentile nations are judged by how they treated that Jew. If they treated him right and stood by him during his trials and persecutions, they are saved sheep, and if they didn't, they are lost goats. You won't find one mention of salvation by grace alone through faith alone in the finished work of Christ anywhere in Matthew 25:31--46; it's all [works.]{.class_3055} Why? Because [at that time,]{.class_3055} receiving that Jew in kindness and pity [is receiving Christ.]{.class_3055}

You say, "Where does that child of Mark 9:36--37 come in?" Haven't you ever read back there in the Old Testament about ["the CHILDREN of Israel"]{.class_3054}? The new "Bibles" change that word ["children"]{.class_3054} to ["people"]{.class_3055} or ["sons"]{.class_3055} so you will lose the cross-reference. They are just as crooked as a dog's hind leg.

For further study, see our comments on Matthew 25:31--46 in that Commentary.

38 And John answered him, saying, Master, we saw one casting out devils in thy name, and he followeth not us: and we forbad him, because he followeth not us.

39 But Jesus said, Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me.

40 For he that is not against us is on our part.

41 For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.

Here is one of ["The sons of thunder"]{.class_3054} (3:17) being jealous of someone ["casting out devils"]{.class_3054} in the name of Christ. I just showed you, back under my remarks on verse 25, some Jews over in Acts 19:13--16 who did that, and that devil tore them up because they didn't know Christ. This fellow here is different. He knows about Christ and is not against Him: ["For he that is not against us is on our part"]{.class_3054} (vs. 40). The implication is that, although this fellow is not in the company of the disciples, he is still following Christ on his own, so Jesus says, ["Forbid him not"]{.class_3054} (vs. 39).

That was Moses' attitude in Numbers 11:26--29: ["would God that all the LORD'S people were prophets, and that the LORD would put his spirit upon them!"]{.class_3054} That was Paul's attitude in Philippians 1:18---["notwithstanding, every way, whether in pretence, or in truth, Christ is preached; and I therein do rejoice."]{.class_3054}

That is my attitude also. I don't care if Bob Jones University or [The Sword of the Lord]{.class_3055} hates my guts. I am glad for every soul John R. Rice, Curtis Hutson, and Shelton Smith have led to the Lord. What I have against them is where they mess with that Book and destroy Christians' faith in that Book. Outside of that, I have nothing against them, no matter what they think of me.

["For there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me"]{.class_3054} (vs. 39). Now that is not a [carte blanche]{.class_3055} statement any more than is the promise that whatever you ask in the name of Jesus you'll get (see John 14:13, 15:7, 16, 16:23). There are [conditions]{.class_3055} to that kind of thing. In the []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_245}case here, you have to know the Jesus Christ of the Bible (cf. 2 Cor. 11:4), and He has to know you (Matt. 7:22--23). And one should never forget that the Devil can come with ["power and signs and LYING WONDERS"]{.class_3054} (2 Thess. 2:9). So just because someone comes along talking about "Jesus" and "loving Jesus," and can perform a miracle, doesn't mean you should follow him across the street. You are commanded to ["try the spirits whether they are of God"]{.class_3054} (1 John 4:1) to make sure you are not following a false prophet (Matt. 24:11) preaching a false Christ (Matt. 24:5, 24).

Now I have already covered the [doctrinal]{.class_3055} application of verse 41 under my comments on verse 37. But I do not want to skip over the practical application of the verse to the Christian. Notice the ["reward"]{.class_3054} in verse 41. The application is to the disciples representing Jesus Christ. That application is much stronger in the Body of Christ because the Lord actually dwells in you (Col. 1:27). The New Testament believer is commanded by the Apostle Paul to distribute ["to the necessity of saints"]{.class_3054} and to be ["given to hospitality"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 12:13). Peter adds that he is to do it with ["charity"]{.class_3054} and ["without grudging"]{.class_3054} (1 Pet. 4:9). If you do, you have a reward coming to you (2 Cor. 9:6--8).

Brethren, when you give to someone putting out the Gospel, like a preacher, missionary, or evangelist, you have a part in his ministry and, therefore, a part in his reward. Back in the Old Testament, it was a law that the ones who stayed behind and guarded the camp divided the spoils with the ones who went to battle (Num. 31:27; 1 Sam. 30:24). When you give to the ministry of the Gospel, the Lord considers that ["fruit . . . to your account"]{.class_3054} (Phil. 4:17) and ["a sacrifice acceptable, well pleasing to God"]{.class_3054} (Phil. 4:18).

42 And whosoever shall offend one of these little ones that believe in me, it is better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he were cast into the sea.

43 And if thy hand offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter into life maimed, than having two hands to go into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:

44 Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.

45 And if thy foot offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter halt into life, than having two feet to be cast into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:

46 Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.

47 And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out: it is better for thee to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye, than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire:

48 Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.

The ["little ones that believe in me"]{.class_3054} (vs. 42) certainly has application to actual children (see vs. 36). Jesus said it would be better for you to tie a millstone around your neck and go drown yourself than to destroy a child's faith in Jesus Christ and the Bible. Those millstones could weigh anywhere from 100--1,500 pounds!

That being the case, where does that leave the federal (public) schools and the National Education Association? Both push a religion: [secular humanism.]{.class_3055} That's a polite term for [atheism.]{.class_3055} In 1961, in the case of Torcaso vs. Watkins, one of the justices on the Supreme Court said that "among religions . . . which do not teach . . . a belief in the existence of God are . . . Secular Humanism . . . ." The lower courts then proceeded to make atheism a protected religion under the law (decisions in 1965, 1970, 1977--79, and 2005).

In 1962, the Supreme Court threw prayer out of the public schools (Engel vs. Vitale). In 1963, it threw Bible reading out of the public schools (Abington School District vs. Schempp). In 1980, it threw the Ten Commandments out lest the students "read, meditate upon, and perhaps venerate and [obey]{.class_3055} the Ten Commandments, which is [not a permissible . . . objective"]{.class_3055} (Stone vs. Graham). In 1987, it ruled [only one]{.class_3055} theory of origins can be taught in the government schools, and the only theory that is to be taught is [evolution.]{.class_3055} Creationism is forbidden because it "lacks a [secular]{.class_3055} purpose" (Edwards vs. Aquillard).

The entire public school system is designed by [law]{.class_3055} (see the decisions above) to offend Christian children and destroy their faith in the Book. The children are taught that [man]{.class_3055} makes the rules and [man]{.class_3055} creates liberty and [man]{.class_3055}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_247} is the measure of all things. God is not just overlooked or ignored; He is [actively opposed.]{.class_3055} The government will fund educational programs that overtly state "the Bible is wrong" (originally on creation but now on [sex perversion]{.class_3055}), but it is "unconstitutional" to fund any program that says the Bible is [right.]{.class_3055}

Children have been forbidden to read their own personal Bibles during "free time." They have had their Bibles confiscated. They have had their Bibles thrown in the trash. They have been forbidden from writing essays on Jesus Christ as their example or "hero." They have been forbidden from praying over their lunch to thank God for their food. They have been thrown off buses for witnessing to their classmates. You talk about child abuse, man; [that's it!]{.class_3055} That's contrary to everything the Founding Fathers, [saved or lost,]{.class_3055} intended for this country. The principals, teachers, bus drivers, judges, politicians, and bureaucrats responsible for that mess should take a long walk off a short pier in "concrete galoshes." You say, "According to whom?" According to [Jesus Christ!]{.class_3055}

But there is another application to verse 42 that should not be overlooked. In the New Testament, new believers in Christ are called ["little children,"]{.class_3054} no matter what age they are physically (John 13:33; Gal. 4:19; 1 John 2:1, 12--13, 18, 3:7, 18, 4:4, 5:21). The faith of those young believers in Christ can be destroyed, and the ones responsible for doing this are [Christian educators:]{.class_3055} the professors and faculty members at Christian Colleges, Universities, and Seminaries. Young men enter believing the Book, and they graduate correcting it and questioning what God said. The Conservatives and Fundamentalists may not correct or question as much as the New Evangelicals and the Liberals, but they will still correct and question the Book nevertheless.

Don't tell me; I'll tell you. Within a year of being saved, I went off to Greenville, South Carolina to attend the largest Fundamental institution of higher learning at the time: Bob Jones University. I hadn't been in classes a week before the teachers were correcting my Bible with Irenaeus, Eusebius, "a better translation should be . . . ," the [RSV]{.class_3055} (the New Testament was being sold in the campus bookstore in 1950), the oldest and best manuscripts, authoritative scholars, the [ASV,]{.class_3055} and "historic positions." I "butted heads" with Dr. Charles []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_248}Brokenshire, who could speak, read, and write eight different languages. When I called his hand on "the Parable of [Dives]{.class_3055} and Lazarus" (that's Christ's account of the rich man and Lazarus in Luke 16 for those of you who don't know Latin or Roman Catholicism), he threw a temper tantrum, stomped his feet, and screamed, "Have you ever studied Greek or Hebrew?" When I replied, "No, sir," he shot back, "Then I suggest that you keep quiet until you know what you are talking about!" That was one of the most brilliant professors Bob Jones University ever had, and he couldn't even answer a first-year Bible student with Scripture when I asked him why he kept calling the account a parable when Christ never said it was. He "buffaloed" every other ministerial student in that class with his education and caused them all to doubt and contradict the plain words of Scripture.

I would say, conservatively speaking, that since 1950, between all the Christian schools, America has been flooded with over 7,000 ministerial graduates in just that condition. In turn, those ministerial graduates have taught the congregations of the churches to doubt the Book until a good 95% of them don't believe any "Bible" is the words of God without error. According to your Saviour, it would have been better for the professors and teachers responsible for that infidelity to go swimming with a half-ton cement block tied around their necks. [That's]{.class_3055} the Jesus Christ of that Book. Stewart Custer, Rick Warren, and Joel Osteen wouldn't even recognize Him if He showed up in person at their door.

Now, verses 43--48 deal with the subject of [Hell.]{.class_3055} Naturally you can expect the new "Bibles" to mess with the passage. Nearly every corrupt modern translation (e.g., the [ASV,]{.class_3055} the [RSV,]{.class_3055} the [NASV,]{.class_3055} the [NIV,]{.class_3055} the [ESV,]{.class_3055} and the [NLT]{.class_3055}) gets rid of verses 44 and 46, while retaining the same statement in verse 48. The [NASV]{.class_3055} will put the verses in, though putting them in brackets to indicate they really shouldn't be there (because they aren't "in the early manuscripts"; i.e., [Vaticanus]{.class_3055} and [Sinaiticus]{.class_3055}); but the [NIV, RSV,]{.class_3055} and its "Conservative" counterpart, the [ESV,]{.class_3055} will remove the verses altogether while [retaining the KJV verse numbering.]{.class_3055} So if you are a scholar on the [NIV, NLT, RSV,]{.class_3055} or [ESV]{.class_3055} committees, you count 43, 45, 47, 48. You never met a bunch of bigger hypocrites unless they were from Rome or Mecca. Compared to the revisers and translators of []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_249}the modern versions, the Mafia are fine folks.

The word ["hell"]{.class_3054} occurs 23 times in the New Testament. They are: Matthew 5:22, 29--30, 10:28, 11:23, 16:18, 18:9, 23:15, 33; Mark 9:43, 45, 47; Luke 10:15, 12:5, 16:23; Acts 2:27, 31; James 3:6; 2 Peter 2:4; Revelation 1:18, 6:8, 20:13--14. The new versions leave the word [untranslated]{.class_3055} in Matthew 11, 16; Luke 10, 16; Acts 2; and Revelation 1, 6, 20. The reason is that the scholars try to make a distinction between [hades]{.class_3055} and [gehenna]{.class_3055} (the two Greek words for [Hell]{.class_3055}) that is meaningless. Both are places of [fire and torment]{.class_3055} (Luke 16:23--24 cf. Rev. 14:10--11), and Hell ([hades]{.class_3055}) is cast into the Lake of Fire ([gehenna]{.class_3055}) in Revelation 20:14.

Friend, if you die and go to a place where you burn forever, you are not going to be quibbling over terms like [hades]{.class_3055} and [gehenna]{.class_3055}; you will be in [Hell.]{.class_3055} It won't matter whether it is "temporary" Hell ([hades]{.class_3055}) or "permanent" Hell ([gehenna]{.class_3055}); it will still be [Hell.]{.class_3055} That's H-E-L-L, and I don't mean ["Hades."]{.class_3055} Without Christ, you are going to [burn,]{.class_3055} and you are going to burn in [Hell,]{.class_3055} and I don't mean "[Gehenna]{.class_3055}." If you want a Bible in modern language, don't get one that says ["Hades."]{.class_3055} No one in the bars, the construction sites, the ball games, the dance halls, the cat houses, the military, or Washington, D.C. says, "Go to [hades]{.class_3055}." It's always: "Go to [hell."]{.class_3055} The reason the new versions won't translate the word is that the [King James]{.class_3055} Bible is a little too plain for thin-skinned, politically correct readers who are easily offended because many of them are headed to [Hell.]{.class_3055}

Now, on the heels of Christ's statement on offending ["one of these little ones that believe in me"]{.class_3054} are three statements in a row on what to do if some member of one's body offends [him:]{.class_3055} ["if thy hand offend thee, cut it off"]{.class_3054} (vs. 43), ["if thy foot offend thee, cut it off"]{.class_3054} (vs. 45), ["if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out"]{.class_3054} (vs. 47). Why? Because ["it is better for thee to enter into life ]{.class_3054}\[vs. 47 says ["the kingdom of God"]{.class_3054}\][, than . . . to be cast into hell]{.class_3054} \[["hell fire"]{.class_3054} in vs. 47\]["]{.class_3054} (vss. 43, 45, 47).

Before expositing the passage doctrinally and devotionally, let's pause a second and ask ourselves: "Is this the [Pauline]{.class_3055} doctrine for staying out of Hell?" Will cutting off one of your members keep you out of Hell? The head of the first Christian school in Alexandria, Egypt thought it could. Origen (A.D. 184--253), who constructed the text for the modern versions, [castrated himself]{.class_3055} because he had a []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_250}[sex problem:]{.class_3055} he cut off the "offending member."

But in the New Testament, this side of Calvary, what keeps you out of Hell is [believing the Gospel of Jesus Christ]{.class_3055} (Rom. 1:16; 1 Cor. 15:1--2; Eph. 1:13, 3:6). You can cut off body parts until you bleed out, and wake up in Hell afterwards if you don't receive Christ. So from a [practical, devotional, spiritual]{.class_3055} point of view, we can say that Jesus is using a figure of speech known as "hyperbole" here: exaggeration to get across a point. The point here is it would be better for you to get rid of any part of your body that keeps you from trusting Christ as your Saviour than to go to Hell with two hands, two feet, and two eyes. [Doctrinally,]{.class_3055} though, you are still dealing with [salvation in the Tribulation,]{.class_3055} just as you were back in verses 37 and 41. An element of [works]{.class_3055} is involved that affects salvation (see Rev. 12:17, 14:12).

Now look at Christ's emphasis on Hell; it is one of His favorite subjects on which to preach. In fact, the expression ["hell fire"]{.class_3054}---as in Hell-fire-and-damnation preaching which the Liberals and Emergents despise so much---is peculiar to [Jesus Christ]{.class_3055} (Matt. 5:22, 18:9; Mark 9:47). The reason you know the new versions are wrong for getting rid of verses 44 and 46 is that it does away with the Holy Spirit's three-fold witness to the truth (Deut. 19:15; Matt. 18:16; 2 Cor. 13:1). The verses follow naturally Christ's statements in verses 43 and 45. Such repetition is a common Hebrew literary tool (see our comments on Gen. 41:32 in that Commentary).

Verses 44, 46, and 48 are a quote from Isaiah 66:24. It is a reference to a lake of fire in the land of Edom (see Isa. 34:5--10) during the Millennium. The Greek word [gehenna]{.class_3055} is used here for Hell because Christ is using the [Valley of Hinnom]{.class_3055} on the south side of Jerusalem (Josh. 15:8) as a "visual aid" of Hell. The Valley of Hinnom was where the priests of Molech burned babies as sacrifices to their god (2 Kings 23:10). It was called ["Tophet"]{.class_3054} in the Old Testament (Isa. 30:33; Jer. 19:6), meaning "a place of drums." It was called that because in order to drown out the screams of the babies being cast into the fire, great drums were beaten.

It came to be known as "the place of burnings" because when King Josiah got rid of the idol worship in Judah, he turned Tophet []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_251}into the city dump where the garbage and refuse were continually burned (2 Kings 23:10). When Christ wanted to show those Jews what Hell was like, He pointed over to [Gehenna]{.class_3055} and said, "You see that? That's what Hell is like: a place of garbage and filth, maggots, and perpetual burning." Hell is essentially the garbage dump of the universe (Rev. 21:8).

There's a "cutesy" saying going around on these motivational, "feel-good" posters set up in offices. It says, "I know I'm special 'cause God didn't make no junk." Well, God didn't intend for you to be a sinner, but you were born in Adam (Rom. 5:12), ["sold under sin"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 7:14). Unless you experience the New Birth (John 3:3--7) and become ["a new creature" "in Christ"]{.class_3054} (2 Cor. 5:17), you are headed to God's junkyard, and [you ain't getting out.]{.class_3055} It won't matter if you are God's ["offspring"]{.class_3054} (Acts 17:28--29).

Now look at that ["worm"]{.class_3054} in verses 44, 46, and 48. Doesn't it say ["their worm"]{.class_3054}? It does in Isaiah 66:24 too. Then it's not some generic "worm." There is an evangelistic movie that was popular years ago called "The Burning Hell." That movie showed people in Hell with maggots crawling all over them, eating them alive. While that is an interesting visualization of one of the torments of Hell, that is not what the verses you read said. They said, ["their worm." "Their"]{.class_3054}---it's [personal]{.class_3055};[ "worm"]{.class_3054}---it's [singular.]{.class_3055} It's not talking about maggots crawling on and eating a person in the Lake of Fire; it's a maggot (a ["worm"]{.class_3054}) peculiar to each person.

Because of that, some of the expositors try to "wax eloquent" and make it the [memories]{.class_3055} of the sinner eating away at him; i.e., the sinner in Hell is remembering all the chances he had to receive Christ, and those memories are tormenting him. And of course, that is so much [poppycock. ]{.class_3055}As is our custom, we will let the Scriptures define the Scriptures (1 Cor. 2:13) and not indulge in flights of fanciful ["private interpretation"]{.class_3054} (2 Pet. 1:20).

Now what is that ["worm"]{.class_3054} in the Scriptures? Turn first to the oldest book in the Bible, written before Moses wrote anything---Job 17:14.

"I have said to corruption, Thou art my father: to the worm, Thou art my mother, and my sister."

Job says he was in a family of [worms.]{.class_3055} That ["corruption,"]{.class_3054} of course, is the corruption of [death]{.class_3055}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_252} (1 Cor. 15:52--53). Let's try Job again---Job 25:20.

"The womb shall forget him; the worm shall feed sweetly upon him; he shall be no more remembered; and wickedness shall be broken as a tree."

That's where "The Burning Hell" got its maggot scene. Job 21:26 says,

"They shall lie down alike in the dust, and the worms shall cover them."

So when you die, the worms get your body. But Mark 9:43--48 have nothing to do with the Jehovah's Witness "grave." You can amputate any body part you want, and you'll still be put to bed with a shovel after you die. The ["worm"]{.class_3054} of Mark 9 is much more than merely the worms that get your body in the grave. Look at Job 25:6.

"How much less man, THAT IS A WORM? and the son of man, WHICH IS A WORM?"

As we discussed in our remarks under Mark 8:36 on the sinner losing his soul in the Lake of Fire (which see), what you have is Darwin in reverse. In the Lake of Fire, the sinner degenerates from a man to a being like his father (John 8:44)---a [red maggot]{.class_3055} (cf. Rev. 12:3, 9). The Lake of Fire is called ["the second death"]{.class_3054} (Rev. 20:14). In the first death, you lose your bodily shape by being eaten by worms. In the second death, you lose the bodily shape of your soul and become a [worm.]{.class_3055}

The no-Hellers, like the JWs and the Seventh-day Adventists, demand a verse showing the sinner burns in Hell forever. The proof text is right here in Mark 9:44, 46, 48. ["Their worm dieth not"]{.class_3054}---that's the sinner in Hell. ["And the fire is not quenched"]{.class_3054}---that worm doesn't burn up; it isn't "annihilated." It just keeps burning.

Now just in case there is some poor, lost sinner reading this, let me ask you something: Is that how you want to end up? You say, "I don't believe it, Ruckman." It doesn't matter whether you believe it or not; it's still going to happen. You can deny the law of gravity all you want, but if you jump off the Empire State Building without something to hold you up, you'll still be scraped off the sidewalk where you go splat. The only way you can avoid that "destiny" is to accept the One who became sin for you so you could become God's []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_253}righteousness in Him (2 Cor. 5:21).

49 For every one shall be salted with fire, and every sacrifice shall be salted with salt.

50 Salt is good: but if the salt have lost his saltness, wherewith will ye season it? Have salt in yourselves, and have peace one with another.

Now verses 49--50 have never been successfully applied to the Body of Christ in this age in any way. Even a spiritual application has to be stretched beyond reason. The mystery to the passage is the ["salt."]{.class_3054}

When God rained fire and brimstone down on Sodom and Gomorrah (Gen. 19:24), Lot's wife ["became a pillar of salt"]{.class_3054} (Gen. 19:26), and that inland lake there turned into ["the salt sea"]{.class_3054} (Gen. 14:3). That's the Dead Sea. It has so much salt in it that no fish can live in its waters; it has so much salt in it that birds flying over it in hot weather will fall out of the sky due to the fumes rising up from it. The salt concentration is so high that anybody---fat, thin, heavy, light---can float in it; if you couldn't swim a lick, you could float in the Dead Sea. It is saltier than the Pacific and Atlantic Oceans combined.

Question: from where did all that salt come? There's no way enough salt could be leeched from Mt. Hermon, where the Jordan River originates, to make the Dead Sea that salty. I'll tell you from where it came---fire!

["For every one shall be salted with FIRE"]{.class_3054} (vs. 49). Is the ["every one"]{.class_3054} there everyone in every age? How about you as a Christian? Will you be ["salted with fire"]{.class_3054}? There's ["fire"]{.class_3054} at the Judgment Seat of Christ, but it doesn't burn [you;]{.class_3055} it burns your [works]{.class_3055} (1 Cor. 3:13). So obviously the ones ["salted with fire"]{.class_3054} are the ones who end up in Hell in verses 43--48. If you have trusted Christ, you won't have to be ["salted with fire"]{.class_3054} because your Saviour got ["salted"]{.class_3054} in your place: He suffered the torments of Hell for you on the cross (see Psa. 57:4, 88:6--7).

["Every sacrifice shall be salted with salt."]{.class_3054} That's clear: those Old Testament sacrifices were to be offered with salt (Lev. 2:13). (The same is true of offerings in the Millennium---Ezek. 43:24, []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_254}but that is a topic for another time. In the meantime, you can check out our comments on Col. 2:16--17 in the [Bible Believers' Commentary on Galatians--Colossians.]{.class_3055}) That salt in the offering was a symbol of the perpetual covenant between God and the nation of Israel (Num. 18:19; 2 Chron. 13:5).

During His sacrifice for our sins on the cross, Jesus said, ["I thirst"]{.class_3054} (John 19:28). One of the qualities of salt is to create [thirst.]{.class_3055} It should also be noted that the nation with whom God made ["a covenant of salt"]{.class_3054} (Num. 18:19), during the Tribulation, will be [sacrifices]{.class_3055} on an altar and [eaten]{.class_3055} (Psa. 16:4; Isa. 6:13; Mic. 3:3; Zech. 13:8; Rev. 6:9--11).

Verse 50: ["Salt is good."]{.class_3054} There's nothing wrong with the ["salt"]{.class_3054} in verse 49; that ["fire,"]{.class_3054} though, is a different story (see our comments on 1:8). Salt adds flavor; salt disinfects; salt preserves; salt stings when you need it; salt creates a thirst when you need it. ["Salt is good."]{.class_3054}

["But if the salt have lost his saltness, wherewith will ye season it?"]{.class_3054} You use salt to season; but if the salt has lost its ["saltness,"]{.class_3054} its ["savour"]{.class_3054} (Luke 14:34), its flavor, what are you going to use to season the salt? The answer is [nothing.]{.class_3055} Jesus said over in Matthew 5:13, ["it is thenceforth good for nothing, but to be cast out, and to be trodden under foot of men."]{.class_3054} In that passage, ["the salt"]{.class_3054} is [Jesus' disciples:]{.class_3055}[ "YE are the salt of the earth."]{.class_3054} [You]{.class_3055} are to be ["salt"]{.class_3054} to this lost world.

["Have salt in yourself."]{.class_3054} I don't completely understand that. I know Paul said, ["Let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt"]{.class_3054} (Col. 4:6). That has to do with speaking the truth plainly and not with ["good words and fair speeches"]{.class_3054} in order to ["deceive the hearts of the simple"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 16:18). Like salt in a wound, when the word of God is spoken plainly and directly, though it may [sting, ]{.class_3055}it will clean you out (John 15:3). I can also say that if Jesus Christ was ["salted with fire"]{.class_3054} for you and His sacrifice on Calvary was ["salted with salt,"]{.class_3054} then as a Christian with ["Christ in you, the hope of glory"]{.class_3054} (Col. 1:27), you ["Have salt in yourselves."]{.class_3054} Beyond that, I will have to say: ["Ich weiss nicht."]{.class_3055} That's German for "I don't know."

["And have peace one with another."]{.class_3054} There's no problem with that. Paul commanded you: []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1521.xhtml_page_255}["If it be possible, as much as lieth in you, live peaceably with all men"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 12:18). Since ["the kingdom of God is . . . peace"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 14:17), you are told to ["follow after the things which make for peace, and things wherewith one may edify another"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 14:19). If you serve the Lord Jesus Christ that way, you are ["acceptable to God, and approved of men"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 14:18). ["Peace"]{.class_3054} is part of ["the fruit of the Spirit"]{.class_3054} (Gal. 5:22), and you are to follow after it (2 Tim. 2:22).

There is no problem applying that last part to the child of God in the Body of Christ, but since verse 37 here in Mark 9, hardly anything could be applied [doctrinally]{.class_3055} to this age. It is either to Jewish disciples [before the Crucifixion]{.class_3055} or Jewish disciples [after the Rapture of the Church.]{.class_3055} It is no different here. While the New Testament believer can certainly apply Christ's words at the end of verse 50 [spiritually]{.class_3055} and[ practically,]{.class_3055} the [doctrine]{.class_3055} of the passage is [not]{.class_3055} aimed at him.

In the book of Hebrews---written to [Hebrews,]{.class_3071} not "Christian Jews"---the writer says this:

["FOLLOW PEACE with all men, and HOLINESS, without which NO MAN SHALL SEE THE LORD"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 12:14).

Since [all]{.class_3055} the members of the Body of Christ will ["meet the Lord in the air"]{.class_3054} at the Rapture (1 Thess. 4:17), and since ["EVERY EYE shall see him"]{.class_3054} at His Advent (Rev. 1:7), Hebrews 12:14 is talking about those Jews who not only ["have the testimony of Jesus Christ"]{.class_3054} but also ["keep the commandments of God"]{.class_3054} in order to be saved in the Tribulation (Rev. 12:17, 14:12). For further study, I recommend you get the [Bible Believer's Commentary on Hebrews]{.class_3055} and study our comments on Hebrews 3:6, 14, 4:1 6:4--6, 11, 10:26--29, 38--39, 12:14--15.

[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml}

# Chapter 10 {.heading_97}

10:1 And he arose from thence, and cometh into the coasts of Judaea by the farther side of Jordan: and the people resort unto him again; and, as he was wont, he taught them again.

2 And the Pharisees came to him, and asked him, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife? tempting him.

3 And he answered and said unto them, What did Moses command you?

4 And they said, Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement, and to put her away.

5 And Jesus answered and said unto them, For the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept.

6 But from the beginning of the creation God made them male and female.

7 For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and cleave to his wife;

8 And they twain shall be one flesh: so then they are no more twain, but one flesh.

9 What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.

10 And in the house his disciples asked him again of the same matter.

11 And he saith unto them, Whosoever shall put away his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her.

12 And if a woman shall put away her husband, and be married to another, she committeth adultery.

Jesus leaves Capernaum in Galilee (9:33 cf. Matt. 19:1) and travels down to the east side of Jordan, bordering on Judea, where He was baptized (vs. 1 cf. Matt. 19:1; John 1:28). That is where Joshua crossed the Jordan (Josh. 2:7 cf. Judg. 7:24). That's where David crossed Jordan when he returned to Jerusalem after the defeat of Absalom (2 Sam. 19:15, 40). And it is where Elijah and []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_257}Elisha crossed Jordan on dry ground just as Joshua did (2 Kings 2:1--14). Do you know what those events picture? They are types of the Second Advent.

As we have discussed in our work [The Path of the Second Advent]{.class_3055} (as well as in our comments on Hab. 3 and Judg. 5 in those Commentaries), Christ will follow the path the Ark of the Covenant took as it came up from Mt. Sinai. He will land on Mt. Sinai, come up through Edom by the King's Highway, and cross the Jordan where He was baptized, and head to the Mount of Olives (Zech. 14:4; Acts 1:11--12) and Jerusalem. He doesn't come to the Mount of Olives first as the Fundamentalists say.

Now the passage here in Mark is a favorite of the Catholics, Holiness, and Fundamentalists who don't believe divorce and remarriage is allowed under any circumstance. That is because Mark doesn't record the full account. First of all, note the question in verse 2: ["Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife?"]{.class_3054} If all you had was Mark 10:2, you would figure the Pharisees were asking if divorce was even allowable under the Mosaic Law.

But Mark 10:2 is not the full question. Matthew records the complete question: ["Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife FOR EVERY CAUSE?"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 19:3). The question is: "Can a man divorce his wife for any reason?" One of the principles of Biblical hermeneutics is always interpret an incomplete statement by a complete statement. Mark's account in both verses 2 and 11 is incomplete and should be interpreted by the fuller account in Matthew 19:1--12.

The "law" in question here (vss. 3--4) is found in Deuteronomy 24:1--4. That passage says if the husband finds ["SOME uncleanness in her"]{.class_3054} (Deut. 24:1). In other words, the divorce can be allowed for more than one reason. There is no specific sin mentioned in Deuteronomy 24:1; in fact, there is no "sin" mentioned at all. The wife didn't have to do anything morally wrong to get ["put away."]{.class_3054}

Notice, as well, that a remarriage is allowed in the context. Deuteronomy 24:2---["And when she is departed out of his house, she may go and be another man's wife."]{.class_3054} The Fundamentalists will run immediately to Mark 10:11--12 and say, []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_258}"Oh no, she can't do that. She'll have two living husbands and be committing adultery."

Do you know who "put away" His wife? [God]{.class_3055}---Isaiah 50:1; Jeremiah 3:8. Do you know what God said when He put away His wife? ["She is not my wife, neither am I her husband"]{.class_3054} (Hos. 2:2). In other words, when a legitimate divorce takes place, the wife is no longer married to the husband, and the husband is no longer married to the wife. God Almighty is the example.

After my first wife deserted me and sued for divorce (the State of Alabama automatically granted [me]{.class_3055} a divorce on the grounds that my spouse deserted me, having been gone a whole year), Pensacola Christian tried to get the property of the Brent Baptist Church where I pastored. Back then, the Hortons weren't in competition with Bob Jones University, so they had Bob Jones Jr. write me a letter to get me to resign. (As long as I was pastor, the church was paying its bills, so the idea was to get rid of me so the church would go under financially and have to sell to the Hortons.) Bob Jones Jr. wrote me and said, "You shouldn't be pastoring because a ['bishop']{.class_3054} must be ['the husband of one wife']{.class_3054} (1 Tim. 3:2). You aren't married, so you don't qualify." I wrote him back and said, "All right, I'll get married so I'll qualify." He wrote me back and said, "You can't do that, because then you would have two living wives." I told him, "You are the only president of a University that I know of who thinks 0+1=2."

Now the Holiness teach (and the Fundamentalists follow suit) that if you get divorced and remarried that you are living in adultery, and the only way of rectifying the situation is to get back with the one you left. That's exactly what God [didn't]{.class_3055} allow under the Law. Deuteronomy 24:3--4, ["And if the latter husband hate her, and write her a bill of divorcement . . . or if the latter husband die, which took her to be his wife, Her former husband, which sent her away, may NOT take her again to be his wife . . . for that is ABOMINATION before the LORD."]{.class_3054}

Now Jesus makes it clear that God [allowed]{.class_3055} divorce in the Old Testament even though He didn't approve of it (vs. 5). In the Old Testament, there was no New Birth, there was no indwelling Christ, there was no eternal security for all believers, etc. You have definite advantages the Old Testament saint didn't have. Because of the []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_259}hardness of those Jews' hearts, God allowed them certain things like divorce for any reason and polygamy that He won't allow a born-again child of God.

That is what is known as God's "permissive" will, as opposed to His "directive" will. In other words, there are certain things God allows, although they are not what He intends. For example, it wasn't God's intent for Paul to go to Jerusalem there in Acts 21, and God warned him not to go several times in that chapter. But Paul went anyway, and the Lord took care of him. But Paul, in the "permissive" will of God, ended up losing two years of his ministry while he sat in jail. He ended up in Rome like the Lord intended (Acts 23:11), but he made it floating in on the plank of a shipwreck as a prisoner; whereas he could have come in on first-class accommodations.

God allowed Jacob to have four wives, and Jacob had trouble he could have avoided if he only had one. God allowed those Jews to divorce their wives for any reason, and it ended up getting them in trouble (Mal. 2:14--16). If you have a choice between doing it God's way or doing it your way, you will save yourself a lot of grief by acting in the directive will of God rather than His permissive will.

Now Jesus shows those Pharisees what the directive will of God is, and it's [anti]{.class_3055}-divorce. Some of you are surprised to hear me say that, aren't you? You have been listening to a bunch of foul-mouthed gossips who have no idea about what they're talking. I do not believe in divorce, I don't recommend it, and I have never ["put away"]{.class_3054} a wife.

You say, "Haven't you been divorced twice, Ruckman?" Yeah, and in neither instance did I divorce my wife. I believe in keeping the marriage vows: "for better or for [worse,]{.class_3055} till death do us part." But did you know that all fifty States have "no-fault" divorce laws, now? That means if one party wants out of a marriage, there is nothing the other party can do to stop the divorce.

I learned with my first marriage that if your spouse is hell-bent to get out of the marriage, there is nothing you can do to stop it unless the Lord intervenes in the heart of the other party. The old saying is: "It takes two to tango." If a marriage is going to survive, both parties have to be in agreement that they want to stay together; otherwise it is going to bust up. So my recommendation to couples []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_260}going through marital problems is to do everything they can to keep the marriage together.

Now, God's intent for marriage is [one man for one woman]{.class_3055}---NO MATTER WHAT THE SUPREME COURT OF THE UNITED STATES SAYS! [God]{.class_3055} set up marriage (vs. 6); not the President, the Congress, or the Supreme Court. The government can say blacks are whites, women are men, children are adults, Mexicans are U.S. citizens, Moslems are a peaceful religion, and two dykes, a butch, and a double-breasted fink constitute a marriage; but that doesn't make any of that so. ["God made them MALE and FEMALE"]{.class_3054} (vs. 6); not two faggots, two queers, two Lesbians, or a dyke and a butch.

According to the [Son of God]{.class_3055} (who cares what [you]{.class_3055} think or "feel"), ["a man"]{.class_3054} leaves ["his father and mother"]{.class_3054} and cleaves ["to his wife"]{.class_3054} (vs. 7). Well, first of all, how can you have ["a man"]{.class_3054} and ["his wife"]{.class_3054} in a "marriage" between two queers or two Lesbians? If two men get "married," which one is the "wife"? Neither one! If two dykes get "hitched," which of them is the "man" or the "husband"? Neither one; I don't care if one is a "butch."

Acting like a man when you're [not]{.class_3055} (or acting like a woman when you're not) only makes you a [hypocrite.]{.class_3055} A hypocrite is someone who [pretends]{.class_3055} to be or [acts]{.class_3055} like something he or she is NOT. A husband is not a woman, and a wife is not a man. You say, "According to whom?" According to a [dictionary.]{.class_3055} What you "believe" has nothing at all to do with what God said or what the words mean.

When a society tried to "liberate" its "sexuality," God burned it to the ground (2 Pet. 2:6), and those sex perverts are still burning today (Jude 7---["ETERNAL fire"]{.class_3054}). God called such perversions ["abominations"]{.class_3054} (Lev. 18:22) that pollute a land until it ["vomiteth out her inhabitants"]{.class_3054} (Lev. 18:25). That didn't change in the New Testament (Rom. 1:26--27). You can expect America to get exactly what Sodom and Gomorrah got, what the Canaanites got, and what [God's chosen people]{.class_3055} got (read Lamentations) for committing those same sins.

Notice the essence of a marriage in verse 8: ["And they twain shall be one flesh: so then they are no more twain, but one flesh."]{.class_3054} So while there are  certainly legal and social aspects to []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_261}marriage, marriage is essentially the physical act that binds a man and a woman together. Paul reiterates that in Ephesians 5:31. That is what makes whoremongering so wrong: you are joining your body to a harlot the same way as you would join it to a wife (1 Cor. 6:16). Some of the brethren think that because they only have one marriage certificate, one ceremony, and one ring, that they meet the qualification of 1 Timothy 3:2; when, if truth be told, they might have had marital relations with half a dozen women before they were joined to the one they've got now. Talk about more than one living wife!

In the immediate context of verses 8--9, the ["putting asunder"]{.class_3054} is [not]{.class_3055} the ["bill of divorcement"]{.class_3054} of Deuteronomy 24:1; it is joining your flesh to someone other than your spouse. So in the parallel passage to verses 11--12, divorce is allowed in the case of ["fornication"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 19:9). That ["fornication"]{.class_3054} isn't just "sex before marriage," as the Fundamentalists would lead you to believe; it is any act of sexual sin (1 Cor. 5:1, 10:8; Jude 7), including adultery. So divorce is allowed in the case of [adultery]{.class_3055} or [fornication.]{.class_3055} A "divorce" takes place when a spouse [dies;]{.class_3055} a widow or widower is no longer ["bound by the law"]{.class_3054} to the dead partner (Rom. 7:1--2). And divorce is allowed in the case of [desertion]{.class_3055} (1 Cor. 7:15), because the departing party is no longer rendering ["due benevolence"]{.class_3054} to the deserted spouse (1 Cor. 7:3) and the marriage bond has been broken. And when a legitimate divorce occurs (adultery, desertion, or death), the "offended" party is allowed to remarry without it being a sin (1 Cor. 7:27--28).

So 1 Timothy 3:2 has nothing to do with a man who has been divorced for legitimate (i.e., [Scriptural]{.class_3055}) reasons. If a wife has stepped out on that man or has deserted him and he becomes divorced legally, he is not bound to that wife and doesn't have "two living wives." He has the one he's got. He should be faithful to her. First Timothy 3:2 is a prohibition on [polygamy,]{.class_3055} not Biblical divorce and remarriage.

There are two things we need to go back and pick up before we leave this passage. The first is in verse 3: ["And he]{.class_3054} \[i.e., Jesus Christ\] [answered and said unto them, What did MOSES command you?"]{.class_3054} There went Astruc, Graf, Wellhausen, and the rest of the higher critics down the drain. According to your Saviour, []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_262}[Moses]{.class_3055} wrote Genesis, Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, and Deuteronomy; not any unknown, anonymous J, E, P, D, H, and R writers who lived up around the Exile and intertestamental period. These stumblebums who were trying to rid the world of the authority of Moses forgot the Highest Critic in the universe wrote the Book they were criticizing; His Book criticizes them while they are criticizing it (Heb. 4:12). The very author they were trying to get rid of will testify against them at the Judgment (John 5:45--47).

The second point to be noted is in verse 6: ["But from the beginning of the creation God made them male and female."]{.class_3054} And there go Darwin, Huxley, Lyell, Hawking, and Dawkins. According to the Son of God, [who was there when it happened]{.class_3055} (John 1:1--3), God created the universe. There was no eternity of matter, "Big Bang," random chance, spontaneous generation, "puddle to paradise," Paleozoic Era, Mesozoic Era, Triassic Period, Jurassic Period, missing links, [Australopithecus,]{.class_3055} "Lucy," Neanderthals, Peking Man, Java Man, Nebraska Man, etc. God created the present world in six days, just as Moses recorded it in 1500 B.C., and God created man ["male and female"]{.class_3054} on the sixth day. He didn't pick a couple of "ape-people" who had evolved up from the primordial slime and designate them "Adam and Eve" like the Pope will tell you. When Pope John Paul II said, "Genesis is a myth" ([Omni]{.class_3055} magazine, Dec. 1984, pg. 68), and then reaffirmed that teaching on October 23, 1996 before the Pontifical Academy of Sciences, he set himself in direct opposition to the One he professed to represent. Jesus Christ affirmed that God created the universe and that God created man exactly as Moses wrote in Genesis 1.

13 And they brought young children to him, that he should touch them: and his disciples rebuked those that brought them.

14 But when Jesus saw it, he was much displeased, and said unto them, Suffer the little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.

15 Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall not enter []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_263}therein.

16 And he took them up in his arms, put his hands upon them, and blessed them.

Now we have already commented on the spiritual application of these verses in our comments on 9:36--37, 42, so we refer you there. While it is true that "Jesus loves the little children," a grown adult needs to be very careful in applying that song to himself. Up to the point where that child knows the difference ["between good and evil"]{.class_3054} (Deut. 1:39), he is innocent, because ["sin is not imputed when there is no law"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 5:13). But when the law of God written in the conscience begins to take effect, it accuses that child of sin (Rom. 2:15), and the older that kid gets, the longer the list of accusations against him becomes. He becomes counted as a sinner under the wrath of God and an enemy of Him (Rom. 5:8--10). No grown man, in his right mind, who is lost, has a right to sing, "Jesus loves me! This I know, for the Bible tells me so." [The Bible says no such thing.]{.class_3055} It says:

["He that believeth on him is not condemned: but he that believeth not is condemned already, because he hath not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God"]{.class_3054} (John 3:18).

["He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him"]{.class_3054} (John 3:36).

What may be true of "little ones" who "to Him belong" is not true of a grown sinner who rejects Christ for his own self-righteousness. If you want God to love you like He does the "little children," then you had better ["receive the kingdom of God as a little child"]{.class_3054} (vs. 15) by receiving the New Birth (John 3:3--7) through faith in Christ (John 3:15--16).

That is why a lot of doctors, lawyers, bankers, businessmen, politicians, and "bigwigs" don't get saved: they don't want to become like a little child in their faith. Paul said:

["For ye see your calling, brethren, how that not many wise men after the flesh, not many mighty, not many noble, are called: But God hath chosen the foolish things of []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_264}the world to confound the wise; and God hath chosen the weak things of the world to confound the things which are mighty"]{.class_3054} (1 Cor. 1:26--27).

The only time the Bible says Jesus ["rejoiced"]{.class_3054} was over God the Father hiding some things ["from the wise and prudent"]{.class_3054} and revealing ["them unto babes"]{.class_3054} (Luke 10:21). So if you want salvation and revelation from God, you have to humble yourself to the level of a child.

17 And when he was gone forth into the way, there came one running, and kneeled to him, and asked him, Good Master, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?

18 And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? there is none good but one, that is, God.

19 Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Defraud not, Honour thy father and mother.

20 And he answered and said unto him, Master, all these have I observed from my youth.

21 Then Jesus beholding him loved him, and said unto him, One thing thou lackest: go thy way, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, take up the cross, and follow me.

22 And he was sad at that saying, and went away grieved: for he had great possessions.

Notice the question: ["what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?"]{.class_3054} (vs. 17). That can be taken two ways in the New Testament. It can refer to an inheritance in the Millennial kingdom (1 Cor. 6:9--10; Gal. 5:19--21). You know that it has nothing to do with [getting]{.class_3055} and [keeping]{.class_3055} eternal life because Paul warns the believer about the sins that cause him to lose that inheritance (Eph. 5:5--6). That is the same apostle who told you: ["And grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption"]{.class_3054} (Eph. 4:30). A Christian may have his flesh destroyed by sin, but his ["spirit"]{.class_3054} will ["be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_265} (1 Cor. 5:5).

But the inheritance can be eternal life itself (1 Pet. 1:4). That is what is referred to in verse 17 (cf. Matt. 19:16). That being the case, how would you answer the question, given what you know about salvation from the rest of the New Testament?

["That whosoever BELIEVETH in him should not perish, but have eternal life . . . . He that BELIEVETH on the Son hath everlasting life . . ."]{.class_3054} (John 3:15, 36).

["My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me; And I GIVE unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand"]{.class_3054} (John 10:27--28).

["For the wages of sin is death; but the GIFT of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 6:23).

["And this is the record, that God hath GIVEN to us eternal life, and this life is IN HIS SON. He that HATH THE SON hath life; and he that hath not the Son of God hath not life. These things have I written unto you that BELIEVE on the name of the Son of God; that ye may know that ye have eternal life, and that ye may believe on the name of the Son of God"]{.class_3054} (1 John 5:11--13).

If you have any sense, [that]{.class_3055} is how you would answer the question. I sure wouldn't tell him any fool thing like: "Repent, believe, confess, and be buptized," or "receive the initial evidence of the baptism of the Holyghost: speaking in other tongues," or "find the church Christ founded, join it, receive the sacraments, and die in a state of grace." Those all end with: "and you MIGHT make it." None of those things give assurance of eternal life. If you don't believe me, just ask anyone doing those things if he knows for [sure]{.class_3055} he'll make it to Heaven when he dies. Not a one of them does. I know; I have asked. The Campbellite will tell you, "I hope I am." The Charismatic will tell you, "I hope I am" (i.e., if he doesn't commit the "unpardonable sin"---see our comments on 3:28--30). The Catholic will tell you no one can know till he dies.

That Bible says you [can]{.class_3055} know (1 John 5:13). Now do you know or don't you? If not, why not? Could it be that you are trusting on something besides the finished work of Jesus Christ? Then no wonder []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_266}you don't know.

Now Jesus doesn't tell this fellow anything at all like ["Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved"]{.class_3054} (Acts 16:31). Jesus didn't say one word to him about faith or grace or the Gospel; it's all [works]{.class_3055} from start to finish.

That's the difference between Old Testament salvation and New Testament salvation: they are NOT the same. Jesus doesn't say anything to this fellow about "looking forward to the cross." It's "don't do this" (vs. 19) and "do this" (vs. 21). If you preached that today, you would be [cursed by God]{.class_3055} (Gal. 1:6--9). That is why you have to rightly divide that Book (2 Tim. 2:15).

This fellow is a [Jew under the Law.]{.class_3055} Jesus hasn't died for anybody yet. ["The gospel of the grace of God"]{.class_3054} (Acts 20:24) hasn't been revealed to anybody yet. Paul isn't even saved yet; he is still a Pharisee at this point (Phil. 3:5; Acts 23:6, 26:4--5). Before the death, burial, and resurrection of Christ, the Law was in effect, and you can read passage after passage in the Old Testament where [works]{.class_3055} are involved in salvation. I will give you one example.

Read Ezekiel 18:4--9. Fundamentalists will quote that last phrase from verse 4 to prove that ["the wages of sin is death"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 6:23): ["the soul that sinneth, it shall die."]{.class_3054} They will then ignore the rest of the passage. Ezekiel 18:5 says, ["But if a man be just, and DO that which is LAWFUL and right"]{.class_3054}; then it's [works]{.class_3055} in verses 6, 7, 8, and 9. There isn't one word about [faith]{.class_3055} or [grace.]{.class_3055} Verse 9 of Ezekiel 18 ends with ["he is JUST, he shall surely LIVE, saith the Lord GOD."]{.class_3054} Based on what? Based on [works.]{.class_3055}

Now look at Ezekiel 18:21. A wicked man can be saved. How? By believing God? By accepting Christ? Not at all.

"But if the wicked will TURN from all his sins that he hath committed, KEEP all my STATUTES, and DO that which is LAWFUL and right, he shall surely LIVE, he shall not die."

WORKS! Works from start to finish. Moreover, a righteous man can [lose his salvation]{.class_3055} if he ["turneth away from his righteousness, and committeth iniquity"]{.class_3054} (Ezek. 18:24). Is that New Testament salvation? Of course not! So don't talk like a fool and say things like: "There is only one way of salvation in the Bible" []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_267}or "People in the Old Testament were saved by looking forward to the cross, and people in the New Testament are saved by looking back at the cross."

Now this fellow who comes to Jesus asking what to do to ["inherit eternal life"]{.class_3054} is known as the "rich, young ruler." He is called that because you are told in Luke 18:23 that he was ["very rich,"]{.class_3054} Matthew 19:20 calls him a ["young man,"]{.class_3054} and Luke 18:18 says he was ["a certain ruler."]{.class_3054} In politeness and respect to Christ, this young man calls Him, ["Good Master"]{.class_3054} (vs. 17).

But Christ turns the compliment right back on the fellow: ["Why callest thou me good? There is none good but one, that is, God"]{.class_3054} (vs. 18). Jesus uses the word ["good"]{.class_3054} in the Pauline doctrinal sense (Rom. 3:12). It's good in the sense of being [sinless.]{.class_3055} According to the Son of God, there hasn't been one human being since Adam, outside of Jesus Christ, who has been ["good."]{.class_3054} People don't like that Book because it has nothing good to say about human nature.

The application of Christ's words to [Himself]{.class_3055} should be obvious. [If He were not God, then He was not good.]{.class_3055} People, if Jesus Christ were just a "good teacher," then He was no better than Moses, Zoroaster, Mohammed, Buddha, Confucius, the Dalai Lama, the Pope, Spinoza, Kant, Marx, Nietzsche, or any professor at any State College or "Ivy League" University; i.e., [He was a sinner just like everyone else.]{.class_3055} If Jesus Christ were not God, then He was [not]{.class_3055} a "good teacher." He would be a blaspheming [liar]{.class_3055} or a deranged, deluded [lunatic,]{.class_3055} but a "good teacher" He would not be. But if He were who He said He was, then He was more than merely a "good teacher"; He was God Almighty ["in the flesh"]{.class_3054} (1 Tim. 3:16; John 1:14). That is where all the [evidence]{.class_3055} points (see our comments on 4:41, 8:31).

Notice what Christ tells the rich man in verse 19 and what He [doesn't]{.class_3055} say. ["Thou knowest the commandments"]{.class_3054} (vs. 19). The reference is to the "Ten" by what follows, but notice what is missing. The Ten Commandments are divided into two groups: the first deals with your love toward God and the second deals with your love toward your fellow man (12:30--31). Christ doesn't bring up the first four commandments dealing with God at all. Let's just face it, shall we, [none of us]{.class_3055} have loved God with ALL our heart, soul, strength, and mind. If you are [honest]{.class_3055} with yourself, you [know]{.class_3055} there have []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_268}been times when you put yourself before God. Well, that's idolatry; you've broken the first commandment of the Ten. Christ left out all the commandments concerning God to teach this young man something: [he didn't love God with all his heart, soul, strength, and mind.]{.class_3055} We are about to see this young man has an idol in his heart: a [materialistic]{.class_3055} one.

That leads us to the second thing Christ leaves out: the commandment against [covetousness.]{.class_3055} According to the Apostle Paul, ["covetousness . . . is idolatry"]{.class_3054} (Col. 3:5). So this fellow isn't loving God as he ought, and he isn't loving his neighbor as he ought because he's covetous. The Lord Jesus is using the Law against this young fellow to expose his sin to him: ["that sin by the commandment might become exceeding sinful"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 7:13). That's one of the uses of the Law: to expose and convict of sin. The other two are to bring order to a sinful world (1 Tim. 1:9--10) and to bring a sinner to the only One who kept the Law so He could give the sinner His righteousness: [Jesus Christ]{.class_3055} (Gal. 3:24).

Now there are two things in the commandments given in verse 19 of which we should take note. The first is: ["Do not kill."]{.class_3054} This is the one on which Liberals and infidels always love to harp, because God does command those Israelites to kill back there in the Old Testament, after telling them: ["Thou shalt not kill"]{.class_3054} (Exod. 20:13). The "bleeding hearts" will condemn capital punishment because it is killing, and the "peaceniks" will condemn war for the same reason. That's because they don't allow God Almighty the same privilege they allow "nine old men" (and women, these days) on the Supreme Court of the United States: [the right to define and interpret His own words.]{.class_3055}

The Lord tells you exactly what He meant by ["Thou shalt not kill"]{.class_3054} in the Scriptures. It has nothing to do with capital punishment; the Lord [commanded]{.class_3055} it, especially in the case of a [murderer]{.class_3055} (Gen. 9:6). It does not include war.[ "The LORD is a man of war"]{.class_3054} (Exod. 15:3), and He uses war as a tool of [judgment]{.class_3055} (Exod. 17:14--16; 1 Sam. 15:2--3). The Lord defines "killing" both in the Old Testament (Exod. 21:12--14; Deut. 19:11) and the New Testament (Matt. 19:18---["Thou shalt do no murder"]{.class_3054}).

The second thing you should notice in verse 19 of Mark 10 is the commandment ["Defraud not."]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_269} That is not technically a part of the Ten, but it is given in one of the definitions of commandments 8 and 9 (["Thou shalt not steal"]{.class_3054}; ["Thou shalt not bear false witness"]{.class_3054}) in Leviticus 19:11--13. Jesus throws in ["Defraud not"]{.class_3054} because of the young man's position as a ruler. The judges who ruled Israel could be subject to defrauding the people (1 Sam. 2:29--30 cf. 1 Sam. 12:3--4).

Now look at how the young man answers Christ in verse 20 and look at Christ's response to that in verse 21. Verse 20: ["Master, ALL THESE have I observed from my youth"]{.class_3054}; Verse 21: ["Then Jesus beholding him loved him, and said unto him, ONE THING THOU LACKEST."]{.class_3054} That young fellow said, "Jesus, I've kept all those commandments," and Jesus didn't call him a liar. That fellow never committed adultery, not even by looking ["on a woman to lust after her"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 5:28). He was never ["angry with his brother without a cause"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 5:22) or hated ["his brother"]{.class_3054} (1 John 3:15). He didn't lie; he honored and obeyed momma and daddy. That was a good man. He wasn't a hypocrite like those Pharisees in Matthew 23.

Jesus loved that young man because of his righteousness and sincerity, but He put a test on that rich, young ruler on the one commandment He omitted: ["Thou shalt not covet."]{.class_3054} ["Sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven"]{.class_3054} (vs. 21). In other words, love your poor neighbors more than you love your stuff. ["And come, take up the cross, and follow me."]{.class_3054} Jesus was saying, "Since I'm God (vs. 18), love me more than you love yourself and what you want."

Of course, that reference to taking ["up the cross"]{.class_3054} would mean no more to that rich, young ruler than it meant to the disciples in 8:34. Jesus didn't say anything here about dying on the cross for that fellow's sins. But he got the gist of what Christ meant: dying to self and wants (["let him DENY HIMSELF"]{.class_3054} ---8:34). It's still [works.]{.class_3055} So although the young man could be considered as good as any man can get outside of Christ, he was still [outside of Christ.]{.class_3055} To get the "inheritance," both in the form of ["eternal life"]{.class_3054} (vs. 17) and a Millennial reign (["treasure in heaven"]{.class_3054}---vs. 21 cf. Matt. 6:19--21), he still had to believe and follow Jesus Christ.

So what you have here is a []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_270}[picture of New Testament salvation]{.class_3055} given in a [completely Old Testament context.]{.class_3055} That is what messes up all the Conservatives and Fundamentalists. They make the [type]{.class_3055} the [actuality,]{.class_3055} and it's [not.]{.class_3055} The Old Testament reality is salvation by [works;]{.class_3055} the New Testament picture is coming to [Jesus Christ]{.class_3055} for salvation. The Book is a double-edged razor blade, man: it will cut you to shreds if you don't treat it with kid gloves. You can't just charge in there and make the whole thing match the Pauline Epistles. What you can apply spiritually or devotionally may not apply [doctrinally]{.class_3055} to [you.]{.class_3055}

Now while this rich, young ruler could have been considered "saved" in the Old Testament sense up to this point, he goes away [lost]{.class_3055} after his encounter with Christ. Why? Because Christ had ["the words of eternal life"]{.class_3054} (John 6:68). Because those Jews had to come to [Christ]{.class_3055} for eternal life (John 5:40). Whether that rich, young ruler realized it or not, the last two words Jesus said (["follow me"]{.class_3054}) were the touchstone for him getting ["eternal life."]{.class_3054} Whatever revelation he had been following up to that point was lost when he turned and walked away from Christ ["grieved: for he had great possessions"]{.class_3054} (vs. 22). He made his choice between[ "God and mammon"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 6:24; Luke 16:13), and the winner was [mammon.]{.class_3055}

If you want a [spiritual]{.class_3055} application of the text, then here it is. Do you consider yourself a "good" person? Well, a "good" man or a "good" woman will agree with God's assessment of him or her in that Book (Rom. 3:10--23). A "good" person won't remain willfully ignorant of God's righteousness when it is revealed to him and freely offered to him (Rom. 10:3--4). A "good" person won't turn up his nose at a truly good man who loved him enough to die for him and his sins. A "good" person would look at such a man and count his own righteousness as ["dung"]{.class_3054} in comparison (Phil. 3:8--9). In other words, if you had ["an honest and good heart,"]{.class_3054} you would hear the word of God concerning you, your own righteousness, and Christ's righteousness; and you would receive it and believe it as God's word and not man's (Luke 8:15 cf. 1 Thess. 2:13). Don't tell me you're a good person when you [reject Jesus Christ.]{.class_3055}

23 And Jesus looked round about, and saith unto his []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_271}disciples, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!

24 And the disciples were astonished at his words. But Jesus answereth again, and saith unto them, Children, how hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter into the kingdom of God!

25 It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.

26 And they were astonished out of measure, saying among themselves, Who then can be saved?

27 And Jesus looking upon them saith, With men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.

The rich, young ruler leaves, being in grief over Christ's words because he loved his ["great possessions"]{.class_3054} (vs. 22). Christ then turns to His disciples and says, ["How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God"]{.class_3054} (vs. 23). Since Jesus is dealing with a [spiritual]{.class_3055} kingdom here, we may make application to New Testament salvation. There are some things that keep you from coming to Christ for salvation. Paul talks that way about the wisdom of this world (1 Cor. 1:21). Over in 4:19 here in Mark, we learned that ["the deceitfulness of riches"]{.class_3054} can cause the believer to become ["unfruitful"]{.class_3054} (see our remarks under that verse). Riches can become a god in competition with the Lord God (Luke 16:13); they can get you to trust in them instead of the Lord, as we will see in verse 24 here in Mark 10.

["The disciples were astonished"]{.class_3054} at Christ's words in verse 23. Why? Because in the Old Testament, [riches were a reward from God for doing right.]{.class_3055} Job was rich because he ["feared God, and eschewed evil"]{.class_3054} (Job 1:1--3). Abraham was rich because he served God (Gen. 13:2--4). ["Riches"]{.class_3054} were part of the Abrahamic covenant (Gen. 27:28, 39 cf. Gen. 36:7). God gave David riches for serving Him (1 Chron. 29:12, 28). God gave Solomon ["riches"]{.class_3054} when he asked wisdom of the Lord (1 Kings 3:11--13; 2 Chron. 1:11--12). God gave Jehoshaphat riches (2 Chron. 17:5); ditto Hezekiah (2 Chron. 32:27). David said in Psalm 112:1, 3---[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw1732.xhtml_page_272}["blessed is the man that feareth the LORD, that delighteth greatly in his commandments . . . . Wealth and riches shall be in his house: and his righteousness endureth for ever."]{.class_3054}

So you can see why the disciples were so shocked at what Jesus said. That young ruler not only had the riches, he had the righteousness to back them up. Those disciples thought for sure that young man was a "shoe in" for eternal life.

But it is not riches or money itself that damns a man. Money is not ["the root of all evil"]{.class_3054}; ["the LOVE of money is"]{.class_3054} (1 Tim. 6:10). Here, it is not the riches that make it hard for the wealthy to ["enter into the kingdom of God"]{.class_3054}; it is [trusting]{.class_3055} in those riches (vs. 24).

Corrupt, apostate versions like the [RSV,]{.class_3055} the [NRSV,]{.class_3055} the "Conservative" [ESV,]{.class_3055} the [NIV,]{.class_3055} and the [NASV]{.class_3055} get rid of ["for them that trust in riches"]{.class_3054} from the verse so it is ["hard"]{.class_3055} or ["difficult"]{.class_3055} to be saved. NO, IT ISN'T! Salvation is as easy as [taking a drink of water]{.class_3055} (Rev. 22:17; note the word ["freely"]{.class_3054} in the verse). It is as easy as receiving a gift (Rom. 6:23; note that that gift is ["free"]{.class_3054} in Rom. 5:15--18). It is as easy as picking up a check at the bank which someone else put in for you (Rom. 3:24; note the word[ "freely"]{.class_3054} again). It is as simple as accepting an invitation (Gen. 24:58; Matt. 11:28--30; Luke 14:16--24). Salvation is as simple as taking a bath or shower (Mark 1:40--44). It is as simple as opening a door to let someone in (Rev. 3:20). What do you mean ["How difficult it is to enter the kingdom of God"]{.class_3055}?
