It destroys a ["tenth part"]{.class_3054} of Jerusalem, killing 7,000 people (Rev. 11:13), and it splits Babylon ["into three parts"]{.class_3054} (Rev. 16:19). It destroys every major city in the world (Rev. 16:19), and sinks every island and levels every mountain (Rev. 16:20). You have to admit, "that am some earthquake." The world has never seen anything like it. It will be worse than the Alaskan earthquake of 1964, the San Francisco earthquake of 1906, the Missouri earthquake of 1811 (and 1812!), the Haitian earthquake of 2010, the Indonesian earthquake of 2004, and the Nepal earthquake of 2015 combined.

["There shall be famines and troubles."]{.class_3054} Deniers of Bible prophecy may dispute the claim; but the Socialists, the right-wingers, the "sky-is-falling" climate-change proponents, the geologists (U.S. Geological Survey), and humanitarian groups will all tell you famines [have increased]{.class_3055} in recent times (the Twenty-first Century), and 61 major famines have occurred (as of 2013), as opposed to 37 from 1900--1999, and 27 from 1800--1899. One out of every nine people on earth is malnourished; in third-world countries, that's almost 13% of the population. Two-thirds of the people in Asia go hungry, but Africa has the highest percentage of malnutrition per capita---one out of four. Over three million children die every year of malnutrition, with another 100 million being severely underweight.

But all of that is just the orchestra tuning up. In Revelation 6:5--6, the black horse rider measures out only enough wheat or barley for daily sustenance. Even then, it costs a full day's wage (["a penny"]{.class_3054}). If you transferred that over into our modern economy, you would be paying nearly \$60.00 for a third of a pound of wheat flour or a pound of barley flour. Since it takes a pound of flour to make a loaf of bread, those folks in the Tribulation are working a full day to get one loaf of barley bread (the cheap stuff) or a third of a loaf of wheat bread. That's going to be rough if you have a family then, boy. With 24 slices of bread in a loaf, with 70 calories per slice, a family of four aren't even getting "starvation rations" (1,100 calories a day in the ghettos during World War II). That's not a "regional" famine in the Tribulation; that's [worldwide.]{.class_3055}

The ["troubles"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_342} of verse 8 are not the normal troubles of life. In reference to the return of Christ, they are like no troubles ever experienced. The Old Testament describes the Tribulation as ["a time of trouble, such as never was since there was a nation even to that same time"]{.class_3054} (Dan. 12:1 cf. Matt. 24:21). That certainly was not true of the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70, nor is it true right now.

Any time you hear some teacher or preacher telling you that we are going through the Tribulation [now,]{.class_3055} awaiting a post-Tribulation rapture of the Church, consider the following passages: Revelation 6:12--13, 8:7--11, 9:1--12, 16:2--11, 21. Those are all ["troubles"]{.class_3054} that take place in the Tribulation and not until. Stars aren't falling from heaven. The sun's not turning black, nor is the moon turning to blood (that's no reference to any "blood moon" lunar eclipse either). A third of the trees and all the grass aren't being burnt up; a third of the sea life isn't dying; a third of the ships aren't being sunk. No water is turning to blood nor being poisoned by meteorites. Demoniac locusts aren't coming out of the Pit and tormenting men for five months. There is no worldwide outbreak of leprosy on those who've received the Mark of the Beast; no fifty-pound hailstones are falling out of the sky; the sun is not scorching men worldwide with fire; and there is no painful darkness on the capital city of the Antichrist. Those are all ["troubles"]{.class_3054} men face during the [Tribulation.]{.class_3055}

The ["sorrows"]{.class_3054} of verse 8 are associated with the pain and travail of childbirth (Isa. 13:8; Jer. 30:6--7; Hos. 13:12--14; 1 Thess. 5:3). The false Christs, wars, earthquakes, famines, and troubles are the "contractions" that finally end up in the Second Advent. Scofield says, "The death-agony of this age is the birth-agony of the next."

9 But take heed to yourselves: for they shall deliver you up to councils; and in the synagogues ye shall be beaten: and ye shall be brought before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them.

10 And the gospel must first be published among all nations.

11 But when they shall lead you, and deliver you up, take no thought beforehand what ye shall speak, neither do ye []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_343}premeditate: but whatsoever shall be given you in that hour, that speak ye: for it is not ye that speak, but the Holy Ghost.

12 Now the brother shall betray the brother to death, and the father the son; and children shall rise up against their parents, and shall cause them to be put to death.

13 And ye shall be hated of all men for my name's sake: but he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.

Now some of the passage can certainly be applied [practically]{.class_3055} to the ministries of the apostles and early Christians in the book of Acts. They certainly were delivered up to councils in the book of Acts (Acts 4:1--21, 5:26--40, 6:12--16, 23:1); they certainly were ["brought before rulers and kings"]{.class_3054} for Christ's sake (Acts 12:1--3, 16:19--20, 24:1, 25:1--23); they were testimonies against those who rejected Christ (Acts 7:51--53, 8:20--23, 13:8--11, 45--51, 18:5--6, 28:23--28). But Acts is not the context of the statement; [the Second Advent is.]{.class_3055}

There are two possibilities, [doctrinally,]{.class_3055} for this passage. The first is, while Christ may be speaking practically to the apostles, prophetically He is addressing the 144,000 in the Tribulation (Rev. 7:1--8). But there is also the possibility that "the Twelve" will have "second comings" and ministries of their own during the Tribulation. After all, Moses and Elijah are coming back (Mal. 4:4--6 cf. Rev. 11:3--6), Judas is coming back (John 17:12 cf. 2 Thess. 2:3; Rev. 9:11), Jesus Christ is coming back (John 14:3), and possibly John the apostle as well (Rev. 10:11).

Over in Matthew 10:23, Jesus told the apostles that they wouldn't go ["over the cities of Israel, till the Son of man be come." ]{.class_3054}That didn't happen at the First Advent because Jesus didn't visit every city in Israel. It awaits future fulfillment. So either "the Twelve" are types of the 144,000, or they're coming back. Wouldn't it be something if Simon Peter confronted his "successor" over in Rome? That would be some conversation.

Verse 10 makes a wonderful missionary challenge and a wonderful challenge for those involved in printing and distributing []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_344}Scriptures, tracts, and Christian literature; but of course, it has nothing to do with the text. There is nothing about the Rapture of the Body of Christ in the chapter. The only body of Christ there is, is the physical one sitting on the Mount of Olives. The spiritual Body made up of both Jews and Gentiles in Christ doesn't even exist yet, and won't until Calvary (Eph. 2:16). Jesus is dealing with Jewish disciples under the Law who know nothing of ["the gospel of the grace of God"]{.class_3054} (Acts 20:24).

As I pointed out to you under 1:1, 14--15, this is the ["gospel of the kingdom,"]{.class_3054} and that is verified by the parallel account in Matthew 24:14. This is yet another example in the book of Mark where you have to watch out for an [incomplete statement]{.class_3055} (see our comments on 10:2, 11).

That doesn't mean the Church shouldn't strive to fulfill the Great Commission (see 16:15). If [every]{.class_3055} Christian who had ever accepted Christ had led at least [one soul]{.class_3055} to the Lord, the whole world would have been evangelized centuries ago. It is to the Church's shame that there are people on this earth who have never heard the name of Jesus Christ or seen a tract or even one verse of Scripture in their native tongue. But it is not necessary that every "people group" have an indigenous church in order for the Rapture to take place. The Rapture will occur in God's own time, but the Advent won't take place until every nation in the Tribulation has had the ["gospel of the kingdom"]{.class_3054} preached in it.

The ["gospel of the kingdom"]{.class_3054} is [not]{.class_3055} ["the gospel of the grace of God"]{.class_3054} preached by Paul. The way you know that is because salvation in the Tribulation involves both faith [and works]{.class_3055} (Rev. 12:17, 14:12). That is [not]{.class_3055} your salvation (Eph. 2:8--9; Tit. 3:5), no matter how many Fundamental Baptist pastors tell you it is. Those saints in the Tribulation are [not]{.class_3055} in ["one body"]{.class_3054} (Rev. 7 cf. 1 Cor. 12:13; Eph. 4:4), and they don't even go to the same place you go when you die (Rev. 6:9). The Fundamental Baptists may have salvation correct [right now,]{.class_3055} but they throw the Book out the window when it doesn't say what they want it to teach.

Again, verses 11--13 certainly can have [practical]{.class_3055} application to the Church Age. The born-again child of God certainly can speak the things given him by the Holy Ghost because the Holy Ghost lives in him (1 Cor. 2:4, 13). Christ came to set the believer []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_345}["at variance"]{.class_3054} with those of his own household who won't trust the Lord for salvation; they become the enemies of the Christian even though they are kin (Matt. 10:35--36). And the lost world certainly has no love for God's people (John 15:18--19, 17:14; 1 John 3:13). Nevertheless, the passage is still aimed [prophetically]{.class_3055} at Jews in the Tribulation.

You know that for sure because of the end of verse 13 and on into the next passage---verse 14. ["The end"]{.class_3054} in verse 13 has nothing to do with the end of a man's life. It is the end of a period of time immediately preceding the return of Christ (vs. 7)---the Tribulation. That's what follows in verse 14. It is a time when the temple has been rebuilt, and the Antichrist stops the sacrifices and sets up his image (Dan. 9:17, 12:11; Rev. 13:14--15; 2 Thess. 2:3--4). So once more, you are dealing with [Jews in the Tribulation.]{.class_3055}

There are Jews in the Tribulation (the resurrected apostles or the 144,000 or [both]{.class_3055}) who are preaching the ["gospel of the kingdom"]{.class_3054} in Jewish synagogues. They and their converts are being persecuted by their fellow Jews and betrayed by their Jewish family members. They are being hauled ["before rulers and kings"]{.class_3054} for the purpose of being executed for their message: get right because the King is coming back, and boy, is He mad!

["But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved"]{.class_3054} (vs. 13). The declaration has nothing to do with any Christian now, this side of the Rapture, who is trying to stay saved by works and not "fall from grace" and lose his salvation. Not only is ["the end"]{.class_3054} not a reference to the end of an individual's life, [there are no "Christians" in Mark 13]{.class_3055}. Jesus is addressing [Jews]{.class_3055} before the Crucifixion about [Jews]{.class_3055} in the Tribulation. Besides, the believer in Christ has the promise that he is confirmed ["unto the end"]{.class_3054} so he will be ["blameless"]{.class_3054} at the return of his Saviour (1 Cor. 1:7--8). He is sealed by the Holy Spirit to that time (Eph. 4:30), and the Christian can be ["confident"]{.class_3054} that the Lord will ["perform"]{.class_3054} what He promised (Phil. 1:6). So the verse does not mean a Christian can lose his salvation.

The Fundamental Baptist has got the doctrine of Eternal Security down pat, but insisting that salvation is the same in all ages, he must []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_346}now explain away Christ's words for what he knows is so in the Church Age. So he makes the salvation of verse 13 [physical salvation]{.class_3055}---God sparing the lives of everyone who survives the Tribulation.

That's kind of an empty, meaningless interpretation. It's like saying, "If you don't die, then you'll live." You think?! You mean those ["souls" "under the altar"]{.class_3054} there in Revelation 6:9, who ["were slain for the word of God, and for the testimony which they hold"]{.class_3054} aren't ["saved"]{.class_3054}? Why, they are resurrected, blessed, holy, and given a reign with Christ in the Millennium (Rev. 20:4--6). You don't call that ["saved"]{.class_3054}?

Now I have already given you the references for what it takes to be saved in the Tribulation. It is [not]{.class_3055} just "by grace through faith" as a free gift. It is having ["the testimony of Jesus"]{.class_3054} AND keeping ["the commandments of God"]{.class_3054} (Rev. 12:17). I don't know if you looked up the companion verse in Revelation 14:12, but if you had, you should have noticed something.

"Here is the patience of the saints: here are they that KEEP the commandments of God, AND the faith of Jesus."

Did you get the [two]{.class_3055} things the Tribulation saints are to ["keep"]{.class_3054}? Not only are they to ["keep the commandments of God,"]{.class_3054} they are to ["keep . . . the faith of Jesus."]{.class_3054} This is not the same as when Paul said at the end of his life, ["I have kept the faith"]{.class_3054} (2 Tim. 4:7). That had to do with a reward in Heaven, not his salvation (2 Tim. 4:8). Not so with Revelation 14:12, which is evident by the following statements written to the [Hebrews]{.class_3055} (not "Christian Jews"). Note the [conditional]{.class_3055} nature of them, pointing clearly to salvation in the[ Tribulation]{.class_3055} and not now.

["But Christ as a son over his own house; whose house are we, IF we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm unto THE END"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 3:6).

["For we are made partakers of Christ, IF we hold the beginning of our confidence stedfast unto THE END"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 3:14).

["For God is not unrighteous to forget your WORK and LABOUR of love, which ye have shewed toward his name, in that ye have ministered to the saints, and do minister ]{.class_3054}\[see Matt. 25:34-40; James 2:14-17\][]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_347}[. And we desire that every one of you shew the same diligence to the FULL ASSURANCE of hope unto THE END"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 6:11).

["The end," "the end," "the end"]{.class_3054}---someone has to hold onto his ["confidence"]{.class_3054} and ["assurance"]{.class_3054} until Jesus Christ comes back at His Advent. That's not you or me, because ["If we believe not, yet he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself"]{.class_3054} (2 Tim. 2:13). If you trusted Christ for salvation and then had a "lapse of faith," it wouldn't affect your salvation one way or another. Your salvation doesn't depend on you holding onto your assurance of salvation; it depends on [who holds onto you]{.class_3055} (1 Pet. 1:5). It has nothing to do with keeping ["yourselves in the love of God"]{.class_3054} (Jude 21) because [nothing]{.class_3055} can ["separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 8:38--39).

So the salvation of verse 13 is much more than physical salvation. It has to do with a Jew in the Tribulation trusting Christ, keeping the Old Testament commandments, and holding onto his assurance of salvation either until he dies for the sake of Christ (Rev. 12:11) or until Christ comes back at His Advent. If he doesn't ["hold fast the profession of ]{.class_3054}\[his\][ faith"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 10:23), he can ["sin wilfully after"]{.class_3054} he has ["received the knowledge of the truth"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 10:26) and can cast away his ["confidence"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 10:35) until he draws back to ["perdition"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 10:39).

In the Tribulation, if a Jew trusts Christ and then goes and takes the mark, he's [damned]{.class_3055}---no eternal security. Far from being "safe am I in the hollow of His hand" (see John 10:28--29), it will be ["a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 10:31), because the Lord is going to ["judge his people"]{.class_3054} (and I don't mean "Christians"; in Hebrews, God's people are [Jews]{.class_3055} just as they were throughout three-quarters of your Bible) and take vengeance on them (Heb. 10:30) just as He promised He would do back in Deuteronomy 32:35--36. You see, people, you can't charge through that Bible forcing it to conform to what you know is so for the Church Age. That Book was written to a lot more people than just the Body of Christ.

A lot of heresies are just [truths]{.class_3055} [misplaced]{.class_3055}. The Seventh-day-Adventist teaching on the Sabbath is right---JUST NOT RIGHT NOW! []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_348}It will be in the Tribulation. Nearly every denomination, except the Baptists and Presbyterians, believe a Christian can lose his salvation. Well, where did they get it? From Scripture\![ Just not any Scripture aimed at a born-again believer in the Body of Christ.]{.class_3055} They pick verses aimed at someone in the Old Testament, the Tribulation, or the Millennium, and force them on the New Testament Christian. So the Baptists and Presbyterians react the other way by forcing Church Age passages on the rest of Scripture. [Both are wrong.]{.class_3055} You had better rightly divide that Book, or you will find yourself in one flat-footed mess.

14 But when ye shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not, (let him that readeth understand,) then let them that be in Judaea flee to the mountains:

15 And let him that is on the housetop not go down into the house, neither enter therein, to take any thing out of his house:

16 And let him that is in the field not turn back again for to take up his garment.

17 But woe to them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!

18 And pray ye that your flight be not in the winter.

This passage settles the setting of the Olivet Discourse once and for all. It is aimed at someone in [Judea,]{.class_3091} in the land of [Israel.]{.class_3091} There is a [rebuilt temple,]{.class_3091} where sacrifices are being made. Someone is coming into a [Jewish temple,]{.class_3091} stopping the [sacrifices,]{.class_3091} and then setting up an abomination there that causes any orthodox [Jew]{.class_3091} to forsake the place and leave it desolate. According to Revelation 11:1--2 (written twenty years after the destruction of Jerusalem), that still awaits [future]{.class_3091} fulfillment (cf. Dan. 9:13); it [can't ]{.class_3091}be the Church Age. As I have told you time and time again, the Olivet Discourse has [no doctrinal application]{.class_3091} to the New Testament believer. Christ is speaking to [Jews in the Tribulation.]{.class_3091}

Now, a proper discussion of ["the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet"]{.class_3054} (vs. 14) should be reserved []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_349}for our commentary on Daniel, and we refer you to our remarks on Daniel 8:13, 9:27, 11:31, and 12:11 in that work. Suffice it to say, here, that it has nothing to do with Antiochus Epiphanes slaughtering a pig in the Temple during the times of the Maccabees or the Romans under Titus offering sacrifices to Jupiter. Whatever type and foreshadowing those events might have been, the real thing will take place in the Tribulation under the Antichrist. Briefly, here is what is going on in verse 14.

1\. A Jewish temple, on the temple mount in Jerusalem, will be rebuilt.

2\. Sacrifices by a Jewish priesthood will begin once more. As of 2016, the Temple Institute in Jerusalem has all the vessels of service and the priests' garments made and ready to go, and Levites have been identified and are being trained in the priesthood.

3\. The Antichrist will confirm a covenant with the Jews for one prophetic week (seven years)---Daniel 9:27. After the first three-and-a-half years, he will break that covenant causing the daily sacrifice to cease.

4\. He will enter into the temple, professing to be God in the flesh, and install himself as the object of worship (2 Thess. 2:3--4).

5\. This worship will involve a living image of himself (Rev. 13:15) and human sacrifice, with the flesh and blood being eaten and drunk by his priests in a "Mass" (Rev. 6:9, 20:4; Isa. 6:13; Psa. 14:4, 16:4).

["Then let them that be in Judaea flee to the mountains."]{.class_3054} That's Israel fleeing ["into the wilderness"]{.class_3054} to escape the Devil (Rev. 12:6). They're fleeing to the mountains in Carmel in the south, Ephraim in the north, and Bashan and Gilead on the east side of Jordan, where God feeds them miraculously like He did back there in Exodus and Numbers (Rev. 12:6; Mic. 7:14--16; Jer. 50:19--20).

Verses 15--16 are pretty straight forward, given what we read above about the Antichrist's plans. If any of those Jews won't take the mark and don't want their heads cut off as a sacrifice to the Antichrist, they had better get out of town the minute that image goes up. They hadn't better try to get back in the house to get their clothes or food or anything else; just [run.]{.class_3055}

The women with babies are going to be especially in a bad []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_350}situation at that time. They won't have enough to eat, which means their milk will dry up if they're breastfeeding. Jeremiah said of the siege of Jerusalem by Nebuchadnezzar:

["The tongue of the sucking child cleaveth to the roof of his mouth for thirst: the young children ask bread, and no man breaketh it unto them"]{.class_3054} (Lam. 4:4).

God told those mommas some of them were going to eat their babies in the siege, and that is what happened in 2 Kings 6:28--29; Lamentations 2:20; and in the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70. Jesus said, ["the days are coming, in the which they shall say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bare, and the paps which never gave suck"]{.class_3054} (Luke 23:29). Why? Because the women wouldn't have to worry about their babies starving to death, or being tempted to eat them when they themselves were starving to death.

The only man in that Bible ever commanded not to marry was [Jeremiah]{.class_3055} (Jer. 16:2). The reason was, Nebuchadnezzar (a type of the Antichrist) was going to come in and kill the mommas and daddies with their children. If they didn't die by famine in the siege, then the sword would get them (Jer. 16:3--4). That's what's going to happen to those Jews in the Tribulation.

By the way, did you notice the context of Luke 23:29, two paragraphs up? It's Revelation 6:16 (cf. Luke 23:30)---the Tribulation. There's no way it can be the destruction of Jerusalem under Titus. Preterists like John Bray are just as crazy as a bed bug.

Next, if you want to get an idea about what Jesus was talking, study the treatment of Jewish babies and children by the Nazis. Children were targeted for death, over a million of them being slaughtered. Babies were separated from their mothers and killed immediately; some by drowning, others left alone until they died. Mothers were humiliated by the guards to join their children in the gas chambers or threatened with death themselves if they didn't burn the bodies of their own children. Children were burned alive, poisoned, strangled, and experimented on. That is the kind of stuff a Jewish momma can expect in the Tribulation. That is not said as a threat or as some sadistic thrill; it is a [warning:]{.class_3055}[ "woe to them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days."]{.class_3054} The only way to escape ["those days"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_351} is to trust Christ NOW (2 Cor. 6:2). If you don't, momma, you will be faced with the choice of your baby starving to death, or you taking the mark so you can buy food and being damned in the process (Rev. 14:9--11).

Verse 18: ["And pray ye that your flight be not in the winter."]{.class_3054} The ["flight"]{.class_3054} is, of course, the ["flee to the mountains"]{.class_3054} of verse 14. But one should not take the Amillennialists too seriously when they mock prophetic expositors for pointing out that the Jews should pray their flight shouldn't be ["on the sabbath day"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 24:20) because flights are grounded in Israel on Saturday in observance of the Jewish Sabbath. You see, that ["flight"]{.class_3054} over in Revelation 12:14 is [literal flying]{.class_3055}---READ IT!

Now although winter temperatures are mild in Palestine, ranging from 43 to 59 degrees Fahrenheit, as recently as 2015 Israel experienced temperatures as low as seven degrees, with 31 inches of snow up in the Golan Heights. Since the ["flight"]{.class_3054} will be ["to the mountains,"]{.class_3054} those Jews are to pray it won't take place in the Winter when the temperatures will be much colder at higher elevations. Those Jews will have nothing but the clothes on their backs, and little to no food, and even temperatures in the low 40s can take a toll on health under such circumstances.

And as I pointed out two paragraphs above, Matthew adds, ["neither on the sabbath day."]{.class_3054} What's the problem? The problem is, contrary to the Fundamentalist misinterpretation of Colossians 2:16, the Jewish Sabbath is coming back (Col. 2:17). The Sabbath is not binding [now]{.class_3055} on the [Body of Christ.]{.class_3055} But in the Tribulation, the Body of Christ is [in Heaven]{.class_3055} (Rev. 5:9). For the Jewish saints down here on earth, [the commandments are back in force]{.class_3055} (Rev. 12:17, 14:12, 22:14), which means those Jews are to ["Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy"]{.class_3054} (Exod. 20:8), and ["do no work therein"]{.class_3054} (Lev. 23:3). That includes not traveling over ["a sabbath day's journey"]{.class_3054} (Acts 1:12), which is about nine-tenths of a mile (Luke 24:50 cf. John 11:18). That's another reason you know the Olivet Discourse is not doctrinally for any born-again child of God in the Body of Christ. You don't have to worry about the fourth commandment, because the Sabbath was a sign to [Israel]{.class_3055} (Ezek. 20:12, 20), [not the Church]{.class_3055} (Col. 2:16). I dare say 99% of the []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_352}Christians reading this page travel over a mile to go to church on Sunday. You think you have to worry about not fleeing anywhere on the Sabbath?

19 For in those days shall be affliction, such as was not from the beginning of the creation which God created unto this time, neither shall be.

20 And except that the Lord had shortened those days, no flesh should be saved: but for the elect's sake, whom he hath chosen, he hath shortened the days.

The ["affliction"]{.class_3054} of verse 19 is the Great Tribulation (Matt. 24:21). It is ["the time of Jacob's trouble"]{.class_3054} (Jer. 30:7 cf. Dan. 12:11), [not the Church's.]{.class_3055} The Christian will certainly experience ["tribulation"]{.class_3054} in this world (John 16:33; Rom. 5:3, 12:12; 2 Cor. 1:4, 7:4; 1 Thess. 3:4; Rev. 1:9), but it is certainly [not]{.class_3055} the Great Tribulation that God recompenses to a lost world for rejecting His Son (2 Thess. 1:6--8).

Now there are a couple of things of importance in verse 19. The first is ["the beginning of the creation which God created,"]{.class_3054} and for that, we refer you to our remarks under 10:16. Jesus Christ was a [creationist,]{.class_3055} not a "theistic" evolutionist like John Paul II, Benedict XVI, and "Francis."

Next, notice the [severity]{.class_3055} of the Tribulation: ["such as was not from the beginning of the creation . . . unto this time."]{.class_3054}[ ]{.class_3079}Matthew adds, ["no, nor ever shall be"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 24:21). Then it can't be the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70, for there were far more atrocious acts than those carried out by the Romans under Titus' command. Just read Jeremiah 52 and Lamentations to see what Nebuchadnezzar did in his conquest of Jerusalem. The Moslem tyrant Tamerlane would behead every man, woman, and child in the cities he conquered (anywhere from 70,000--100,000 at a time), and then he would build towers out of the skulls. Being the good Moslem he was, he would sell any of the women who survived into sexual slavery to his soldiers.

Pol Pot killed over two million Cambodians between 1975 and 1979. He would have villagers buried alive or buried up to their []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_353}necks and beaten to death. Old "Dracula" (Vlad the Impaler) would have them impaled alive, 30,000 at a time, after burning their city to the ground. Basil II (A.D. 957--1025) would put out the eyes of the Bulgars he conquered, one time as many as 14,000 men. So what the Romans did to the Jews in A.D. 70 was not the worst things from Genesis 1 to now.

Second, think of the great Flood. God Almighty drowned out the entire population of the earth, except those on the ark. Could anything the Romans did compare to that? You see, people don't take the Bible seriously. They read of the terrible things that went on in A.D. 70 and think, "How awful!" In their attempt to take over the vineyard (12:1--11), they make those horrible things the fulfillment of Revelation. Nothing in the Olivet Discourse or in Revelation 6--19 was fulfilled literally in A.D. 70. It will be in the Tribulation, and when it is, God compares it to the state of the earth in Genesis 1:2.

["I beheld the earth, and, lo, it was without form, and void; and the heavens, and they had no light"]{.class_3054} (Jer. 4:23).

There have been several movies (both good and bad) about the Tribulation, but nothing made by Christians or Hollywood will ever be able to sufficiently portray how devastating the judgments of God on this earth will be. Like I told you back under my comments on verse 7, you can expect a [minimum]{.class_3055} of four billion people killed off. Best estimates only come out to a little over 300 million people on earth before the Flood. So when it comes to the sheer numbers that die, there are better than ten times killed in the Tribulation.

The shortening of the days in verse 20 refers to the fact that in the Tribulation, the rotation of the earth is sped up so that the length of a day is less than 24 hours. Isaiah 24:20 says, ["The earth shall reel to and fro like a drunkard,"]{.class_3054} so much so that ["the moon shall be confounded, and the sun ashamed"]{.class_3054} (Isa. 24:23). Amos 8:9 says God ["will cause the sun to go down at noon."]{.class_3054} We will comment further on this astronomical phenomena in verses 24--25 here in Mark 13.

Back to Mark 13:20---["but for the elect's sake, whom he hath chosen."]{.class_3054} The Amillennialists and Preterists will latch onto the term [elect]{.class_3055} to apply the passage to A.D. 70. This application ignores the context as well as the use of the word throughout Scripture. The word means ["chosen,"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_354} as is evident from verse 20. But it [never]{.class_3055} means God [arbitrarily]{.class_3055} choosing anybody or anything. God's election is always [conditional]{.class_3055} (1 Pet. 1:2). You may deposit the second petal of Calvin's TULIP in the compost pile where it belongs.

The term [elect]{.class_3055} can refer to [angels]{.class_3055} (1 Tim. 5:21), [Israel]{.class_3055} (Isa. 45:4), [Jesus Christ]{.class_3055} (Isa. 42:1; 1 Pet. 2:6), and [the born-again child of God]{.class_3055} (Rom. 8:33; Col. 3:12; 2 Tim. 2:10; Tit. 1:1). Given the [context]{.class_3055} of Mark 13, to which of those do you think Jesus is referring? Well, it's not Himself; He's not in danger of being seduced (vs. 22). It's not the angels; they're the ones being sent to gather the elect in verse 27. And I'll tell you something else, [it's not anyone in the Body of Christ.]{.class_3055} There are no "Christians" in Mark 13; "Christians" don't even show up until Acts 11:26. Whoever these ["elect"]{.class_3054} are, they are living in Judea and worshiping at a temple and seeing an ["abomination"]{.class_3054} that awaits future fulfillment (see comments on vs. 14). These are the elect remnant of Israel saved at the return of Christ (Rom. 11:25--28).

21 And then if any man shall say to you, Lo, here is Christ; or, lo, he is there; believe him not:

22 For false Christs and false prophets shall rise, and shall shew signs and wonders, to seduce, if it were possible, even the elect.

23 But take ye heed: behold, I have foretold you all things.

We have commented extensively on verses 21--22 in our remarks on verse 6. If we apply verse 21[ practically]{.class_3055} to a Christian today, we may say that no Christian need be deceived by the likes of Daddy Grace, Reverend Ike, Father Divine, or any Pope in the last 2,000 years (every one of them professed to be the "Vicar"---the [substitute]{.class_3055}---of Christ) if he [studies]{.class_3055} that Book and [rightly divides]{.class_3055} it (2 Tim. 2:15). The only way Mary Baker Eddy, Ellen G. White, Joseph Smith, Charles Taze Russell, Brigham Young, Judge Rutherford, or Mohammed could fool a "Christian" is if that "Christian" had put down that Book and picked up the material by the Watchtower Bible and Tract Society (JWs), Review and Herald Publishing Association []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_355}and the Pacific Press Publishing Association (Seventh-day Adventist), the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Day Saints (Mormons), the Christian Science Publishing Society, the [Koran]{.class_3055} and the [Hadith]{.class_3055} (Islam), or Ascension Press, Ave Maria Press, Our Sunday Visitor, etc. (Catholic).

No Christian who spends time in that Book is looking for his Saviour in a cloud formation or a tortilla shell or a seance or a "vision," etc. He is waiting for the Son of God to come from Heaven ["with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God"]{.class_3054} (1 Thess. 1:10, 4:16). Until then, we don't need ["Jesus Calling"]{.class_3055} or [ex cathedra]{.class_3055} statements or anybody's ["Key to the Scriptures,"]{.class_3055} "another testament of Jesus," or any other extra-biblical nonsense. We have the Book the Holy Spirit gave us by inspiration and which He preserved perfect for us in our own language ([AV]{.class_3055} 1611). We are [in Christ]{.class_3055} (2 Cor. 5:17), Christ is [in us]{.class_3055} (Col. 1:27), and if we want to know Him better, we let [His word]{.class_3055} dwell in us ["richly in all wisdom"]{.class_3054} (Col. 3:16) by [searching]{.class_3055} it (John 5:39) and [studying]{.class_3055} it (2 Tim. 2:15) for the [truth]{.class_3055} (John 17:17) about [Him]{.class_3055} (Rom. 1:1; 2 Tim. 3:15). The Christ we follow and worship is not one of our own making and imagination ("WWJD"); it is the Jesus Christ found in that Book and nowhere else.

Now the standard Fundamentalist-Calvinist way of handling verse 22 is to point out the conditional word ["if"]{.class_3054}---["IF it were possible," "even the elect"]{.class_3054} would be seduced by the ["false Christs and false prophets."]{.class_3054} But being "the elect" who are guaranteed to "persevere in the faith," it is [not]{.class_3055} possible, so they don't have to worry about it.

If that be the case, what are all the warnings in the New Testament about false Christs, false prophets, and false teachers? Why, Christ warns the disciples right here in the passage (vs. 23). Think about it, if it's not possible to be seduced and deceived by them (Matt. 24:24), then it's NOT POSSIBLE; [it ain't gonna happen.]{.class_3055} It is ironic that "discernment ministries," that teach "the elect" won't be deceived by false Christs, false prophets, and false teachers, spend so much time warning their listeners about them. I guess their readers and listeners must not be "the elect."

Now the fact of the matter is, no matter whether "the elect" are []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_356}born-again Christians in the Body of Christ or Tribulation Jewish saints, it [is possible]{.class_3055} for them to be deceived and seduced; otherwise, why would the Holy Spirit tell you [three times]{.class_3055} in Scripture: ["Let no man deceive you"]{.class_3054} (Eph. 5:6; 2 Thess. 2:3; 1 John 3:7)? Paul warned about ["grievous wolves"]{.class_3054} entering in and ["not sparing the flock"]{.class_3054} (Acts 20:29). Would the wolves only get the "non-elect" in the flock? Somebody ["bewitched"]{.class_3054} those Galatian Christians who had received the Holy Spirit (Gal. 3:1--2). You reckon they were "non-elect"?

The difference between a saint this side of the Rapture and a saint in the Tribulation is that a saved member of Christ's Body can't lose his salvation if he is seduced and deceived. The Tribulation "am sumptin' else."

In the Tribulation, God has some ["servants"]{.class_3054} in a local church who are being seduced by a ["prophetess" "to commit fornication, and to eat things sacrificed unto idols"]{.class_3054} (Rev. 2:20). Those are [servants of God]{.class_3055} being seduced by a false prophet who's a woman; they aren't any "non-elect." John writes to someone on whom the seducers are working, and he puts a [condition]{.class_3055} on receiving eternal life just like you read back in Hebrews 3:6, 14.

["Let that therefore abide in you, which ye have heard from the beginning. IF that which ye have heard from the beginning shall remain in you, ye also shall continue in the Son, and in the Father. And this is the promise that he hath promised us, even eternal life. These things have I written unto you concerning them that seduce you"]{.class_3054} (1 John 2:24--26).

That's NOT your condition. You don't have to ["continue in the Son"]{.class_3054} to get eternal life. You get eternal life the moment you trust Christ (1 John 5:10--13), and the moment you trust Christ, the Holy Spirit places you in Christ's Body (1 Cor. 12:13) so you are bone of His bone and flesh of His flesh (Eph. 5:30). Someone has to ["continue"]{.class_3054} in Jesus Christ to get eternal life, but it's not you. It's someone who has to ["endure unto the end"]{.class_3054} to ["be saved"]{.class_3054} (vs. 13).

24 But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun shall []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_357}be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light,

25 And the stars of heaven shall fall, and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken.

26 And then shall they see the Son of man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.

27 And then shall he send his angels, and shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from the uttermost part of the earth to the uttermost part of heaven.

As the Tribulation draws to a close, something goes wrong with the solar system. This darkening of the light of the sun and moon shows up several places in the Prophets: Isaiah 13:10; Jeremiah 4:23, 28; Ezekiel 32:7; Joel 2:31; Amos 5:20; Zephaniah 1:15. Its fulfillment is given in Revelation 6:12, 16:10.

The Amillennialists and Preterists will go to Peter quoting Joel 2:30--31 in his Pentecost sermon (Acts 2:19--20) without ever telling you that [nothing]{.class_3055} Joel said took place in Acts 2---including the Holy Spirit being poured out on ["ALL flesh"]{.class_3054} (Acts 2:17). The only people to receive the Holy Spirit in Acts 2 were the disciples of Jesus and those Jews who repented of crucifying their Messiah and got baptized as a testimony to that (Acts 2:36--38). All Peter was saying was, "Here's what Joel said" (Acts 2:16) in order to draw a broad analogy to the coming of the Holy Ghost. The Holy Spirit isn't poured out on ["all flesh"]{.class_3054} until the Second Advent (see our comments on Zech. 12:10 in that Commentary).

Verse 25: ["And the stars of heaven shall fall."]{.class_3054} Absolutely impossible if the reference is to the giant balls of flaming gases like our sun. Christ is not talking about Sirius or Arcturus or Betelgeuse or Alpha Centauri or Rigel or Polaris, etc. Any one of those stars would burn up the earth if it got within a million miles.

Well, if He's not talking about literal stars, what are they? A star falls from heaven in Revelation 9:1--2, and it's a [person]{.class_3055} (["him"]{.class_3054}---Rev. 9:1; ["he"]{.class_3054}---Rev. 9:2). In Revelation 1:20, there are ["seven stars"]{.class_3054} in Christ's hand, and they are defined as ["the ANGELS of the seven churches."]{.class_3054}

When those wise men show up in Jerusalem seeking the Christ child, they say, ["Where is he that is born King of the Jews? for we have seen his STAR in the east."]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_358} Where did they get that? From Numbers 24:17---["there shall come a Star out of Jacob, and a Sceptre shall rise out of Israel."]{.class_3054} That ["Star"]{.class_3054} is a [person, ]{.class_3055}and that person is the [King]{.class_3055} (["Sceptre"]{.class_3054}). It's the Son of God as ["the bright and morning star"]{.class_3054} (Rev. 22:16). So in the Old Testament, the [created]{.class_3055}[ "sons of God"]{.class_3054} (the angels), as a group, are called ["the morning stars"]{.class_3054} (Job 38:7).

In the Tribulation, a third of the angels are cast down to earth by the Devil (Rev. 12:4; Dan. 8:10). ["The powers that are in heaven"]{.class_3054} are the ["powers"]{.class_3054} in Ephesians 6:12 that are associated with ["spiritual wickedness in high places."]{.class_3054} Those ["high places"]{.class_3054} are not the high places of the "power brokers" of this world; they are the high places [above your head.]{.class_3055} So at the Advent, Christ comes to ["punish the host of the high ones that are ON HIGH, and the kings of the earth UPON THE EARTH"]{.class_3054} (Isa. 24:21).

Back to Mark 13. Notice in verse 26 that Jesus uses His designation as ["the Son of man"]{.class_3054} in reference to His Advent. That is the title of the Jewish King come to reign over an earthly, visible, physical, [Jewish]{.class_3055} kingdom (see Matt. 19:28). It is never used in any of the Pauline Epistles to the Gentile churches. Paul refers to Jesus as the [Son of God.]{.class_3055} That's because the New Testament believer isn't looking for "the King is coming"; he is looking for the Son of God to come and get him out of this mess (1 Thess. 1:10, 5:9).

["The clouds"]{.class_3054} of verse 26 aren't the nimbus, stratus, cirrus, and cumulus clouds up in the first heaven. They are the clouds of [God's glory.]{.class_3055} When the Lord came down on Mount Sinai back in Exodus 19:9, He came ["in a thick cloud."]{.class_3054} When Moses set up the Tabernacle, ["the glory of the LORD"]{.class_3054} covered it in a cloud (Exod. 40:34--35); ditto at the dedication of the Temple (1 Kings 8:10--11; 2 Chron. 5:13--14). Remember that cloud that came down on the Mount of Transfiguration back in Mark 9:7? Jesus leaves the earth in a cloud (Acts 1:9), and that's how He'll come back (Acts 1:11 cf. Rev. 1:7). Notice that when Jesus Christ appears to gather His harvest into His barn (Matt. 13:30) that He appears sitting on a cloud (Rev. 14:14--16).

Verse 27 has nothing to do with the Rapture of the Church [before]{.class_3055} []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_359}the Tribulation (see our comments on Rev. 4:1 in that Commentary). Just as in verses 20 and 22, the ["elect"]{.class_3054} of verse 27 are [Jews.]{.class_3055} This is a [post-Tribulation rapture of Jewish saints,]{.class_3055} and you can find a thorough discussion of the subject in our work [The Two Raptures.]{.class_3055}

["The four winds"]{.class_3054} are associated with ["the four corners of the earth"]{.class_3054} (Rev. 7:1). That's a common expression for the four main directions: north, south, east, and west. The Bible is written in plain language that anybody, anywhere, can understand; not the jargon of intellectual stuff-shirts educated beyond their intelligence. You understand what Jesus is saying. From the furthest reaches of the land (["uttermost part of the earth"]{.class_3054}), from horizon to horizon (["uttermost part of heaven"]{.class_3054}), the angels will gather the Jews who are saved at that time in a rapture that takes place at the end of the Tribulation, at some point before the Advent. How long the time between this rapture of Tribulation Jewish saints and Christ's return is not clearly stated in Scripture. There is a "flexibility" of time in regards to when the Tribulation is finished and the Advent takes place (see our comments on Matt. 24:22 in that Commentary).

28 Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When her branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is near:

29 So ye in like manner, when ye shall see these things come to pass, know that it is nigh, even at the doors.

30 Verily I say unto you, that this generation shall not pass, till all these things be done.

We have already discussed ["the fig tree"]{.class_3054} of verse 28 back under 11:13 (which see). It represents self-righteous Israel having a profession of fruit without possession. Christ cursed it back in 11:14 so that it ["dried up from the roots"]{.class_3054} (11:20) and couldn't bear fruit (11:14).

But here in 13:28, ["the fig tree"]{.class_3054} has come back to life: ["her branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves."]{.class_3054} There is still no fruit, but the reappearance of life means that fruit is on its way: ["ye know that summer is near."]{.class_3054} In Song of Solomon 2, there is a rapture in the Spring, and when it takes place, ["the fig tree putteth forth her green figs"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_360} (Song of Sol. 2:13). So when the Church is called out of this earth at the Rapture, Israel will once more be a nation and will be producing fruit as a [nation,]{.class_3055} not just as a dispersed people. So ["the fig tree"]{.class_3054} putting forth ["branches"]{.class_3054} and ["leaves"]{.class_3054} is Israel being restored as a nation in preparation for Christ's return. That took place on May 14, 1948.

Immediately apostate scholarship steps forward to foul up the works. Expositors like J. Vernon McGee will say that the return of Israel to the land in the Twentieth Century is [not]{.class_3055} the restoration of Israel prophesied in the Old Testament because the vast majority of Israelis are [lost]{.class_3055}---either adherents to Rabbinical Judaism or outright agnostics and atheists. The problem with this view is that only [two]{.class_3055} restorations are prophesied in Scripture (Isa. 11:11), not three. The first time Israel returned to the land in Ezra and Nehemiah, they returned in [unbelief]{.class_3055}, as is evidenced in Ezra 9--10 and Nehemiah 13. The second time they return, they return in [unbelief,]{.class_3055} and this is evidenced by the fact that ["all Israel shall be saved"]{.class_3054} after ["the fulness of the Gentiles be come in"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 11:25, cf. Luke 21:24), at the [Advent]{.class_3055} (Rom. 11:26; Zech. 12:10).

Now Christ draws an analogy between seeing the fig tree bud and seeing everything about which He's told the disciples. When they see the ["wars and rumours of wars,"]{.class_3054} the false Christs and prophets, the earthquakes, the famines, the ["gospel of the kingdom"]{.class_3054} being preached to all nations, the wholesale persecution of believers, ["the abomination of desolation"]{.class_3054} being set up in the temple, etc., they can know the coming of the Lord ["is nigh, even at the doors"]{.class_3054} (vs. 29). That includes the budding of the fig tree---Israel restored as a nation---in verse 28.

Now look at verse 30: ["this generation shall not pass, till all these things be done."]{.class_3054} There is the monkey wrench in the machinery. It wouldn't be so bad if He were just talking about everything from verses 5--25, but He includes the generation that saw Israel become a nation again. That means someone from 1948 who saw that will still be alive when Christ returns. Well, you are now (as of 2016) 68 years past that event. That's getting up there in years, you have to admit.

So the question arises, what is a ["generation"]{.class_3054} in Scripture? Well, in the Bible, it can be [33 years]{.class_3055}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_361}---the length of Christ's life (Isa. 53:8). That would have put the Advent at 1981, so it's not that. Calculating a generation according to Matthew 1:17 would make it [42 years.]{.class_3055} The Advent would have been in 1990, according to that number, so it's not that either. Moses makes it [seventy years]{.class_3055} in Psalm 90:10, which would put the date of the Advent in 2018. But even if you allow for only the last half of Daniel's Seventieth Week to be fulfilled (3½ years), that means the Rapture should have taken place between 2014 and 2015, so that's out. If you take the [eighty years]{.class_3055} of Psalm 90:10, that could work because the Advent would be in 2028; so that is a possibility. But there is also the generation of a [hundred years,]{.class_3055} found in Genesis 15:13, 16. That gives you an Advent of [no later]{.class_3055} than 2048. Of course, it could take place before then, and I sure hope it does. That means the Rapture isn't that far away. At 94, with failing health, I might not make it this side of a hole in the ground, but some of you reading this [will.]{.class_3055}

And then a great verse:

31 Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.

Did you get that? He didn't say the thoughts or the principles or the fundamentals or even the "Word" (or "word"). He said His ["W-O-R-D-S."]{.class_3054} Now do you have them or not?

I have kicked the slats out from under the apostates for so long that they finally had to make a profession of belief in that Book without believing anything of the kind. They say, "We believe the [King James]{.class_3055} Bible is the preserved Word of God." They use the designation of the Neo-orthodox (Word) so as to get around believing the individual [words]{.class_3055} are ["given by inspiration"]{.class_3054} (2 Tim. 3:16) and preserved. But the promise isn't that God would preserve some nebulous message or the "thoughts of Scripture" (that's how one apostate at Tennessee Temple put it) or even the "text." The promise was He would preserve His W-O-R-D-S:[ "The WORDS of the LORD are pure WORDS . . . Thou shalt keep THEM, O LORD, thou shalt preserve THEM from this generation for ever"]{.class_3054} (Psa. 12:6--7). The ["word"]{.class_3054} God ["magnified above all ]{.class_3054}\[His\][ name"]{.class_3054} (Psa. 138:2) is composed of ["WORDS"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_362} (Psa. 138:4).

Now one of these days, everything you see around you will burn up. Do you live in a trailer? It is going to burn. Do you live in a mansion? It is going to burn. All those big buildings downtown---they're going to burn. All the slums, ghettos, and the "housing projects"---they'll be nothing but a pile of ashes. The cars, the malls, the restaurants, the jets, the trees, the grass, the deserts, the ice caps, the oceans, the rivers, the stars, the planets, the sun, the moon---THEY'RE ALL GOING UP IN FLAMES.

["But the day of the Lord will come as a thief in the night; in the which the heavens shall pass away with a great noise, and the elements shall melt with fervent heat, the earth also and the works that are therein shall be burned up"]{.class_3054} (2 Pet. 3:10).

Voltaire said that in a hundred years the Bible would only be found in a museum. Voltaire died back in 1778, and the Book celebrated its 400th anniversary in 2011 after having gone out in more than a billion copies. In fact, after his death, Voltaire's house was turned into a headquarters for the Geneva Bible Society.

In 1891, the infidel Bob Ingersoll bragged that in ten years the Bible would be a dead book. Ingersoll died eight years later (1899), and the Book went right on living (Heb. 4:12).

Back around 1920 somewhere, Professor Albert Pinkevich, the head of education in the USSR, said, "The world is getting too small for that Book. Either that Book must go, or the world must go." Aaamen! Preach it, buddy! You're exactly right, even if you are a Communist. One or the other will have to go, and I can tell you which one it will be---[the world]{.class_3055} (1 John 2:17).

"When we've been there ten thousand years bright shining as the sun," long after you've forgotten Pensacola, Florida, cockroaches, the minimum wage job, the water bill, the electric bill, the rolling pile of junk that passed for an automobile, and the whole cotton-picking mess, you'll go by the throne there in the New Jerusalem and see that Book and remember verses you learned back there in Sunday school, vacation Bible school, or for Bible class. ["The grass withereth and the flower thereof falleth away: But the word of the Lord endureth for ever"]{.class_3054} (1 Pet. 1:24--25).

32 But of that day and that hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels which are in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.

That was Jesus speaking as a [man.]{.class_3055} While here on earth, Christ didn't always exercise His Deity, as we saw back in 11:13. Of course, one of the reasons He couldn't say for sure [right then]{.class_3055} when He was coming back was that He was waiting to see what the Jews would do there in the early part of the book of Acts. As I have told you many times throughout this Commentary, Christ was standing up there in Acts 7:5--6 in preparation for His return [if the Sanhedrin had accepted the message of Stephen.]{.class_3055} They didn't, so the Second Advent was put off for 2,000 years.

That's what blows a Calvinist's mind. God works that thing out so that man can have whatever way he chooses and the Lord still has His "sovereign will" in the matter. Two-thousand years don't put any "kink" in God's plan. It was the Jews who got the "short end of the stick" in that deal, and it was their own fault.

Now the Fundamentalists and Conservatives mess up the passage in two ways. First, they apply it to the Rapture of the Church, which most certainly is [not]{.class_3055} the case. The whole passage is about the [Second Advent.]{.class_3055} When it came to "date setting" for the Rapture, Paul believed it would be [in his day]{.class_3055} (1 Thess. 4:17). The idea that you can't [guess]{.class_3055} at when the Rapture will take place based on what the Scriptures say (and that is all I have ever done) is ludicrous.

Moreover, the verse only says you can't know the ["day"]{.class_3054} and ["hour."]{.class_3054} Paul said you could ["know perfectly" "the times and the seasons"]{.class_3054} (1 Thess. 5:1--2). The context of 1 Thessalonians 5:1 compares the Second Coming to a woman in labor (1 Thess. 5:3). Any husband who can't figure out the time and season his wife will give birth is completely oblivious to her condition. Unlike the disciples in Mark 13, you now have the [completed revelation]{.class_3055} of the Bible. You can read 1 Timothy 4; 2 Timothy 3; and the book of Revelation and get a good idea of when Christ is coming back.

33 Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is.

34 For the Son of man is as a man taking a far journey, who left his house, and gave authority to his servants, and to every man his work, and commanded the porter to watch.

35 Watch ye therefore: for ye know not when the master of the house cometh, at even, or at midnight, or at the cockcrowing, or in the morning:

36 Lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping.

37 And what I say unto you I say unto all, Watch.

Now we come to the practical application of the Olivet Discourse. Since the disciples didn't know the day or hour of Christ's return, they were to pay attention to what He said (["Take ye heed"]{.class_3054}---vs. 33) and ["watch and pray." "Watch"]{.class_3054} for what? Watch for ["the master of the house"]{.class_3054} to come back (vss. 34--36). ["Pray"]{.class_3054} for what? Luke 21:36.

"Watch ye therefore, and pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that shall come to pass, and to stand before the Son of man."

That is one of those passages the Pentecostals use to teach a split Rapture. Do you have to pray to ["be accounted worthy to escape"]{.class_3054} the Tribulation and ["to stand before the Son of man"]{.class_3054}? Well, first of all, you aren't looking for ["the Son of man"]{.class_3054}; you are looking for the [Son of God]{.class_3055} (see our comments on vs. 26). Second, your going up in the Rapture isn't conditioned on your "worth"; it's conditioned on your faith in Christ and your position [in Him]{.class_3055} (1 Thess. 4:14--17). If you are saved, you are [going up]{.class_3055}, no matter how you've been living.

In the Tribulation, though, that Jew is to watch and keep ["his garments"]{.class_3054} (Rev. 16:15), and if he doesn't, he doesn't go up when the Lord comes at that post-Tribulation rapture (Matt. 25:10--13). In fact, the ["servants"]{.class_3054} of verse 34 can end up [going to Hell]{.class_3055} if they abuse their ["authority"]{.class_3054} (Luke 12:45--46) or don't use it for the profit of their Lord (Matt. 25:26--30).

You can know for sure that the passage has nothing to do with the Rapture of the Bride of Christ because when ["the Son of man" ]{.class_3054}takes that ["far journey"]{.class_3054} in verse 34, it is for the purpose of a [wedding]{.class_3055}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_365} (Luke 12:36). The servants He leaves behind aren't waiting to [marry]{.class_3055} Him (cf. Matt. 25:6); they are waiting to be [rewarded]{.class_3055} by Him for being faithful (Luke 12:37; Matt. 25:19--23). Contrary to the Pentecostal position that being a part of the Bride of Christ is a [reward,]{.class_3055} it is rather a [position.]{.class_3055} If you have been saved, you have been put by the Holy Spirit into the Body of Christ (1 Cor. 12:13), and if you are in Christ's Body, you are a part of His Bride (Eph. 5:30--32). That's not true of the servants or virgins of Matthew 25.

The ["house"]{.class_3054} of verse 34 is a reference to the nation of Israel---Christ's ["own house"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 3:6). The ["servants"]{.class_3054} are part of that house on a [condition]{.class_3055}---["IF we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm unto the end."]{.class_3054} This is confirmed by Matthew 25:29 and Luke 12:46, where some of the servants are thrown out of the house to suffer ["with the unbelievers"]{.class_3054} (Luke 12:46--48).

There is no clear indication of who ["the porter"]{.class_3054} of verse 34 is, but it is a reference to a watchman set over the city gate to sound the alarm in case of trouble (Ezek. 3:17, 33:2, 6--7). This watchman is questioned about ["the night"]{.class_3054} in Isaiah 21:11. That watchman was watching in the night, and he said, ["The morning cometh, and also the night"]{.class_3054} (Isa. 21:12). So he was watching in the night, but was awaiting the morning. But after that, another night was on the way.

What you have there is a prophetic layout of the Church Age, Rapture, Tribulation, and Advent. The Church Age is a nighttime (Rom. 13:12), awaiting a dawning (2 Pet. 1:19). But after that comes another nighttime---the Tribulation. The Second Coming of Christ at the Advent is likened to the sun coming up (Mal. 4:2) after a time of great darkness on the earth (Isa. 60:1--3).

We have already commented on verse 35 and its application to the Rapture of the Church under 6:48 (which see). In regards to its application to the Second Advent, the reason the disciples didn't know which watch Christ would return was because they did not have the complete revelation of Scripture yet (see our remarks under vs. 32); all they had was the Old Testament.

Of course, you can apply what you just read practically to your situation in the Church Age. Sure you can; after all, Jesus ends the discourse with: []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2196.xhtml_page_366}["And what I say unto you I say unto ALL, Watch"]{.class_3054} (vs. 37). The Christian should be careful not to "sleep on the job" (vs. 36). Paul tells the Christian ["to awake out of sleep: for now is our salvation nearer than when we believed"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 13:11)---that's a reference to your [body]{.class_3055} being saved at the Rapture. He says, ["Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light"]{.class_3054} (Eph. 5:14). That wasn't written to lost people; it was written to the [saved.]{.class_3055} The Christian shouldn't be "asleep at the wheel" because he's a child ["of the day,"]{.class_3054} not the night (1 Thess. 5:6--8).

The Christian should ["watch and pray"]{.class_3054} (vs. 33). He is to ["Continue in prayer, and watch in the same with thanksgiving"]{.class_3054} (Col. 4:2). In regards to the Rapture, he is to ["watch and be sober"]{.class_3054} (1 Thess. 5:6).

For what should he watch? He should watch his [words;]{.class_3055} he should watch his [actions;]{.class_3055} he should watch his [thoughts;]{.class_3055} he should watch his [conduct;]{.class_3055} he should watch his [heart.]{.class_3055} The Christian should be watching for His Lord's return (Tit. 2:13; Phil. 3:20). We are not to be ignorant of the coming of the Antichrist or the Tribulation, but that's not for what we are to be watching. We are watching for our [Saviour to come back]{.class_3055} and get us out of here (John 14:3).

I even preach to the lost to look for Christ's return. Why? To get ready for ["the master of the house"]{.class_3054} to come back. After all, it's [His house,]{.class_3055} isn't it? You reckon He'll take kindly to squatters making a wreck of it? Then those squatters had better "get on the payroll" and become ["servants to God"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 6:22). Like the little boy said when he learned about the Second Coming of Christ in Sunday school, "Jesus is coming back, and boy, is He [mad]{.class_3055}!"

[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml}

# [Chapter]{.class_3082}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_367}[ 14]{.class_3093} {.heading_2031}

14:1 After two days was the feast of the passover, and of unleavened bread: and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might take him by craft, and put him to death.

2 But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an uproar of the people.

["The feast of the passover, and of unleavened bread"]{.class_3057-0} are one and the same in this verse (see vs. 12), even though ["the feast of unleavened bread"]{.class_3057-0} technically started the day after Passover (Lev. 23:5--6). That's because the Passover was to be eaten with unleavened bread (Exod. 12:8), and the original Passover was included with the days when unleavened bread was to be eaten (Exod. 12:18).

Now why make such a big deal about this? Because in Luke 22:7, the Scriptures make a distinction between ["the DAY]{.class_3057-0} \[singular\][ of unleavened bread"]{.class_3057-0} and ["the DAYS]{.class_3057-0} \[plural\][ of unleavened bread"]{.class_3057-0} (Acts 12:3). The Scriptures may call the Passover ["the feast of unleavened bread"]{.class_3057-0} (Luke 22:1), but it never calls the Feast of Unleavened Bread the Passover. That is why, whether they knew it or not, the Holy Spirit led the translators of the [AV]{.class_3058-0} to retain the word ["Easter"]{.class_3057-0} in Acts 12:4, instead of changing it to "[Passover.]{.class_3058-0}" Passover, ["the DAY of unleavened bread,"]{.class_3057-0} had already come and gone, and the Jews were into ["the DAYS of unleavened bread"]{.class_3057-0} that followed. Nothing like a Bible to clear up a Seminary education, is there?

Notice, once again, the precise timing of the "Passion Week" given in verse 1. I showed you back under my comments on 11:11 that Mark gives you exactly what happens on Sunday, Monday, and Tuesday. To force a "Good Friday" Crucifixion on you, the scholars have to make the ["two days"]{.class_3057-0} of verse 1 two full 24-hour days, after denying the ["three days and three nights"]{.class_3057-0} of Matthew 12:40.

Measuring time as a Jew would, from 6 p.m. to 6 p.m. (see Gen. 1:5 and our comments in that Commentary), the statement of Mark []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_368}14:1 would be given before 6 p.m. on Tuesday. That would make the first day Wednesday, when the Passover lamb was killed (vs. 12) in preparation (John 19:14) for it being eaten after 6 p.m. Wednesday, when the Jewish Thursday began---the second day. Christ is in the tomb Wednesday night, Thursday day, Thursday night, Friday day, Friday night, and Saturday day---["three days and three nights."]{.class_3057-0} He comes out of the tomb Saturday night after 6 p.m., when the Jewish first day of the week began. "Good Friday" is nothing but Roman Catholic, pagan nonsense.

Now the religious leaders---["the chief priests and the scribes"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 1), along with ["the elders of the people"]{.class_3057-0} (Matt. 26:3)---put their heads together to come up with a way to take Jesus ["by craft"]{.class_3057-0}; that is, subtly (Matt. 26:4), craftily, without all the Jews who were in Jerusalem for the Passover knowing what they had done. Why did they want to hide it from the people? Because they were afraid of inciting a riot (vs. 2).

A lot of those Jews had seen Jesus heal the sick, give sight to the blind, cast out devils, raise the dead, cleanse the leper, and feed thousands of people miraculously; they were sure He was a prophet. If the Pharisees and Sadducees had tried taking Christ openly while He was teaching, a lot of those Jews would have [stoned them]{.class_3058-0} (cf. Luke 20:6). On the other hand, some of those Jews were ready to stone Christ because He claimed to be God in the flesh (John 10:30--33), so they would have taken sides with the Pharisees and Sadducees against Him. There would have been a couple of million Jews at each others' throats. That wouldn't have set very well with the Romans' ["Pax Romana"]{.class_3058-0} ("Peace of Rome"); Pilate would have had to send troops in to "knock some heads together." That's what those Jewish leaders were afraid of (John 11:48), so they were trying to "keep it on the down low."

3 And being in Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at meat, there came a woman having an alabaster box of ointment of spikenard very precious; and she brake the box, and poured it on his head.

4 And there were some that had indignation within themselves, and said, Why was this waste of the ointment []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_369}made?

5 For it might have been sold for more than three hundred pence, and have been given to the poor. And they murmured against her.

6 And Jesus said, Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me.

7 For ye have the poor with you always, and whensoever ye will ye may do them good: but me ye have not always.

8 She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying.

9 Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world, this also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.

The passage is a parenthetical insertion between verses 2 and 10, as is evident from what you are told in John 12:1. This incident actually takes place on what we would call Friday evening, the beginning of the Jewish Sabbath, before the Triumphal Entry on Sunday (cf. John 12:12--15).

The meal of verse 3 is a special supper for Jesus (John 12:3). While Mary, Martha, and Lazarus were present (John 12:2--3), the supper is not at their house. It is ["in the house of Simon the leper"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 3). Although we do not know who this Simon is, he is obviously one of the many lepers Jesus healed in His ministry.

The ["woman"]{.class_3057-0} of the passage is identified by John as Mary, the sister of Martha and Lazarus (John 12:3). ["Alabaster"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 3) is a stone that comes from Egypt. It looks like marble but is much softer. It was used for perfume bottles; in this case, a box. ["Spikenard"]{.class_3057-0} is a perfume derived from a plant which grows in the Himalaya mountains. In Bible times, it was ["very precious"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 3) because it was ["very costly"]{.class_3057-0} (John 12:3). This box of spikenard was valued at ten months' wages (["three hundred pence"]{.class_3057-0}---John 12:5; Mark puts it at ["more than"]{.class_3057-0} that---vs. 5). In today's economy, it could be worth no less than \$12,000. That shows you how much Mary valued the Lord: she gave the most costly thing she had to Him.

So Mary gave the [best]{.class_3058-0} she had. She gave it [sacrificially.]{.class_3058-0} ["She brake the box"]{.class_3057-0}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_370 .class-0}; there was no going back and saving any of it for another time. We have already commented on the necessity of a Christian being "broken" by the Lord, if He is going to use him, under our remarks on 8:6 (which see). That's what I call "the unhappy work of the Holy Spirit"; that is, if you are going to have revival as an individual or a church, the Holy Spirit is going to have to break some things.

Mary poured the ointment on Christ's head (vs. 3), and John 12:3 adds she ["anointed the feet of Jesus."]{.class_3057-0} So this was an anointing like that of the [high priest]{.class_3058-0} in the Old Testament (Exod. 29:7; Lev. 8:12) or like that of a [King]{.class_3058-0} (1 Sam. 10:1; 2 Kings 9:6). In this case, it is the anointing of a [Prophet,]{.class_3058-0} for she anoints Christ's body ahead of time for its burial (vs. 8).

So while Jesus is sitting at the table eating His supper, Mary comes with a box of spikenard in her hands that she must have saved up years to buy. Whatever the occasion for which she had been saving it, she now wants to use it in an act of love toward her Saviour. She breaks the box and pours out the perfume on Jesus' head and feet, filling the whole house with the aroma of the spikenard.

But not everyone at the dinner table appreciated what Mary had done to Jesus. ["There were some that had indignation"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 4): they were [angry ]{.class_3058-0}at Mary for apparently "wasting" this expensive perfume on the Lord. Mark says it was ["some,"]{.class_3057-0} Matthew narrows it down to Christ's ["disciples"]{.class_3057-0} (Matt. 26:8), but John gives you the name of the "ringleader" of malcontent: it was [Judas]{.class_3058-0} (John 12:4--5).

["Why was this WASTE of the ointment made?"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 4). Now there's a "Greek nugget" for you. It was [Judas,]{.class_3058-0} ["the son of perdition"]{.class_3057-0} (John 17:12), who asked the question. The Greek word for ["perdition"]{.class_3057-0} is [apoleia]{.class_3058-0}; it is the same word used for ["waste"]{.class_3057-0} here in Mark 14:4. It is related to the word ["Apollyon"]{.class_3057-0} in Revelation 9:11. In other words, any act of sacrificial love toward Jesus Christ is counted as [damnable waste]{.class_3058-0} by the Devil and his crowd.

Now what was Judas' "beef" with what Mary had done? ["For it might have been sold for more than three hundred pence, and have been given to the poor"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 5). "Oh, the poor, []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_371}oppressed minorities of the inner city!" "Oh, look at all these poor, diseased children traveling all the way from Central America on foot! You wouldn't dare turn them away." "How could you possibly turn away all these tens of thousands of [Moslem]{.class_3058-0} Syrian refugees (many of them young men between the ages of twenty and forty, the typical age of Moslem[ terrorists]{.class_3058-0}) who have been displaced from their homelands?" (All the while turning away the "Christian minorities," from the same place, who are being persecuted, beheaded, and raped by the Moslems.)

You had better look out for people---especially politicians---who are always worrying about the poor when they were never poor themselves. When you hear Democrats like Hillary Clinton and Obama talking about "free" health care and "free" College tuition and "free" food stamps, that's nothing but the government [stealing]{.class_3058-0} from the "haves" to buy the votes of the "have nots." They don't care about "the poor"; they only care about staying in office. Such Socialists don't give as much as 1% of their own income to charity to help out the poor, but they'll pass laws to take [your]{.class_3058-0} money from [you,]{.class_3058-0} by [force]{.class_3058-0} if necessary.

Judas wasn't concerned about[ "the poor"]{.class_3057-0} at all. He was complaining ["because he was a thief, and had the bag, and bare what was put therein"]{.class_3057-0} (John 12:6). He was stealing from "the kitty"; he was "skimming from the top"; he was [embezzling funds.]{.class_3058-0} A lot of charities spend less than 10% of the funds raised for their stated purpose. In other words, most of the money that goes to the cancer fund, the "police benevolent society," the wounded veterans, the "make-a-wish" fund, the burn clinic, the missing children, the "youth fund," etc., actually goes into [someone's pocket]{.class_3058-0} and not to those causes. Judas wanted whatever he could pilfer of the ["three hundred pence"]{.class_3057-0}; he didn't care one wit about ["the poor."]{.class_3057-0}

Verse 6 is clear. What Mary did wasn't a ["waste"]{.class_3057-0}; it was ["a good work"]{.class_3057-0} performed on Jesus Christ. Notice the application of ["the first commandment"]{.class_3057-0} (see 12:30 and our comments on the verse) in verse 7. Jesus is saying, "Never mind the poor folks; tend to me." That is quite an arrogant thing to say if He weren't God; some people today would even think it arrogant even of God to say a thing like []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_372}that. That is because they elevate the second commandment over the first.

Now you are in the situation about which Jesus talks at the end of the verse. He is no longer here on earth for you to do anything for Him personally. If you want to give something to Christ today that costs you money, you have to give to His [work.]{.class_3058-0}

["For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ"]{.class_3057-0} (Eph. 4:12).

Of course, that's talking about the [ministers]{.class_3058-0} Christ gave the Church when He ascended into Heaven, but the reasons are true of why you give. The money you give to the local church should be used to perfect the saints (discipleship, training), carry out Christian ministry (that can include everything from purchasing tracts and supporting missionaries to replacing the light bulbs and toilet paper), and edify the members (whatever is necessary to put out sound Bible preaching and teaching---the necessary microphones, sound equipment, recording equipment, literature if it is used, audio-visual equipment and supplies, etc.).

Of course, not all giving to the Lord is by money. Thanksgiving and praise are sacrifices we can make to the Lord (Heb. 13:15). When you put out the Gospel, you put out ["a sweet savour"]{.class_3057-0} to God just like the odor of that spikenard in the house (2 Cor. 2:14--15). ["The prayers of the saints"]{.class_3057-0} are an odor of sweet incense to God, as well (Rev. 5:8).

And then a great statement in verse 8: ["She hath done what she could."]{.class_3057-0} That's the inscription on Fanny Crosby's gravestone. Could someone put that on your gravestone? I'm not condemning anyone. If you've done what you could do for the Lord, OK. It doesn't matter if it's big or little as long as it is what you could do. That Bible says, ["it is accepted according to that a man hath, and not according to that he hath not"]{.class_3057-0} (2 Cor. 8:12). If you aren't able to do something or don't have a certain amount you can give, God doesn't hold it against you. What the Lord wants to know is, [does He have what you've got?]{.class_3096}

["She is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying."]{.class_3057-0} Then Mary got something none of the disciples got (see 8:31--32, 9:31--32 and our comments on those passages). She got []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_373}that by sitting at Jesus' feet and hearing His word (Luke 10:39)---and [believing it]{.class_3058-0}, I might add (see 1 Thess. 2:13).

Verse 9 is an enigmatic verse. So far in Mark, the ["gospel"]{.class_3057-0} has [not]{.class_3058-0} been the Gospel Paul preached (see our comments on 1:1, 14--15 and 13:10), but here, it is connected with Christ's death and burial, and in turn, with His resurrection (8:31, 9:31, 10:34). That was certainly no gospel preached during Christ's ministry. The only way you could think it was would be to put down that Bible and become a "godly Fundamentalist" or a "reverent Biblicist."

The verse is obviously a prophecy, but whether it is a prophecy about the Gospel in this age or the gospel of the kingdom is unclear. If it were [any]{.class_3058-0} gospel preached in the book of Acts (Peter, in Acts 2, certainly did NOT preach the Gospel Paul preached in 1 Cor. 15:1--4 or Gal. 3:14---read Acts 2:37--40), no one even brought up the incident. Paul never mentioned it in any of his epistles; nor did James, Peter, John, or Jude. I have preached on it; I know other preachers who have done so as well. But do you reckon it has been preached on by every saved minister who has ever stood behind a pulpit.

I suppose you could say that since it is recorded in Matthew, Mark, and John, that wherever the Scriptures with those portions in it are translated, distributed, and read, that that could fulfill Christ's words. Of course, there are places where the Gospel has been preached, but no Scriptures have yet been translated into the tongue in which it was preached, so that's a problem.

And of course, Christ could be referring to "the gospel" of Mark 13:10. But if that be the case, there is nothing in the book of Revelation to indicate that it is done. So until the Holy Spirit gives more light on the subject [doctrinally,]{.class_3058-0} we will leave the matter right there. Practically, devotionally, the passage makes great preaching on love, devotion, giving, and attentiveness to the Lord. I would encourage every preacher to hold a "memorial service," from time to time, for this woman (vs. 9).

10 And Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went unto the chief priests, to betray him unto them.

11 And when they heard it, they were glad, and promised to give him money. And he sought how he might []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_374}conveniently betray him.

This is the Son of Perdition betraying the Son of God. Just as Jesus being God ["manifest in the flesh"]{.class_3057-0} is ["the mystery of godliness"]{.class_3057-0} (1 Tim. 3:16), Judas as a devil incarnate (John 6:70--71) is ["the mystery of iniquity"]{.class_3057-0} (2 Thess. 2:3--7).
