The whole thing is illegal from start to finish. Look at John 18:31; that is the same crowd admitting that what they were doing was [against the law.]{.class_3058-0}

"Then said Pilate unto them, Take ye him, and judge him according to your law. The Jews therefore said unto him, It is NOT LAWFUL for us to put any man to death."

That religious court had no power under Roman law to try a man for a capital offense. He was arrested with no charge; that's illegal. He was tried with no charge. Look at verse 55 here in Mark 14. They were looking for some ["witness against Jesus to put him to death."]{.class_3057-0} Why? Because they still hadn't brought any charge against Him yet. They were hoping to find someone who could say, "I saw Him do this particular thing wrong," but they couldn't find anyone who could give credible witness against Him.

They finally had to resort to false witnesses (vs. 56; Matt. 26:59), which was a violation of the ninth commandment (["Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour,"]{.class_3057-0} Exod. 20:16). According to the Mosaic Law, a false witness was to receive the punishment he sought to inflict on the defendant (Deut. 19:18--19). But here, the judges were in cahoots with the false witnesses. In fact, they had been among the mob who arrested Christ (Luke 22:52). How would you like to be judged by the cop who arrested you? You think there might be a little bias and prejudice in the verdict? I think []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_407}so!

Do you know why they keep bringing in so many witnesses? Because under the Mosaic Law they claimed to follow and teach, no man could be condemned on the testimony of just a single witness. ["At the mouth of two witnesses, or at the mouth of three witnesses, shall the matter be established"]{.class_3057-0} (Deut. 19:15). None of the false witnesses could "get their act together" (vs. 56). The closest they came was some witnesses to Christ's words in John 2:19 (cf. vs. 58), but even then they [lied]{.class_3058-0} about what He said. Here's what He actually said:

["Jesus answered and said unto them, Destroy THIS TEMPLE, and in three days I will raise it up"]{.class_3057-0} (John 2:19).

["This temple"]{.class_3057-0}? Is He talking about Herod's Temple (John 2:20)? Of course not.

John 2:21---["But he spake of THE TEMPLE OF HIS BODY."]{.class_3057-0}

Well, did He do it? Yes! They killed Him, and He arose from the dead, so He didn't sin in what He said. But you see, those chief priests and Pharisees didn't care what the truth was; they were just looking for anything they could to get rid of Him.

Of course, there were other things not mentioned in Scripture, but which were true historically, that made this trial a farce. The trial was held at night, which was illegal. It was held at a time when no witnesses could be procured for the defense. It was held on the day before a Sabbath, during the Passover season, which was also against the Jewish law of that time. Christ was condemned on His own testimony (vss. 63--64) with no consideration at all for the veracity of His statement. The sentence of death was pronounced at a private residence (Luke 22:54), not in a court of law. And to get a unanimous decision of condemnation, any jurists who would have voted in favor of Jesus were excluded from the proceedings (as was the case with Joseph of Arimathea---Luke 23:50--51).

This was the work of the [religious]{.class_3058-0} crowd---the ones entrusted with the moral and Scriptural leadership of the nation. To condemn Christ, they had to violate the Mosaic Law and their own civil law. What a fine bunch of "godly" folks, just as "godly" as the Mafia and ISIS.

Now, all these witnesses are coming forward making accusations, []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_408}but no two of them are in agreement. So the high priest tries to get Jesus to say something in response so he can ["catch him in his words"]{.class_3057-0} like the ones tried to do back in 12:13. ["Answerest thou nothing? what is it which these witness against thee?"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 60).

Well, Jesus was under no obligation to answer anything they said. The burden of proof was on them to prove the accusations, not on him to disprove them. If none of them, from the high priest down to the false witnesses, could even come up with two stories that agreed with each other, why should He answer any of it?

But there is a spiritual side to Christ's silence (vs. 61). First of all, the Lord never answers a [dishonest skeptic.]{.class_3058-0} Over in Luke 23:7--9, Pilate sends Jesus to Herod for judgment, hoping to get Christ off his hands. But Jesus didn't say anything to Herod. Do you know why? Because Herod had already [rejected the truth]{.class_3058-0} when it was preached to him by John the Baptist. The Lord has nothing further to say to someone who rejects the truth he's given.

But Christ's silence was also a fulfillment of prophecy. Here it is 783 years before it happens.

["He was oppressed, and he was afflicted, yet he opened not his mouth: he is brought as a lamb to the slaughter, and as a sheep before her shearers is dumb, so he openeth not his mouth"]{.class_3057-0} (Isa. 53:7).

Here it is again over a thousand years before it happens.

["I said, I will take heed to my ways, that I sin not with my tongue: I will keep my mouth with a bridle, while the wicked is before me. I was dumb with silence, I held my peace, even from good; and my sorrow was stirred"]{.class_3057-0} (Psa. 39:1--2).

Back to verse 61. Having failed to get two witnesses to agree on anything against Jesus, and having failed to get Jesus to answer His accusers, the high priest "plays a card" he figures will force Jesus to say something which the council can use against Him. ["I adjure thee by the living God"]{.class_3057-0} (Matt. 26:63). That's the equivalent of putting Jesus under the penalty or curse of an oath (see Num. 5:19--21; 1 Sam. 14:28). So the Lord has to answer or be guilty under the Law.

["Art thou the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?"]{.class_3057-0}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_409 .class-0} (Matt. 26:63 says, ["the Son of God."]{.class_3057-0}) We have already discussed the designation ["THE Christ"]{.class_3057-0} under Peter's confession in 8:29 (which see). Any good Jew knew there would be a difference between God's anointed (Psa. 2:2) and Satan's anointed (Ezek. 28:14). Moreover, any good Jew knew that the Lord's anointed, the Christ, the Messiah, would be God's only begotten Son (Psa. 2:6--7). And before the nation of Israel rejected Jesus Christ as its Messiah, any good Jew knew that the Son of God, the Messiah, would be God Almighty in the flesh (Mic. 5:2; Isa. 9:6; Jer. 23:5--6 cf. John 10:30--33). Having rejected Christ, the Rabbis now teach their people that the Messiah will be just a man, like David or Hezekiah; i.e., he will be a [sinner just like they are. They will get one to their specifications]{.class_3058-0} (John 5:43).

The cross-references for calling God ["the Blessed"]{.class_3057-0} will be Genesis 9:26, 14:20, 24:27; Exodus 18:10; Ruth 4:14; 1 Samuel 25:32, 39; 2 Samuel 18:28, 22:47; 1 Kings 1:48, 5:7, 8:15, 56, 10:9; 1 Chronicles 16:36, 29:10; 2 Chronicles 2:12, 6:4, 9:8; etc. That's about half of them. It's speaking of God being worthy of all praise and glory for who He is and what He has done. In the New Testament, it is applied to [Jesus Christ]{.class_3058-0} (1 Tim. 1:11, 6:15 cf. Rev. 5:12).

Christ's answer to the high priest opens with a bombshell: ["I am."]{.class_3057-0} BLAM! That's what knocked Judas and the ones who came to take the Lord in the Garden for a loop (John 18:5). It's the Tetragrammaton (Exod. 3:14). It's not just an admission to being the Messiah; it's a claim of Deity (John 8:58--59). Despite the denials of the Liberals and Mohammedans, Jesus Christ professed to be [Jehovah God.]{.class_3058-0}

Following up on this profession of Deity, Jesus identifies Himself as ["the Son of man."]{.class_3057-0} Dual natures---100% God; 100% man. He's the God-man.

Note the two positions in which the Jews will see Christ in the future: 1) ["sitting on the right hand of power"]{.class_3068-0}; 2) ["coming in the clouds of heaven."]{.class_3068-0} Those are both fulfillments of Old Testament Messianic prophecies. The first is Psalm 110:1.

"The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool."

That's the verse Christ used back in 12:36 to trip up those []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_410}Pharisees and Sadducees. The point He was making was that He was the second member of the Trinity.

But more than that here, it is a reference to a post-Tribulation rapture of Jewish saints at some point preceding the Second Advent (Rev. 14:14--16). That is what follows here in Mark 14:61---["and coming in the clouds of heaven."]{.class_3057-0} That's a fulfillment of Daniel 7:13--14, and you can see our comments on that passage in the [Bible Believer's Commentary on Daniel.]{.class_3058-0} The last clause of Mark 14:61 is a reference to the Second Advent, as given in Revelation 1:7 and Zechariah 12:10 and Zephaniah 1:15 and Joel 2:1--2.

Having heard Christ's "confession," there were only two ways of looking at it: either He was lying, or He was telling the truth. There is no middle ground with the Lord (Luke 11:23). He is either who He said He was (God Almighty in human form---Phil. 2:6--7), or He was a liar or a madman.

If this had been a fair trial, then the next step would have been to investigate the claim to see if it were true or not (see Deut. 13:14, 17:4, 19:18). There was certainly enough evidence between fulfilled prophecy and His miracles to validate the claims of Jesus; at the very least those Pharisees on the council knew God was with Jesus (John 3:2). But the whole proceeding was "rigged." There was only one outcome that would be allowed.

["Ye have heard the blasphemy"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 64). Well, it was only blasphemy if it weren't true. Let's just face it, if Jesus Christ weren't who He said He was, He got exactly what He deserved. I am betting my eternal soul that [He was telling the truth.]{.class_3058-0}

The abuse in verse 65 was prophesied 712 years before it took place (Isa. 50:6). There is no way it could be "orchestrated" from a human standpoint, nor would those Christ-rejecting Jews have tried to fulfill Scripture if they believed it would lend credence to the claims of Jesus Christ.

Listen, do you know what the penalty was for blasphemy? It wasn't just death; it was death by [stoning]{.class_3058-0} (Lev. 24:16). Then how do you explain the fact that Jesus Christ was executed by [crucifixion]{.class_3058-0}? After all, those Jews tried to stone Him twice, even though they couldn't do it (John 8:59, 10:31). They did stone Stephen in Acts 7:58 despite the fact that it wasn't lawful for them, under Roman []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_411}rule, to put a man to death (John 18:31).

Again, how do you explain the Crucifixion? There is only one rational explanation: God Almighty was going to fulfill what He said about the death of His Son no matter what those Jews or Romans wanted to do (John 19:31--36). Buddha couldn't do anything like it. Zoroaster and Mohammed as prophets were horselaughs. There's only one Book that will give you history 1,500--400 years ahead of time and have everything come through "on the money." It's not the [Tripitaka]{.class_3058-0} or the [Vedas]{.class_3058-0} or the [Shastas]{.class_3058-0} or the [Puranas]{.class_3058-0} or the [Analects of Confucius]{.class_3058-0} or the[ Zend Avesta,]{.class_3058-0} and it sure ain't the [Koran]{.class_3058-0} or the [Hadith.]{.class_3058-0} It's the [Holy Bible:]{.class_3058-0} the ["sure word of prophecy"]{.class_3057-0} (2 Pet. 1:19).

The covering of Christ's face in verse 65 is defined in Luke 22:64 as Jesus being ["blindfolded."]{.class_3057-0} This is a cruel game of "blind-man's bluff." He's blindfolded and struck by the high priest's servants.

["Prophesy"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 65). [He had prophesied]{.class_3058-0} (vs. 62), and they refused to believe Him. They were fulfilling prophecy right then with no intent of doing so. Of course, what they are asking Him to do is tell them, with each blow, who was the one who had hit Him (Matt. 26:68; Luke 22:64). If the Lord had been so inclined, He could have given the name, rank, and serial number of every "dogface" in the room.

66 And as Peter was beneath in the palace, there cometh one of the maids of the high priest:

67 And when she saw Peter warming himself, she looked upon him, and said, And thou also wast with Jesus of Nazareth.

68 But he denied, saying, I know not, neither understand I what thou sayest. And he went out into the porch; and the cock crew.

69 And a maid saw him again, and began to say to them that stood by, This is one of them.

70 And he denied it again. And a little after, they that stood by said again to Peter, Surely thou art one of them: for thou art a Galilaean, and thy speech agreeth thereto.

71 But he began to curse and to swear, saying, I know []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_412}not this man of whom ye speak.

72 And the second time the cock crew. And Peter called to mind the word that Jesus said unto him, Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice. And when he thought thereon, he wept.

The scene now switches to Simon Peter ["beneath in the palace"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 66). When Christ had been taken in the garden and led away to the high priest, ["Peter followed him afar off"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 54). Jesus had told Peter earlier that evening, ["before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 30). [Following the Lord from a distance,]{.class_3058-0} so as not to be associated with His reproach (1 Tim. 4:10; Heb. 11:26, 13:13), was the first step toward that denial.

The second step in Peter's backsliding was [having fellowship with the Lord's enemies:]{.class_3058-0}[ "he sat with the servants ]{.class_3057-0}\[of the high priest\][, and warmed himself at the fire"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 54). Christian, you are not to love the world (1 John 2:15) or be conformed to it (Rom. 12:2). You are commanded not to have ["fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather reprove them"]{.class_3057-0} (Eph. 5:11). Peter was hanging around the same crowd who spit on, beat on, and ridiculed his Lord (vs. 65). Christian, you had better learn, and learn quickly, that this lost world has no love or goodwill at all for your Saviour. If you hang around them, you will begin to act like them, you will begin to think like them, and you will begin to talk like them (see Rom. 3:14). That is what happens to Peter (vs. 71).

Now, Peter hadn't gone in to watch the proceedings against Jesus, but he had a buddy who did. It was John. John had some connection to the high priest, and he had "sicced" the servant girl of verses 66--67 on Peter (John 18:15--18). John may have run away in the garden (vs. 50), but he came back and followed the Lord all the way to the cross (John 19:26--27). He's the only one of the Twelve who did. That's why, throughout his Gospel, he calls himself ["the disciple whom Jesus loved."]{.class_3057-0} He loved the Lord so much that he wasn't ashamed of Him at His trial or His execution. That is why he is given the privilege of seeing the return of Christ in the Revelation (John 21:20--22).

John gave Peter the chance to speak up for Jesus Christ as one of []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_413}the Lord's disciples, and Peter failed miserably. He told that servant girl, "I don't know that fella, and I don't even know what you're talking about" (see vs. 68). About that time, the rooster sounds off for the first time.

Don't you know that put Peter in a sweat, man. He gets away from that door just as quickly as he can and goes out on the porch of that palace. About that time, ["another maid saw him"]{.class_3057-0} (Matt. 26:71) and says to everyone hanging around, ["This fellow was also with Jesus of Nazareth"]{.class_3057-0} (Matt. 26:71); ["This is one of them]{.class_3057-0} \[i.e., Christ's disciples\]["]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 69).

And here we have one of those great "contradictions" in the Bible. Luke 22:58 says it was a ["Man."]{.class_3057-0} Of course, being a little too eager to accuse the Bible of fault, the infidels and critics are deficient in their ability to read sixth-grade English. John says, ["THEY]{.class_3057-0} \[[plural]{.class_3058-0}---both the maid and the man\][ said therefore unto him, Art not thou also one of his disciples?"]{.class_3057-0} (John 18:25). Nothing like a Bible to clear up an "open mind" that has been filled with [garbage]{.class_3058-0} at a University or Seminary.

Once more, Peter denies the Lord (vs. 70), but this time, he uses ["an oath"]{.class_3057-0} (Matt. 26:72). So he's saying something like "By God" or "As the Lord lives." He's bringing God to witness that he doesn't know Jesus. So he's taking the Lord's name in vain before he ever starts "cussin'."

But not long after that, that bunch on the porch turn to Peter and say, ["Surely thou art one of them: for thou art a Galilaean, and thy speech agreeth thereto"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 70). Peter's a "Yankee" talking to a bunch of "Southerners." All of those disciples, except for Judas Iscariot (see our comments on 3:19), were from [northern]{.class_3058-0} Israel, and Jerusalem is in the [south.]{.class_3058-0}

There is nearly always a difference in accent between those in the north and those in the south, even though they speak the same language.That'll go for Scotland (north) and England (south), for Germany (north) and Austria (south), and for Mexico (north) and South America (south).

But there is more going on here than Peter saying "you'se guys" around a bunch who say "y'all." Matthew 26:73 records that crowd saying, ["thy speech bewrayeth]{.class_3057-0} \[betrays\][ thee."]{.class_3057-0} For all the effort Peter put into distancing himself from Jesus Christ, he still []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_414 .class-0}[talks like a "Christian."]{.class_3058-0} So to prove he was not a disciple of the Lord, Peter ["began to curse and to swear"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 71). Peter "cusses up a blue streak," and all those soldiers standing around that fire are taking notes to use in drill the next day.

Peter says, "Blankety-blank, I don't know the blankety-blank!" He gets through using his "few choice words," and now no one around that fire thinks he's saved. But as soon as he finishes, that rooster sounds off ["the second time"]{.class_3057-0} (vs. 72), and the weight of what he had just done hits Peter like a ton of bricks. He runs out of that place bawling like a baby. Matthew and Luke tell you he ["wept bitterly"]{.class_3057-0} (Matt. 26:75; Luke 22:62).

And of course, we don't know exactly where Peter went because the Scriptures don't tell us, but I believe I know. I think he went back to the Garden of Gethsemane where he had fallen asleep at prayer just a few hours before. He didn't fall asleep this time. I'll bet you he wept and prayed till long past the sun coming up.

In closing this chapter, I want us to notice the difference between the repentance of Peter and the repentance of Judas Iscariot. In 2 Corinthians 7:10, there are two kinds of repentance: one is ["godly sorrow"]{.class_3057-0}; the other is ["the sorrow of the world."]{.class_3057-0} Peter illustrates the former; Judas illustrates the latter. Both sorrowed over their sin, both made restitution, but both weren't the same. The motivation behind Peter's repentance was a [look from the Lord ]{.class_3058-0}(Luke 22:61); the motivation for Judas was a [look at his failed plan]{.class_3058-0} (Matt 27:3). Peter was sorry for what he [was]{.class_3058-0} (cf. Luke 5:8); Judas was sorry for what he had [done]{.class_3058-0} (Matt. 27:4). Peter confessed to [Jesus Christ]{.class_3058-0} (John 21:15--17); Judas---and you had better get this---confessed to [priests]{.class_3058-0} (Matt. 27:3). Peter's repentance ended in a restoration of his apostleship and living for his Lord so that he won thousands to Jesus Christ (Acts 2:41, 4:4); Judas' repentance ended in a total loss of his apostleship and suicide (Acts 1:16--20).

Some of the brethren harp on "repentance" a lot these days. I believe in repentance, but if your "repentance" doesn't result in trusting Christ and His righteousness for salvation, [you haven't truly repented.]{.class_3058-0} If all you do is "stop sinning," you are no better than that rich young ruler back in chapter 10, trying to get to Heaven by your own self-righteousness (see our comments on 10:17--22). It's []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2374.xhtml_page_415 .class-0}[both]{.class_3058-0}[ "repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Jesus Christ"]{.class_3057-0} (Acts 20:21).

As a Christian, your "repentance" from a sin is to be accompanied by the works of the new man in Christ in opposition to that sin. You can read Paul's "take" on the subject in Galatians 5:16--26; Ephesians 4:17--32; and Colossians 3:1--17.

[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml}

# Chapter 15 {.heading_97}

15:1 And straightway in the morning the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council, and bound Jesus, and carried him away, and delivered him to Pilate.

2 And Pilate asked him, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answering said unto him, Thou sayest it.

3 And the chief priests accused him of many things: but he answered nothing.

4 And Pilate asked him again, saying, Answerest thou nothing? behold how many things they witness against thee.

5 But Jesus yet answered nothing; so that Pilate marvelled.

Again, Mark gives an abbreviated account of Jesus being ["delivered . . . to Pilate"]{.class_3054} (vs. 1). The details are filled in, this time, by Luke and John.

1\. The Jews lead Jesus to Pilate's judgment hall, but they refuse to go in so they can remain ceremonially "clean" enough to ["eat the passover"]{.class_3054} (John 18:28). These "clean," "godly" men would not defile themselves by going into a  Gentile building, but they had no problem being accessories to the [murder]{.class_3055} of an innocent man (Acts 7:52).

2\. Pilate is forced to come out to the Jews to find out ["What accusation bring ye against this man?"]{.class_3054} (John 18:29). The first answer by the priests was not an ["accusation"]{.class_3054} at all; it was mere posturing. ["If he were not a malefactor, we would not have delivered him up unto thee"]{.class_3054} (John 18:30). In other words, "Put this fellow to death just because we say so. Do you think we would have brought Him to you if He weren't a criminal worthy of death?" You have to admit, that qualifies as "chutzpah" (see our remarks on James and John's request under 10:35--37).

3\. Pilate responds with his first attempt to get rid of Jesus Christ: ["Take ye him, and judge him according to your law"]{.class_3054} (John []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_417}18:31). But those Jewish leaders want what is known as "plausible deniabililty." That is, they want Jesus put to death, but they want to be able to say they didn't actually commit the act (see Acts 5:28). So they use an excuse about which they never worried when putting Stephen to death in Acts 7: ["It is not lawful for us to put any man to death"]{.class_3054} (John 18:31). "You're the army of occupation; only you have the authority to execute a prisoner." And Pilate had Christ back on his hands.

4\. Having failed to "buffalo" Pilate, the Jews make up a string of false accusations: ["perverting the nation, and forbidding to give tribute to Caesar, saying that he himself is Christ a King"]{.class_3054} (Luke 23:2). "This Jesus is a rabble-rouser and is trying to raise insurrection against Rome." A lie---He never said one word about rebelling against Rome or Caesar. His message was ["repent ye, and believe the gospel"]{.class_3054} (1:15). He paid tribute to Rome (Matt. 17:24--27) and never told anyone not to pay his taxes (12:14--17). And as far as publicly proclaiming Himself as ["Christ a King,"]{.class_3054} He gave strict instructions to His disciples [not]{.class_3055} to do that (8:29--30). Jesus wouldn't even let the people ["take him by force, to make him a king"]{.class_3054} (John 6:15). Three lies right in a row.

Why didn't those Jews just bring the charge they had trumped up in their "kangaroo court"---blasphemy? Because Pilate didn't care one wit about the Jews' religion. He didn't care about some itinerant preacher going around yelling "Repent!" and telling stories, as long as He behaved Himself and paid His taxes.

Verse 2 in Mark 15 coincides with Luke 23:3 and John 18:33, but the simple ["Thou sayest it"]{.class_3054} of Matthew, Mark, and Luke is expanded in John. Christ's admission is followed up with a question: ["Sayest thou this thing of thyself, or did others tell it thee of me?"]{.class_3054} (John 18:34).

Now think about what is going on. Those Jews accused Jesus of claiming to be a King. That was illegal under Roman law; only Kings set up by Caesar and subservient to him were allowed to reign (like Herod). Jesus admitted to being a King; that should have ended the whole trial with a verdict of guilty. But Pilate goes out to those priests and declares Christ not guilty (["I find no fault in this man"]{.class_3054}---Luke 23:4). Why? Because of what Jesus told him in private []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_418}about His kingdom and His reason for coming to earth.

John 18:36---["My kingdom is not of this world . . . ."]{.class_3064-0} The Jews had rejected their King, so the physical kingdom had been postponed. Hence, the follow-up statement by the Lord: [". . . but NOW is my kingdom not from hence." ]{.class_3064-0}Perversions of the Holy Bible, like the[ ESV,]{.class_3066} get rid of that word ["now"]{.class_3064-0} so Christ's kingdom will never be a worldly, earthly, visible, physical kingdom. That's a heresy known as Amillennialism, and it is contradicted in literally hundreds of passages (e.g., Isa. 2, 11, 60, 61, 65, 66; Jer. 23, 30, 31, 33; Ezek. 40--48; Dan. 2; Joel 3; Amos 9; Zech. 14; Rev. 20).

Pilate then repeats his question (["Art thou a king then?"]{.class_3054}---John 18:37). The answer he is given by Christ convinces him that Jesus may have been a "crazy preacher," but an insurrectionist He was not---["To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, THAT I SHOULD BEAR WITNESS UNTO THE TRUTH"]{.class_3054} (John 18:37). Pilate may have been cynical about the truth (John 18:38), but he was convinced of Christ's innocence (John 18:38).

The additional accusations of Mark 15:3--5 are found in Luke 23:5. Once more (see 14:60--61), Jesus remains silent as the Lamb of God brought ["to the slaughter"]{.class_3054} (Isa. 53:7). Pilate marvels over Christ's silence. Peter says of this scene:

["Who ]{.class_3054}\[speaking of the Lord Jesus Christ\][, when he was reviled, reviled not again; when he suffered, he threatened not; but committed himself to him that judgeth righteously"]{.class_3054} (1 Pet. 2:23).

When Christ stood before Caiaphas and Pilate, in reality, neither one was His judge. Jesus had committed His judgment to the Father, and ["it pleased the LORD to bruise him"]{.class_3054} so that ["his soul"]{.class_3054} could be made ["an offering for sin"]{.class_3054} (Isa. 53:10; 1 Pet. 2:24).

Now before moving on to the next passage here in Mark 15, it should be noted, once more, that Luke adds something to the "timeline" of events. Before the incident with Barabbas, Pilate grasps onto the fact that Jesus is a Galilean (Luke 23:6), so Pilate makes his second attempt to get rid of Jesus Christ. He sends Jesus to Herod because Galilee was ["Herod's jurisdiction"]{.class_3054} and Herod was in Jerusalem for the Passover (Luke 23:7).

While Herod was excited to see Jesus, the Lord had nothing to say to a dishonest skeptic (Luke 23:9). Herod had rejected the preaching of John the Baptist, and the Lord had nothing further to say to Him. So Herod and his soldiers ["set him at nought"]{.class_3054} (Luke 23:11)---they set Him to public shame and disgrace. We don't know exactly how, but in all likelihood, the ["and mocked him, and arrayed him in a gorgeous robe"]{.class_3054} of Luke 23:11 is acting as a "hendiadys" and simply restating what the ["set him at nought"]{.class_3054} is.

We shall comment more on that ["gorgeous robe"]{.class_3054} under verses 17--20 and verse 24 here in Mark 15.

The passage in Luke 23 ends with: ["And the same day Pilate and Herod were made friends together: for before they were at enmity between themselves"]{.class_3054} (Luke 23:12). People who have nothing in common whatsoever (like queers and Mohammedans, or feminists and Mohammedans) can get together on one thing:[ their hatred of Jesus Christ and that Book.]{.class_3055} It will eventually culminate in a coalition of nations gathered together against ["the Word of God"]{.class_3054} (Rev. 19:11--19); their "destiny" is to be [burned]{.class_3055} (Zeph. 3:8; Matt. 13:40--42).

6 Now at that feast he released unto them one prisoner, whomsoever they desired.

7 And there was one named Barabbas, which lay bound with them that had made insurrection with him, who had committed murder in the insurrection.

8 And the multitude crying aloud began to desire him to do as he had ever done unto them.

9 But Pilate answered them, saying, Will ye that I release unto you the King of the Jews?

10 For he knew that the chief priests had delivered him for envy.

11 But the chief priests moved the people, that he should rather release Barabbas unto them.

12 And Pilate answered and said again unto them, What will ye then that I shall do unto him whom ye call the King of the Jews?

13 And they cried out again, Crucify him.

14 Then Pilate said unto them, Why, what evil hath he done? And they cried out the more exceedingly, Crucify him.

15 And so Pilate, willing to content the people, released Barabbas unto them, and delivered Jesus, when he had scourged him, to be crucified.

16 And the soldiers led him away into the hall, called Praetorium; and they call together the whole band.

17 And they clothed him with purple, and platted a crown of thorns, and put it about his head,

18 And began to salute him, Hail, King of the Jews!

19 And they smote him on the head with a reed, and did spit upon him, and bowing their knees worshipped him.

20 And when they had mocked him, they took off the purple from him, and put his own clothes on him, and led him out to crucify him.

So far, Pilate has tried [carelessness]{.class_3055} to get rid of Jesus Christ---"You take Him and judge Him yourself." He has tried [cowardice---]{.class_3055}"He's from Herod's jurisdiction, so let Herod take care of the problem." But Jesus Christ is the hardest person you'll ever try to get rid of; like a bad penny, He keeps coming back. Herod sent Jesus Christ back to Pilate, and Pilate had Him on his hands again.

Having failed to rid himself of Jesus Christ by carelessness and cowardice, Pilate now tries [cleverness.]{.class_3055} Pilate tries taking advantage of the Jewish custom of releasing a prisoner at the time of the Passover to get Jesus Christ "off the hook" (vs. 6 cf. John 18:39).

Being a "positive thinker" like Norman Vincent Peale, Robert Schuller, and Joel Osteen, Pilate was convinced that all men were "basically good" and that if given a clear choice between Jesus Christ and a really bad man, the crowd would choose to let Jesus Christ go. So Pilate picked the worst man he could think of: Barabbas. Barabbas was ["a notable prisoner"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 27:16) because not only was he ["a robber"]{.class_3054} (John 18:40), but he had ["made insurrection"]{.class_3054} against the Romans and ["had committed murder in the insurrection"]{.class_3054} (vs. 7). He had actually done what the Jews had accused Christ of (see our remarks under vs. 1). Barabbas was a "bad []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_421}cat."

Pilate figured that mob would look at Jesus Christ and say, "Well, He may preach out there on the street and say I'm going to Hell, but He never really hurt anybody. I mean, He's healed lepers, given sight to the blind, caused the crippled to walk again. That's a good man; let Him go." And then they'd look at Barabbas and say, "That fellow's a thief; he's a murderer. He's causing problems with the Romans, and he's gonna get us all killed. He's a pistol ball. Kill him!"

But that's not how people think. That crowd looked at Barabbas and thought to themselves, "Well he may be a robber and a murderer and a traitor, but he was raised in the ghetto, you know. He didn't have the advantages you and I had growing up. And to be called that name all your life, no wonder he turned out the way he did. In fact, [he's kind of like me]{.class_3055}. Give him a break and let him go." Then they looked at Christ: "There's that Prophet. I never did like that fellow. He thinks He's right and everyone else is wrong. Who does He think He is? KILL HIM!"

That's the way people are. They will pick a thieving murderer over a [critical preacher who preached against their sin ]{.class_3055}because that thief and murderer is [like them.]{.class_3055} That mob picked a murderer over a righteous man (vss. 11--13 cf. Acts 3:14), and Pilate had Jesus Christ right back on his hands again.

Now there are a couple of important things to note in this passage. The first is in verse 10. Pilate may have been a conniving politician looking out for his own self-interests, but he had those chief priests' number. He knew they had delivered Jesus over to him out of [envy.]{.class_3055}

Those chief priests, scribes, Pharisees, and Sadducees were [jealous]{.class_3055} of the Lord. They were jealous of the crowds He drew. They were envious of the fact that He spoke with the authority of Holy Scripture (see our comments on 1:22), while all they had were the opinions and preferences of men (see our comments on 7:6--13). They envied Him for the power of God that was evident in His ministry.

Envy was the "original sin": Lucifer was envious of God (Isa. 14:12--14). Cain envied his brother's sacrifice being accepted by the Lord while his own was rejected. Joseph's brothers envied him []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_422}because of the favor bestowed on him by God and their daddy. Saul envied David for having ["ten thousands"]{.class_3054} ascribed to him after he slew Goliath (1 Sam. 18:7--9). Those Princes and Presidents in Daniel 6 were envious of Daniel and his position. Envy of what God is doing with someone else is one of the greatest temptations a preacher will face in the ministry.

Now I'll tell you what to do when you become jealous of someone. There's an old American Indian saying: "Don't judge a man until you have spent two moons in his moccasins." Before you envy someone, consider what that person had to go through to get what he has. That's particularly true of these Hollywood stars and starlets. Would you want to go through seven divorces and eight spouses like Mickey Rooney and Elizabeth Taylor? Would you want to be hooked on pills and die before you're fifty years old like Judy Garland, Elvis Presley, Michael Jackson, or Hank Williams? No? Then don't be ["envious against the workers of iniquity"]{.class_3054} (Psa. 37:1).

I have often thought about this thing in regards to the million-dollar launching pad for "lady fingers" down the road from us here at the Pensacola Bible Institute. I mean, here we are with a couple of tin buildings and only a few hundred students at any given time (between 100 and 300), trying to restore their faith in the Scriptures ([AV]{.class_3055} 1611) and train them to be bold in their witness for Jesus Christ. Meanwhile, down the road, those professors are replacing the faith of their students in the English Scriptures of the Protestant Reformation with the opinions and preferences of Christian scholarship (it used to be Alexandrian scholarship, but now it is Byzantine--[TR]{.class_3055} scholarship. Anything but believe the Book); they are turning out a bunch of effeminate, scared bunny rabbits (at one point the students weren't allowed to open their Bibles if they were witnessing to someone on the street corners lest they be associated with "Ruckman"). I look at that and think, "Lord, why didn't you give me a couple of thousand young men a year like that to train?"

Then I think about what the founders went through (and [didn't]{.class_3055} go through) to get that enrollment: promoting the [NASV]{.class_3055} and the Alexandrian text for years and years in order to stand in good with Christian scholarship, no street preaching, no summer preaching junkets to the prisons seeing hundreds of souls saved, and [no kids of their own.]{.class_3055}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_423} With as much as I love kids, I couldn't imagine what it would be like not to have any of my own. I ended up with ten kids, twenty grandkids, and fourteen great-grandkids. That's great! So I shut up and take what I've got.

We should also take note of Barabbas' position in these proceedings. The name [Barabbas]{.class_3055} means "son of the father." So that mob is faced with two "sons of the father." One is the Son of God the Father. The other is a "son of the father" who is like his "daddy": he was a [robber]{.class_3055} (John 10:1 cf. John 18:40), and he was a [murderer]{.class_3055} (John 8:44 cf. Acts 3:14). And if you go back to Isaiah 14:12--14, he was a [rebel]{.class_3055} like his "daddy." So here we have a type of what Jesus said in John 5:43 concerning the Antichrist.

"I am come in my Father's name, and ye receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive."

But there is another type Barabbas fulfills in regards to our salvation. Barabbas stands in the place of every fallen son of Adam who left "the Father," and it is the Father's own Son (John 1:18) who takes the fallen son's place on Calvary. Paul may have understood the spiritual and doctrinal ramifications of the vicarious atonement and the Crucifixion in regards to salvation and the crucified life of the believer, and there are theologians who can explain it doctrinally, but no one before, during, or after the bloody enactment on Golgotha's hill could grasp the practical implications of Calvary better than Barabbas. Barabbas knew by [first-hand experience]{.class_3055} what it meant for Christ to die for sinners, because he, literally and physically, was the sinner for whom Christ died; that middle cross was meant for him. I call Barabbas: "the only man who understood the Crucifixion."

Barabbas knew he was [a sinner justly condemned.]{.class_3055} There was no doubt in his mind about his own guilt. If you are ever going to come to Christ for salvation, you are going to have to abandon any claims to your own righteousness and plead [guilty as charged.]{.class_3055}

Barabbas knew[ an innocent sufferer had been unjustly condemned.]{.class_3055} You don't fool a professional crook like Barabbas. He knew an innocent man when he saw one, and he knew Jesus was [innocent]{.class_3055} (Heb. 7:26).

Barabbas knew [a substitute was taking his place.]{.class_3055}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_424} I guarantee you that when that Roman guard released Barabbas from his cell and told him the reason for his "reprieve," that Barabbas went to see the One who was taking his place on a cross.

One of the reasons that nearly a billion Moslems will end up in the Lake of Fire, along with their "prophet," is because they won't accept that Someone [died in their place for their sins.]{.class_3055} Allah is so deficient in compassion for his followers, and so lacking in holiness himself, that he has made no provision for their sins: every Moslem, including Mohammed, has to pay for any of his own sins that were not offset by his good works. Ditto every Catholic, Buddhist, Hindu, Shintoist, Confucianist, Sikh, Protestant, Jew, and Greek Orthodox on the planet. Human religion hasn't progressed half an inch past the Egyptian "Book of the Dead," where a man's "heart" had to be lighter on the gods' scale than the "feather of truth" to enter into eternal bliss. If that be the case, every man, woman, and child would go to Hell. Don't believe me? Look up Romans 3:4, 13 and READ THEM! Don't be a superstitious pagan all your life.

When you hit the "Last Judgment," you will face something more damning than any "feather of truth" or your good works outweighing your bad. You will answer to the perfect ["righteousness of God"]{.class_3054} in Jesus Christ (Rom. 10:3--4), and if you don't accept that righteousness as a free gift now (Rom. 5:18), you will be condemned by it then (Rom. 2:2--5, 16). Turning down the substitutionary death of Christ for your sins is like a prisoner on death row turning down a pardon from the Governor the night before he goes to the "hot squat."

Barabbas knew[ the Substitute satisfied the demands of the law.]{.class_3055} Under the law, someone had to die for Barabbas' crimes; if not him, then someone the law allowed in his place. Jesus Christ took Barabbas' place on that middle cross according to the laws of that time. Jesus Christ met the demands of God's Law to pay the price of our sin debt:[ "Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us"]{.class_3054} (Gal. 3:13).

Barabbas knew [he had done nothing to merit the substitution.]{.class_3055} The old song goes: "Nothing in my hands I bring; Simply to Thy cross I cling"; that's the business.

Back to Mark 15. Having failed to get rid of Jesus Christ by carelessness, cowardice, and cleverness, Pilate tries one last stratagem: [compromise.]{.class_3055} Pilate figures that mob is in a bloodlust, so to satisfy it, he has Jesus beaten to within an inch of His life (vs. 15).

Jesus is led back in Pilate's judgment hall (the ["Praetorium"]{.class_3054}---vs. 16 cf. John 18:28, 33) where Pilate's guards all gather together. Jesus' hands are tied over His head to a post with His back to the soldiers. One of them steps forward with a "cat-o'-nine-tails"---a whip with nine long leather straps with bits of broken glass, pottery shards, and bone along each strap. That soldier brings that whip back and lashes it forward. The straps wrap themselves around Christ's body and the sharp pieces in the thongs dig deep into the skin and muscle. As the whip is drawn back, chunks of flesh are torn out. Isaiah prophesied, ["his visage was so marred more than any man, and his form more than the sons of men"]{.class_3054} (Isa. 52:14). Unlike the Jewish restriction of forty lashes (Deut. 25:2--3), the Romans had no such limit; a soldier beat a man until the one administering the whipping was worn out. Some prisoners died from the beating before they ever went to the cross. By the time that soldier was through, Jesus Christ was a bloody mess.

But that was not the end of Christ's humiliation. Those soldiers stripped Him of His clothing and ["clothed him with purple"]{.class_3054} (vs. 17). ["Purple"]{.class_3054} what? Mark doesn't say (see vs. 20). It's a ["purple ROBE"]{.class_3054} (John 19:2, 5). Luke calls it ["a gorgeous robe"]{.class_3054} (Luke 23:11). It's the kind of robe worn by royalty (Judg. 8:26; Esth. 8:15) and the wealthy (Luke 16:19). Matthew tells you it was also ["scarlet"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 27:28), so this robe is connected with a religious [whore]{.class_3055} (Rev. 17:4--5) from a city on seven hills (Rev. 17:9). Who, oh who, could that possibly be?

Now this is important to note, because the Catholic Church has long had an affinity for religious relics. These range from the obscene (the foreskin of the baby Jesus and Mary's breast milk) to the weird (a feather from the wing of the Holy Spirit and a smelly part of Lazarus' body \[I imagine it would smell after 1,500 years\]) to the morbid (the nails that held Christ to the cross) to the ridiculous (two heads of John the Baptist and eight arms of Matthew). There are enough pieces of the "true cross" floating around Europe to build a []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_426}ranch-style house.

In Roman Catholic mythology (as popularized by Lloyd Douglas in his book, and Victor Mature and Richard Burton in the movie), this "robe" was supposed to have miraculous powers to heal (plagiarizing Mark 5:27--29) and convict of sin, righteousness, and judgment (plagiarizing John 16:8; Acts 24:25). The answer to that is "NUTS!"---brazil, cashews, peanuts, filberts, pecans, walnuts, etc.

When you hear Eddie Arnold or George Beverly Shea sing about "His robe, His robe, the robe of Calvary," just throw that robe back in the "Goodwill" bin where it belongs. Christ's clothes (which were put back on Him when the robe was taken off---vs. 20) were [never]{.class_3055} called a "robe" anywhere in the Bible. They were His ["clothes"]{.class_3054} (vs. 20), His ["garments"]{.class_3054} (vs. 24), His ["raiment"]{.class_3054} (Luke 23:34), and His ["vesture"]{.class_3054} (John 9:24); but never a "robe." The "robe" was something the Roman soldiers put on Him to mock the ["vesture dipped in blood"]{.class_3054} He will wear when He does come as a King (Rev. 19:13; Isa. 63:1--6). The ["crown of thorns"]{.class_3054} (vs. 17) was the Devil's way of mocking those ["many crowns"]{.class_3054} He will take off the ten Kings He conquers at His return (Rev. 17:12; Dan. 7:24 cf. 2 Sam. 12:30).

Mark tells you about the ["reed"]{.class_3054} the soldiers use to smack Jesus on His head (vs. 19). There are two purposes for that. First, they can't push that crown of thorns on His head without tearing up their own hands, so they put the crown of thorns on His head and beat it down with the reed. But what Mark doesn't tell you, that Matthew does, is that the reed was given to Jesus as a mock scepter (Matt. 27:29--30), to ridicule that ["rod of iron"]{.class_3054} He will use to rule the nations (Rev. 2:27, 12:5). There in the Praetorium, those Gentile G.I.s were smiting the ["King of the Jews,"]{.class_3054} but when He returns in power, He will ["smite the nations"]{.class_3054} (Rev. 19:15). That is, He will smash their skulls in and then stomp all over their bodies. (Don't get mad with me; I didn't write the Book. Go read Rev. 19:15--21 and see what Stewart Custer's meek, mild, gentle "Jesus" plans on doing when He comes back.)

["And did spit upon him"]{.class_3054} (vs. 19). That's a fulfillment of the same prophecy that told you of His scourging.

["I gave my back to the smiters, and my cheeks to them that plucked off the hair: I hid not my face from shame and spitting"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_427} (Isa. 50:6).

So not only are they spitting in His face, they are plucking off chunks of His beard. In His human nature, He is going through all of this, trusting in God to help Him and justify Him (Isa. 50:7--9 cf. Heb. 12:2). The disciple is to consider how his Lord ["endured such contradiction of sinners against himself"]{.class_3054} so he will not ["be weary in well doing"]{.class_3054} and faint in adversity (Heb. 12:3 cf. Gal. 6:9).

["And bowing their knees worshipped him."]{.class_3054} Of course, it is all in mockery. None of them (yet!---vs. 39) are showing Him any honor as the King, much less worshiping Him as God. But one day they will (Isa. 45:23; Rom. 14:11--12), and when they do, it will be because of what He went through right here (Phil. 2:8--11). As a Baptist preacher on my way to Heaven, I have the satisfaction of knowing that, someday, everyone to whom I've ever preached or witnessed will be converted to my way of thinking. Of course, it won't do the ones who rejected the message any good then. They will "convert" right before being pitched into the Lake of Fire. That's why Paul said, ["NOW is the accepted time; behold, NOW is the day of salvation"]{.class_3054} (2 Cor. 6:2). The time to acknowledge Christ as Lord is not at the White Throne Judgment when He will be your Judge; it is NOW when He can be your Saviour. I took advantage of that deal on March 14, 1949, in the record room of WEAR at the corner of Gregory and Palafox.

Now before Jesus is given back His own clothes and led off to His execution, two things happen. First, Pilate brings out Jesus before the mob. He stands there in that scarlet-purple robe with that crown of thorns on His head, flesh shredded, a bloody mess. Pilate is visibly impressed. He says, ["Behold the man!"]{.class_3054} (John 19:5), as if He were the only man on earth.

But ["the chief priests"]{.class_3054} (John 19:6). There's the problem. They were the ones who ["moved the people"]{.class_3054} to ask for Barabbas' release (vs. 11). They are now the ones who call for Christ's crucifixion ["because he made himself the Son of God"]{.class_3054} (John 19:7). "Religion" will damn you just as sure as the world.

That last accusation shakes Pilate up (John 19:8). ["The Son of God"]{.class_3054}?! As an idol-worshiping pagan, Pilate knew more (practically speaking) about Genesis 6 and Daniel 3 than the faculties and staffs of []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_428}the major Christian Universities, Colleges, and Seminaries. He takes Christ back into that judgment hall and asks Him, "Where are you from?!" (John 19:9). That's a "Star Trek" question. Pilate knew Jesus was from Galilee as far as His earthly background; that's why he had sent Him to Herod. But now, Pilate has a sneaking suspicion that Jesus Christ is "not of this world."

But Jesus doesn't answer Pilate's question. Pilate has violated his conscience one too many times. What would you think of a judge who pronounces a man "not guilty" three times and then turns around and gives him the death sentence? That's what Pilate did.

Pilate makes one last attempt to free Jesus Christ. He brings Jesus out and says, ["Behold your King!"]{.class_3054} (John 19:14). But those chief priests aren't about to have their office usurped by God's King: "Kill Him!" ["Shall I crucify your King?"]{.class_3054} (John 19:15). ["We have no king but Caesar"]{.class_3054} (John 19:15).

Uh oh! They never should have said that! They turned down the Messiah for a megalomaniacal Roman dictator. So God gave them Claudius (Acts 18:2), Nero, Titus (destruction of Jerusalem A.D. 70), the Popes, the Czars, Mussolini, and Hitler. Why? That's what they said they wanted.

Some prophetic expositors today are "squeamish" about attributing the persecution of the Jews to God Almighty for their rejection of Christ, but one cannot read Deuteronomy 28 and Judges and Lamentations without reaching that very conclusion. God promised those Jews if they lived right, He would bless them, and if they didn't, He would send in the heathen nations to persecute them, enslave them, kill them, and make their lives miserable.

You say, "Are you against those Jews, Ruckman?" Nosiree! I wouldn't touch them to do them harm for all the tea in China! Why not, if that is what God wants? Because as soon as the Lord is through with whomever He raises up to whip His people, the same thing happens to them (read Psa. 137:7--9). God sends in Assyria to punish the northern tribes for their idolatry, so He sends in Babylon to wipe out Assyria. He uses Nebuchadnezzar to punish Judah and Benjamin for doing what Israel did, so He sends in the Medes and the Persians to take care of Nebuchadnezzar's kingdom. When He's finished with the Persians, He sends in Alexander the Great to mop up Persia. []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_429}After those Greeks have accomplished their purpose, He sends in the Romans. When the Romans are of no more use to Him, He wipes them out with the Huns, the Visigoths, the Ostrogoths, the Vandals, etc. He lets those Popes put the Jews in ghettos and exile them and burn them at the stake until He's had enough of that, so He lets old Martin come in and bust the whole thing up. He wants His people to return to Israel after 1,900 years of dispersion among the Gentile nations, so He calls in the Czars, Mussolini, and Adolf  to get them out of Russia and Europe. And when he's done with them, the Bolsheviks overthrow the Czars, and the Allies take back Europe from the Krauts.

Persecute those Jews? I'd just as soon crawl across broken glass on my hands and knees. But that doesn't change the fact that the greatest "anti-Semite" in that Book is [God Almighty ]{.class_3055}(see Ezek. 5:8--9).

["What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ?"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 27:22). "Kill Him! Crucify Him!"[ "Why, what evil hath he done?"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 27:23). Not one cotton-picking thing on the face of this earth! His reputation was that of One ["who went about doing GOOD, and healing all that were oppressed of the devil"]{.class_3054} (Acts 10:38). But by now that crowd has been whipped into such a frenzy by the chief priests that they don't even answer Pilate. It's: ["Let him be crucified"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 27:23).

So Pilate, knowing more about Deuteronomy 21:6--7 than the people he governed (which see), called for a basin of water and washed his hands of the whole mess (Matt. 27:24). Of course, Pilate could no more get rid of his sin of rejecting Jesus Christ with water than can a Catholic Priest or a Campbellite elder. "What can wash away my sin?" Well, it sure ain't the city water system. (I suppose if the water is fluoridated, one would be a "hard-shell" Baptist.)

And once more the Jews put themselves under a curse: ["His blood be on us and on our children"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 27:25). And once more the prophetic expositors try to worm their way around the obvious: that God has been judging that nation for 2,000 years for rejecting their Messiah. "Well, that just applies to the generation immediately following; it was over in A.D. 70." Better think again. God's wrath has ["come upon them to the uttermost"]{.class_3054} (1 Thess. 2:16) ["until the fulness of the Gentiles be come in"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_430} (Rom. 11:25).[ That's]{.class_3055} when God sends a ["Deliverer"]{.class_3054} to ["turn away ungodliness from Jacob"]{.class_3054} (Rom. 11:26), and not until. As usual, Christian scholarship has totally confounded the salvation of an [individual Jew in this age]{.class_3055} with the salvation of [the nation of Israel at the Second Advent.]{.class_3055}

["And led him out to crucify him."]{.class_3054} Compromise never works. It didn't work for Pilate, and it won't work for you either, sinner friend. Halfway to the goal doesn't make it; a miss is as good as a mile. Pilate has Christ beaten to within an inch of His life in hopes that His mangled flesh will satisfy the bloodlust of the crowd. It misfires, of course. There is no way for you or anyone else to avoid the decision Pilate had to make.

If only Pilate could have risen to the occasion, he could have gone down in history as one of the greatest men in history outside of Jesus Christ Himself. If Pilate had been able to take off the robe of his authority as Governor and place it on the shoulders of Jesus Christ and say, "There's your King; crucify me!", he would have had more books written about him than Napoleon or Abraham Lincoln. But Pilate defiled his conscience, prostituted his office, and for the sake of political expediency, ended up [damning his soul.]{.class_3055} Poor, plastic, pliable, pitiful Pontius Pilate. Like 90% of American males (or European, Asian, African, or Latino men, for that matter), he was a [moral coward.]{.class_3055}

21 And they compel one Simon a Cyrenian, who passed by, coming out of the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to bear his cross.

22 And they bring him unto the place Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, The place of a skull.

Verse 20 begins what is known in Roman Catholicism as "The Stations of the Cross." There are twelve from Pilate condemning Jesus to death, to Jesus dying on the cross. As usual, though, the Popes mix a little truth with a dump-truck load of BALONEY (see our comments on 7:6--13).

The papists have Simon of Cyrene helping Jesus carry the cross after Christ [falls]{.class_3055}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_431}. You will search the Scriptures in vain to find Jesus falling at all on the so-called "Via Dolorosa," much less three times. Things like Jesus meeting His mother on the way, Veronica and her hankey, or praying through the "stations" is Daffy Duck on crack cocaine. Ditto "Good Friday," Calvary being inside Jerusalem, Jesus Christ as a Irish "Mick" with red-brown hair, or the cross being a dogwood tree. All of that is absolute nonsense made up out of floss candy. You'd be just as "Scriptural" believing in Rumpelstiltskin, the three little pigs, and the billy goats gruff.

For reasons not recorded in the Scriptures, the Romans grab a North African from around Libya (Acts 2:10) and make him carry Christ's cross the fifteen-minute trek up to Calvary. (He either carried it by himself or in conjunction with Jesus.) We may suppose that Simon the Cyrenian, like the Ethiopian eunuch of Acts 8, was an African proselyte to Judaism, and as such, he stands as a prefigure of the Holy Spirit saving a member of a race cursed by God (Gen. 9:25; Zech. 14:21).

["For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall ALL be made alive"]{.class_3054} (1 Cor. 15:22).

["And by him]{.class_3054} \[Jesus Christ\][ ALL that believe are justified from all things, from which ye could not be justified by the law of Moses"]{.class_3054} (Acts 13:39).

Do you want to qualify for Galatians 3:28, like all the Liberals and integrationists and women's libbers preach? Then get ["in Christ Jesus" "by faith"]{.class_3054} (Gal. 3:26). Otherwise go on to Hell as an "oppressed minority."

["The father of Alexander and Rufus"]{.class_3054} (vs. 21). The name [Rufus]{.class_3055} shows up again in Romans 16:13, and because of this, Scofield and others entertain the possibility that the two are one and the same. If that be so (and there's no way of knowing for sure), then there is a possibility that Simon came out of this experience a saved man. It is hard to believe that men like Barabbas and Simon could have such an encounter with the Saviour and walk away from it unchanged spiritually, but it is not without precedent (see our comments on 10:17--23).

The location of the Crucifixion was ["Golgotha"]{.class_3054} (vs. 22); that's a Hebrew word meaning ["the place of a skull"]{.class_3054} (vs. 22; Matt. 27:33; John 19:17). The [Latin]{.class_3055}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_432} term for skull (or cranium) is used in Luke 23:33---["Calvary"]{.class_3054} (see our comments on John 19:17 in that Commentary). For every time you find the term [Golgotha]{.class_3055} in a hymn, you'll find a hundred more that refer to [Calvary.]{.class_3055}

The imagery is interesting here. What you have is a man who referred to Himself as ["the light of the world"]{.class_3054} dying on a hill shaped like a skull. So do you know what the satanists, witches, and voodoo practitioners have? A skull with a candle on top. They do it without even realizing [why]{.class_3055} they do it. It has nothing to do with "life burning away until it is snuffed out in death." That [King James]{.class_3055} Bible will run you whether you like it or not, or believe it or not.

23 And they gave him to drink wine mingled with myrrh: but he received it not.

Here we run into a perfect "test case" to see if a teacher is a Bible believer or a Roman Catholic by any other name. You see, there are two different offerings of "drink" to Christ on the cross. One is ["wine"]{.class_3054} (Gk. [oinos]{.class_3055}) here in our text, and the other is ["vinegar"]{.class_3054} (Gk. [oxos]{.class_3055}), as in Matthew 27:34; Luke 23:36; John 19:29--30. He does not receive the wine of Mark 15:23 at all. He tastes the vinegar of Matthew 27:34 but does not drink of it then because it is to deaden the pain, and for our sakes, He intends to experience everything the Crucifixion has to offer. It is not until the end, just before He dies, that He receives the vinegar without the gall of Matthew 27:34.

That is, unless you are a Roman Catholic Priest hawking his shinny for the Mass, or a teacher in a Fundamental Bible College or University pushing a corrupt "Bible" version that supports the readings of the Vatican text. You see, corrupt, apostate translations like the [ESV]{.class_3055} (the "Conservative" edition of the Liberal [RSV]{.class_3055}) translate [oxos]{.class_3055} in Luke 23:36 and John 19:30 as ["sour wine,"]{.class_3055} which is [one]{.class_3055} of the definitions assigned to the word by lexicographers like Thayer. Now, having a choice between "sour wine" and "vinegar" (which is the other definition for [oxos]{.class_3055}), why would those "godly Conservatives" on the [ESV]{.class_3055} committee choose the translation that would make Jesus Christ a wino? It is not merely a matter of "preference" on how to translate [oxos;]{.class_3055} it is a matter of trying to []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_433}conform their translation to a corrupt Roman Catholic text.

You see, based mainly on the authority of [Aleph]{.class_3055} and B ([Sinaiticus]{.class_3055} and [Vaticanus]{.class_3055}), as well as five other uncials and a few other cursive manuscripts, Westcott and Hort (and Nestle, Aland, and Metzger in turn) went against [eighteen]{.class_3055} uncial manuscripts, including A---[Alexandrinus]{.class_3055}---and the [majority]{.class_3055} of cursive manuscripts to change the [oxos]{.class_3055} of Matthew 27:34 to the [oinos]{.class_3055} of Mark 15:23 so the Lord [would ]{.class_3055}end up at least tasting the wine. This was done to match the [Roman Catholic Latin Vulgate.]{.class_3055}

But we Bible believers never have a Greek or a Hebrew lexicon as our final authority. We have a much surer authority than that---we have the [Holy Scriptures]{.class_3055} (2 Pet. 1:19--20). The Author of the Scriptures, the Holy Spirit of God (2 Pet. 1:21), told us in Psalm 69:21, 1,000 years before Jesus hung on the cross, what He would drink; and it wasn't any kind of wine at all, sour or otherwise. It was ["vinegar"]{.class_3054} (Heb. [khomets]{.class_3055})---period. The only way the apostates on the [ESV]{.class_3055} committee could come up with ["sour wine"]{.class_3055} in Psalm 69:21 was to go to the [Septuagint]{.class_3055} and privately interpret[ oxos]{.class_3055} again. But such are the ways of death and Hell on the translation committees of "Conservatives." Not even a perversion of the Scriptures like the [NIV]{.class_3055} or [NASV]{.class_3055} went that far this time.

24 And when they had crucified him, they parted his garments, casting lots upon them, what every man should take.

The Holy Spirit simply and unceremoniously has Mark write, ["And when they had crucified him,"]{.class_3054} without ever telling you, in any Gospel, what all was involved in the process. If you want all the gory details, from a medical point of view, you can get a work entitled [The Crucifixion of Jesus, A Forensic Inquiry]{.class_3055} by a Dr. Frederick Zugibe.

What we want to note here are those nail prints of John 20:25 that were in Christ's ["hands"]{.class_3054} at His resurrection. Because John recorded the Lord as saying they were ["in his hands,"]{.class_3054} the Roman Catholic artists of the Dark Ages put them in the center of the hand, like the stigmata on the hands of Catholic mystics (e.g., Francis of []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_434}Assisi). Naturally the Catholics got it wrong AGAIN. (When have they ever been right more than a busted clock?)

The nails could not have been in the palms of Christ's hands because the weight of His body would have caused the nails to tear through the hands between the fingers. (To "fix" this problem---which they created---they had Christ's arms bound to the cross by ropes. Of course there is no evidence for this in or out of the Bible. Typically, the Romans either nailed a victim to his cross or bound him, but not both.) The nails were placed just above the wrist, in the space between the four wrist bones (the "carpal" bones). Again, the Scriptures themselves clear up any "contradictions" imagined by the scholars in this regard, for you are told in Genesis 24:22, 47 that [the wrist is part of the hand]{.class_3055} (Eliezer placed bracelets on Rebekah's ["hands"]{.class_3054}).

We have already commented on the difference between Christ's personal ["garments"]{.class_3054} and the robe the Roman soldiers put on Him to mock Him back up under our remarks under verse 17 (which see). Note that the soldiers are shooting "craps" for ["his garments"]{.class_3054} because they have stripped Christ [naked.]{.class_3055} Of course, artists (including me) put a loin cloth on Him for the sake of decency, but the fact of the matter was that Jesus hung naked on the cross. Crucifixion was not only a painful death, it was a shameful one as well.

This dice game for Christ's garments was prophesied a thousand years before it took place. Psalm 22:18, and the rest of the psalm, is so specific as to what would happen to Jesus on the cross, what He would go through, and what He would say, that the Rabbis in Israel today are forbidding their congregants from reading Psalm 22 (and Isaiah 53 as well). One missionary who was trying to import Hebrew Bibles (the Old Testament) into Israel was told they would only be allowed in the country on the provision that they excluded Psalm 22 and Isaiah 53. Why? Because it is obvious to anyone who is able to read sixth-grade English or Hebrew about whom the prophets are talking. No one else but Jesus Christ ever has (or ever will) fulfill those Scriptures.

Now you may not realize what is going on in verse 24, but someone is gambling for [the control of the universe.]{.class_3055} Do you know what God's ["garment"]{.class_3054} and ["vesture"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_435} (the same terms used of His Son's clothing while here on earth) is in Hebrews 1:10--12? It's ["the heavens"]{.class_3054} (Heb. 1:10). Without having a clue what they are doing, those Roman "dogfaces" are involved in Star Wars, Star Trek, "Battle for the Stars," Battlestar Galactica, NASA, Apollo, etc. This is man trying to take control of the constellations, galaxies, and star clusters from his Creator.

The day will come when God says, "All right, when I was down there on earth, you stripped me naked. You want to see me naked? Here goes!" And off comes the Milky Way, Andromeda, Alpha-Draconis, etc., and up the whole shebang goes in a mushroom cloud that would make Nagasaki and Hiroshima combined look like a "snap and pop" next to a MOAB bomb (2 Pet. 3:10--12). Then God will haul all the unsaved dead of all the ages before that Great White Throne, and they will see the One they ignored, cursed, blasphemed, rebelled against, and rejected in all His naked glory and wrath (Rev. 20:11--12).

You say, "What about you, Ruckman?" I'll be "fireproof," man. You see, I took the One in the garment. I wasn't interested in His clothes (the sun, the moon, Mars, Jupiter, Venus, Virgo, Leo, etc.); I was interested in [Him.]{.class_3055}

"No condemnation now I dread;

Jesus, and all in Him is mine!

Alive in Him, my living Head,

AND CLOTHED IN RIGHTEOUSNESS DIVINE."

You go ahead and build a colony on the moon and populate Mars. I'll take the righteousness of God in Jesus Christ, and in a few thousand years, we'll see who got the better deal.

25 And it was the third hour, and they crucified him.

26 And the superscription of his accusation was written over, THE KING OF THE JEWS.

["The third hour"]{.class_3054} of verse 25 would be Jewish time. That would be three hours from 6:00 a.m. to 9:00 in the morning. He hangs on the cross until ["the ninth hour"]{.class_3054}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_436}---3:00 in what we would call the "afternoon," but in the Jewish way of reckoning the day, the beginning of the evening. He is taken down, "embalmed" by Joseph and Nicodemus (John 19:38--39), and sealed in the tomb before 6:00 p.m., when the first night of His burial began (Matt. 12:40).

Verse 26 gives you ["the superscription"]{.class_3054} Pilate had hung over the head of Jesus on the cross (John 19:19). Each Gospel writer gives you [part]{.class_3055} of that inscription, so you have to take them all together supplementally to get the full writing: "THIS IS JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS" (Matt. 27:37; here; Luke 23:38; John 19:19).

Luke 23:38 and John 19:20 tell you it was recorded in three languages, which just happen to represent the three main races from Noah. (I know; I know: "There is only one race, the human race." That's the current mantra of the "neo-creationists." But that's because they are trying to be in good standing with the integrationists and race-mixers. It wouldn't be "politically correct" to go by a Bible and an [English dictionary. ]{.class_3055}So just make up your own "facts" and definitions to "get along." \[I believe that's how Rodney King put it after a black mob beat a white man nearly to death in revenge for some white policemen beating Rodney King for resisting arrest after driving a car as a deadly weapon at speeds over 100 m.p.h. because he was hyped up on drugs.\]) That would be Hebrew (Shem---Gen. 10:22--24), Latin (Ham---the ["lingua franca"]{.class_3055} of the Roman Empire in [North Africa]{.class_3055}), and Greek (Japheth---the common street language used throughout the [European]{.class_3055} part of the Roman Empire). Christ's death is for [all]{.class_3055} men---black, white, yellow, "chartreuse," or polka-dotted. So when the Gospel goes out in the book of Acts, it goes to an African (the Ethiopian eunuch---Acts 8), an Oriental (Paul, a Jew---Acts 9), and a white European (Cornelius---Acts 10)---all three "branches" of mankind. And when Jesus Christ returns to reign, He is ["King over all the earth"]{.class_3054} (Zech. 14:9), and He rules from a [Jewish]{.class_3055} city, Jerusalem (Zech. 14:16). So both Advents are in view in this superscription.

Now verse 26 and Matthew 27:37 call the inscription Christ's ["accusation."]{.class_3054} It was the common Roman practice to give the crime on a board over the head of the criminal being crucified. This is what the chief priests wanted: an [accusation.]{.class_3055}[]{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_437} They wanted the inscription to read ["HE SAID, I am King of the Jews"]{.class_3054} (John 19:21). In other words, it wasn't true except in Jesus' own twisted, blasphemous mind.

But you are not going to do an "end run" around God Almighty. That was His chosen King hanging there on that cross, and He was going to have the "last laugh" (Psa. 2:1--6). He used a cowardly, pliable, cynical politician---who was just as lost as a golf ball in high weeds---to write His words, just like He used a lost "Fundamentalist" in Numbers 23--24 and a lost priest in John 11:49--51. Pilate told those Jewish priests, ["What I have written I have written"]{.class_3054} (John 19:22): he wasn't going to change one word of it to pacify them anymore.

What had Pilate written? It wasn't just an ["accusation"]{.class_3054}; it was ["a title"]{.class_3054} (John 19:19). Pilate may have damned his soul by giving in to a bunch of jealous murderers just to keep his job, but he knew exactly who Jesus was after John 18:36--37 and John 19:7--14. This was Pilate's way of getting back at those religious leaders for forcing him to have Jesus executed after declaring Him innocent three times. This superscription and its emphatic declaration is imperative in understanding what follows in regards to the two thieves crucified with Christ.

27 And with him they crucify two thieves; the one on his right hand, and the other on his left.

28 And the scripture was fulfilled, which saith, And he was numbered with the transgressors.

29 And they that passed by railed on him, wagging their heads, and saying, Ah, thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days,

30 Save thyself, and come down from the cross.

31 Likewise also the chief priests mocking said among themselves with the scribes, He saved others; himself he cannot save.

32 Let Christ the King of Israel descend now from the cross, that we may see and believe. And they that were crucified with him reviled him.

Verse 27 summarizes humanity in a nutshell. On those three crosses (Ethelbert Bullinger has five---two thieves, two ["malefactors"]{.class_3054} \[Luke 23:32\], and Christ; the old "hyper" couldn't resist dividing the Bread of Life to the point of being crumbs) is found every man and woman who has ever lived or will live.

On the middle cross, you have a sinless Man. He has [no]{.class_3055} sin [in]{.class_3055} Him (2 Cor. 5:21), but He has sin [on]{.class_3055} Him as our Sin-bearer (Heb. 9:28).[ "He was numbered with the transgressors"]{.class_3054} (vs. 28---a fulfillment of Isa. 53:12) both [physically]{.class_3055} and [spiritually.]{.class_3055}

On one side of Christ hangs a sinner who mocks the Lord the same as the passers-by and the chief priests (vss. 29--31 cf. Luke 23:39). He has sin [on him and in him.]{.class_3055} He remains that way until he dies and wakes up in Hell.

Then on the other side of Christ hangs a sinner. There is no doubt about that. He admitted he was getting just what he had coming to him for his crimes (Luke 23:41). He starts out reviling the Lord just as his buddy, but he doesn't stay that way. He ends up believing on Christ and going home to Paradise with Him. He is no longer just a sinner; he is a [saved]{.class_3055} sinner. His sins may be [in him,]{.class_3055} but they are no longer [on him.]{.class_3055} They have been imputed to that "Man in the middle" (see Rom. 4:8).

Now what changed for that penitent thief? Well, it sure wasn't anything he could [do.]{.class_3055} He couldn't get [baptized.]{.class_3055} He never "spoke in tongues, the initial evidence of the baptism of the Holyghost." He didn't keep the Ten Commandments or the Golden Rule, and there was no time left to make sure his good works outweighed his bad. He was in a position where there was [absolutely nothing he could do to save himself.]{.class_3055}

Here is a sinner who is saved by grace through faith without works. Of course, it is not faith in the same thing you believe to save you. If you are saved, you got saved by believing Christ died for your sins and rose again for your justification ["according to the scriptures"]{.class_3054} (1 Cor. 15:1--4; Rom. 4:24--25). If you trusted anything else besides that to save you, you are as lost as a goose in a horse race (Gal. 1:8--9).

[That's not what this saved thief believed to get saved.]{.class_3055} You say, "Why do you say that, Ruckman?" [Because Jesus Christ hadn't died yet,]{.class_3055} and []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2670.xhtml_page_439}[He hadn't risen from the dead yet.]{.class_3055}

That Bible says, ["So then faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God" ]{.class_3054}(Rom. 10:17). That being the case, what part of ["the word of God"]{.class_3054} did the penitent thief "hear" that produced saving faith in him? Well, it certainly wasn't 1 Corinthians 15:3--4; you won't find anything like that said to the penitent thief at all.

But there was something that thief read and to which he responded. He said to Jesus, ["Lord, remember me when thou comest into thy KINGDOM"]{.class_3054} (Luke 23:42). ["Kingdom"]{.class_3054}? What ["kingdom"]{.class_3054}? Where did he get the idea that Jesus was going to be a King over a ["kingdom"]{.class_3054}? We can speculate what that thief heard about and from Jesus before he got caught and thrown in the "pokie," but there is one thing we know for sure from the [Scriptures,]{.class_3055} not speculation. That thief could read a superscription over the Lord's head which read: "THIS IS JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS."

That thief read the same superscription his buddy did, but the difference between the two was that the penitent thief believed what he read was ["the word of God"]{.class_3054} instead of ["the word of men"]{.class_3054} (1 Thess. 2:13). Now you understand why I said what I said about an unsaved Roman Governor writing the words of God. Pilate may have been a lost pagan polytheist, but when he put that title over Christ's head, he was writing the [truth,]{.class_3055} even though he wouldn't acknowledge it when he stood face to face with it (see our comments on John 18:38 in that Commentary). If Pilate had given in to those Jewish priests and written merely an accusation rather than Christ's legitimate title, it would have been a [half-truth,]{.class_3055} and as anyone knows, a half-truth is a [whole lie.]{.class_3055}

That's why it is not enough merely to profess the [King James]{.class_3055} Bible is "the preserved Word of God." That may be true, but it is not the [whole truth]{.class_3055} and [nothing but the truth.]{.class_3055} The [Authorized Version]{.class_3055} of the Holy Bible is not merely some "message" preserved by God for us, like the Neo-orthodox teach; it is the very words of God He intended us to have in our language, without proven error. It is the Holy Scriptures in English, and ["ALL SCRIPTURE is given by inspiration of God"]{.class_3054} (2 Tim. 3:16).

That penitent thief read the truth of God on that superscription, and he believed what he read. Having read the word of God, he believed it, and because he believed it, he called on the name of the Lord (see Rom. 10:14): ["Lord . . . ,"]{.class_3054} not just ["Jesus"]{.class_3055} as you read in the new versions. That's an ancient heresy called "Monarchianism" that teaches "the Christ" came on the man Jesus at His baptism, making Him God, and left Him on the cross, where He died as a mere man. Jesus was God before He ever was born "Jesus" (see Mic. 5:2). He never ceased to be God, not even when He died as a man. He was just as much ["Lord"]{.class_3054} hanging there on the cross as He was in His ministry. The modern "Bibles" will get rid of the Deity of Christ just as a sinner is believing on Him for salvation (see our comments on Acts 8:37 and Acts 9:6 in that Commentary).

Back to Mark 15, verse 29: ["Ah, thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days."]{.class_3054} That was the false accusation brought against Him at His illegal trial before Caiaphas, and you can see our comments on it under 14:58. They lied about what He said then, and they lie about it now. He never said He would destroy the Temple, and He never said He would rebuild Herod's Temple within three days. Go back to the "original" and READ John 2:19--21. [They]{.class_3055}, not He, would destroy the temple; and it would be ["the temple OF HIS BODY"]{.class_3054} (John 2:21), not Herod's Temple. They are fulfilling their part of what He actually said, without intending to, even as they lie about what He said.

Verse 31: ["He saved others; himself he cannot save."]{.class_3054} Isn't the Devil subtle? He just had his children (John 8:44) lie in verse 29, but "you can't sell a bushel of bad apples unless you have some good ones on top." Immediately following the lie is a true statement, but not for the reason they thought.
