This proves that the atonement for our sins was [complete]{.class_3055} (Heb. 1:3, 8:1, 10:12, 12:2). From there, He sent His Spirit to indwell every believer (Acts 2:33). It is a "foretaste of glory divine" in that all spiritual powers have been subjected to His authority; from there, he sits as[ "the head"]{.class_3054} of His spiritual Body (Eph. 1:20--23; 1 Pet. 3:22). From the right hand of His Father He makes intercession for those who have trusted Him as Saviour (Rom. 8:34). We should set our affections (what we [love;]{.class_3055} not just our [minds]{.class_3055} as in the corrupt, apostate translations) on heavenly things because we are seated spiritually with our Saviour at God's right hand (Col. 3:1--2 cf. Eph. 2:6). And it is where Jesus Christ awaits His coming visible, physical, literal kingdom over this earth (Psa. 110:1).

We have already discussed the beginning and the continuing of the Great Commission back under verse 15. The ["every where"]{.class_3054} of verse 20 is defined in Acts 1:8. The apostles started out in Jerusalem, and they expanded into Judea (the region where Jerusalem was located).

But notice that with Samaria, the preaching goes beyond the ministry of the apostles. It wasn't the twelve apostles who originally ["went every where preaching the word"]{.class_3054} (Acts 8:4). It was the Jewish believers who ["were all scattered abroad"]{.class_3054} over the persecution by Saul of Tarsus (Acts 8:1--4). In fact, it was a [deacon]{.class_3055} (Philip---Acts 6:5) who first preached Christ to the Samaritans and exercised the apostolic signs (Acts 8:5--7). So the Great Commission is incumbent on [every Christian,]{.class_3055} not just the apostles.

["The uttermost part of the earth"]{.class_3054} is given in such places as Romans 15:19, 28; 2 Thessalonians 4:10; and 1 Peter 1:1. After the New Testament, the Gospel goes to Ireland through St. Patrick, to Scotland through Columba, to Germany through Ulfilas, to India through Thomas, and all the way to China through the Nestorians. After the Reformation, the Gospel went to Greenland and North []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2892.xhtml_page_475}America and Jamaica through the Moravians, to India through William Carey, to Burma through Adoniram Judson, and on and on.

Be like those apostles: [preach everywhere.]{.class_3055} Don't relegate your witness to a church building. Get out on the street corners and yell, "Rome enslaves; Jesus saves! Turn or burn! Repent or perish!" Go out on door-to-door visitation. Pass out tracts wherever you are allowed. Witness when and where you can. Be "soul conscious."

Back to Mark 16:20---["the Lord working with them."]{.class_3054} Remember you are not alone when you witness. Jesus Christ is right there with you (Matt. 28:20), in the person of the Holy Spirit, working with you. You are a laborer ["together with God"]{.class_3054} (1 Cor. 3:6--9), a worker ["together with him"]{.class_3054} (2 Cor. 6:1). If you were in business for yourself, how would you like to have a partner like that? Well, you do [if]{.class_3055} you are laboring for eternity.

Folks, there are only two eternal things on this earth. One is that Book; it will be around when the heavens and earth are a cinder (Matt. 24:35; 1 Pet. 1:23 cf. 2 Pet. 3:10--12). The other is the souls of anyone you lead to Christ. There are things you will have to do in this world to keep a roof over your head, to keep body and soul together, and to maintain a ["peaceable life in all godliness and honesty"]{.class_3054} (1 Tim. 2:2); but make your life's vocation to reach lost sinners for Jesus Christ. It is the greatest thing you can do.

We have already discussed the ["signs"]{.class_3054} of verse 20 under our comments on verses 17--18 (which see).

Mark ends his Gospel as all the "evangelists": ["Amen"]{.class_3054} (Matt. 28:20; Luke 24:53; John 21:25). The word means "so let it be" or "truly." The old black preachers would say, "Dat's troofin' it."

In other words, the Gospel is being preached everywhere, the Lord is working with His witnesses, the Holy Spirit is confirming everything the apostles and their converts are saying with signs and wonders, so Aaaamen! Keep going, brother.

The old Negro spiritual says, "Let de Church roll on!" Don't let any ornery church member, any greedy "televangelist," any professor in a Christian College or University puffed up with his own self-importance, any "wolf" trying to destroy the "sheep" with false doctrine, or any self-serving person consumed with his or her own ambitions stop the Church from doing what it was called to do by its []{#11hUKl1KToiErvrMCxN7Aw2892.xhtml_page_476}Saviour. Forward, brother, forward. Advance; no retreat. Like the old song says, "No turning back, no turning back." Let the Church roll on! Amen, amen, and AMEN!
